050608.phonetic: S-di-wi-no u-ka-li-tsv-hi u-wo-hi-yu-sv a-le u-li-ni-gv-gv  u-tsa-te u-s-qua-ni-go-di a-le u-ye-lv-dv du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-he yv-wi a-ne-hv-i.

050609.english: Then there arose certain of the synagogue, which is called the synagogue of the Libertines, and Cyrenians, and Alexandrians, and of them of Cilicia and of Asia, disputing with Stephen.
050609.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏚᎾᎴᏁ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᏁᎳ ᎠᏂᎵᏆᏗᏂ ᏧᏂᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏥᏚᏙᎥ, ᎠᎴ ᏌᎵᏂ ᎠᏂᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎡᎵᏱᎩ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏏᎵᏏᏱ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎡᏗᏱ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎤᎾᎵᏃᎮᏔᏁ ᏍᏗᏫ.
050609.phonetic: Hna-quo-no du-na-le-ne gi-lo i-yu-na-s-di di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi a-ne-la A-ni-li-qua-di-ni tsu-ni-la-wi-i-s-di-yi tsi-du-do-v, a-le Sa-li-ni a-ni-hi, a-le E-li-yi-gi a-ne-hi, a-le Si-li-si-yi a-ne-hi, a-le E-di-yi a-ne-hi, u-na-li-no-he-ta-ne S-di-wi.

050610.english: And they were not able to resist the wisdom and the spirit by which he spake.
050610.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏃ ᏰᎵ ᎬᏩᏂᎭᎷᎩᏍᏗ ᏱᎨᏎ ᎠᏏᎾᏌᏅ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᏕᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎬᎢ.
050610.phonetic: V-tla-no ye-li gv-wa-ni-ha-lu-gi-s-di yi-ge-se a-si-na-sa-nv a-le a-da-nv-do de-gv-di-s-gv ga-wo-ni-s-gv-i.

050611.english: Then they suborned men, which said, We have heard him speak blasphemous words against Moses, and against God.
050611.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏚᏂᏅᏎ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎢᏯᏂᏪᏍᎩ; ᎣᏣᏛᎦᏁᎸ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎧᏁᎬᎢ ᎠᏐᏢᎢᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᎼᏏ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ.
050611.phonetic: Hna-quo-no du-ni-nv-se a-ni-s-ga-ya na-s-gi hi-a i-ya-ni-we-s-gi; o-tsa-dv-ga-ne-lv na-s-gi ka-ne-gv-i a-so-tlv-i-s-di-s-gv Mo-si a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi.

050612.english: And they stirred up the people, and the elders, and the scribes, and came upon him, and caught him, and brought him to the council,
050612.cherokee: ᏚᏂᏖᎸᏁᏃ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏴᏫ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᎳᏫᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩ, ᎤᏂᏃᎴᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏂᏴᎮ ᎠᎴ ᏕᎦᎳᏫᎥ ᎤᎾᏘᏃᎴᎢ.
050612.phonetic: Du-ni-te-lv-ne-no u-ni-tsa-ti yv-wi a-le di-ni-la-wi-gi a-le di-no-we-li-s-gi, u-ni-no-le-no a-le u-ni-ni-yv-he a-le de-ga-la-wi-v u-na-ti-no-le-i.

050613.english: And set up false witnesses, which said, This man ceaseth not to speak blasphemous words against this holy place, and the law:
050613.cherokee: ᎦᏰᎪᎩᏃ ᎠᏂᏃᎮᏍᎩ ᏚᏂᎧᏁᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎢᏯᏂᏪᏍᎩ; ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ Ꮭ ᏯᏑᎵᎪᎦ ᎧᏁᎬ ᎠᏐᏢᎢᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ.
050613.phonetic: Ga-ye-go-gi-no a-ni-no-he-s-gi du-ni-ka-ne-i, na-s-gi hi-a i-ya-ni-we-s-gi; Hi-a a-s-ga-ya tla ya-su-li-go-ga ka-ne-gv a-so-tlv-i-s-di-s-gv hi-a na-s-gi ga-lv-quo-di-yu ge-sv a-le di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di.

050614.english: For we have heard him say, that this Jesus of Nazareth shall destroy this place, and shall change the customs which Moses delivered us.
050614.cherokee: ᎣᎦᏛᎦᏅᏰᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎧᏪᏍᎬᎩ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏌ ᎾᏎᎵᏗ ᏤᎲᎩ ᏛᏲᏍᏔᏂ ᎠᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᎦᏁᏟᏴᏏ ᎢᏯᏛᏁᎵᏓᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎼᏏ ᎢᎩᏁᎸᎯ.
050614.phonetic: O-ga-dv-ga-nv-ye-no hi-a ni-ka-we-s-gv-gi; Na-s-gi hi-a Tsi-sa Na-se-li-di tse-hv-gi dv-yo-s-ta-ni a-ni, a-le da-ga-ne-tli-yv-si i-ya-dv-ne-li-da-s-di na-s-gi Mo-si i-gi-ne-lv-hi.

050615.english: And all that sat in the council, looking stedfastly on him, saw his face as it had been the face of an angel.
050615.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᏕᎦᎳᏫᎥ ᎠᏂᏂ ᎤᏯᏅᏒᎯ ᏓᏂᎧᏅ ᎤᏂᎪᎮ ᎤᎧᏛ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎤᎧᏛ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎨᏎᎢ.
050615.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv-no de-ga-la-wi-v a-ni-ni u-ya-nv-sv-hi da-ni-ka-nv u-ni-go-he u-ka-dv di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi u-ka-dv na-s-gi-ya ge-se-i.

050701.english: Then said the high priest, Are these things so?
050701.cherokee: ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒᏃ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎶᎯ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎪ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᎯ ᏄᏍᏗ?
050701.phonetic: Nu-gv-wi-yu-sv-no a-tsi-lv--ge-lo-hi hi-a nu-we-se-i; Na-s-gi-s-go u-no-hi-yu-hi nu-s-di?

050702.english: And he said, Men, brethren, and fathers, hearken; The God of glory appeared unto our father Abraham, when he was in Mesopotamia, before he dwelt in Charran,
050702.cherokee: [ᏍᏗᏫᏃ] ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ ᎢᏓᏓᏅᏟ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎩᏙᏓ, ᎢᏣᏛᏓᏍᏓ.  ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏄᏛᏁᎴ ᎢᎩᏙᏓ ᎡᏆᎭᎻ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎺᏌᏆᏕᎻ ᏤᎮᎢ, ᎠᏏᏉ ᎨᎳᏂ ᎾᏁᎳᏗᏍᎬᎾ ᏥᎨᏎᎢ;
050702.phonetic: [S-di-wi-no] hi-a nu-we-se-i; I-tsi-s-ga-ya i-da-da-nv-tli a-le di-gi-do-da, i-tsa-dv-da-s-da.  U-ne-la-nv-hi a-tsi-lv-quo-di gv-ni-ge-sv nu-dv-ne-le i-gi-do-da E-qua-ha-mi na-s-gi na-hi-yu Me-sa-qua-de-mi tse-he-i, a-si-quo Ge-la-ni na-ne-la-di-s-gv-na tsi-ge-se-i;

050703.english: And said unto him, Get thee out of thy country, and from thy kindred, and come into the land which I shall shew thee.
050703.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏣᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᎯᏄᎪᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏗᏨᎿᎭ ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᎦᏙᎯ ᎬᏯᏎᎮᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏫᎷᎩ.
050703.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-we-se-le-i; Tsa-tse-li-go-hi hi-nu-go-i a-le go-hu-s-di di-tsv-hna, a-le na-hna ga-do-hi gv-ya-se-he-di ge-sv wi-lu-gi.

050704.english: Then came he out of the land of the Chaldaeans, and dwelt in Charran: and from thence, when his father was dead, he removed him into this land, wherein ye now dwell.
050704.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᏓᏅᏎ ᎠᏂᎦᎵᏗ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎪᎯ, ᎨᎳᏂᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏕᎢ, ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᏗᎤᏓᏅᏒ, ᎤᏙᏓ ᎤᏲᎱᏒ, ᎡᏍᎦᏂ ᎤᎷᏤᎴᎢ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏂ ᎪᎯ ᏥᏤᎭ.
050704.phonetic: Hna-quo-no u-da-nv-se A-ni-ga-li-di u-na-tse-li-go-hi, Ge-la-ni-no u-ne-la-de-i, na-hna-no di-u-da-nv-sv, u-do-da u-yo-hu-sv, e-s-ga-ni u-lu-tse-le-i na-s-gi a-ni go-hi tsi-tse-ha.

050705.english: And he gave him none inheritance in it, no, not so much as to set his foot on: yet he promised that he would give it to him for a possession, and to his seed after him, when as yet he had no child.
050705.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᎦᏓ ᎠᏂ ᏳᏁᎴᎢ ᏂᎤᏟᏍᏕ ᏏᎳᏏᏅᎯᏉ ᎢᎩᏛ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏚᎢᏍᏓᏁᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎢᏳᏩᏁᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᏢᏔᏅᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏏᏉ ᎤᏪᏥ ᏁᎲᎾ ᎨᏎᎢ.
050705.phonetic: A-le v-tla ga-da a-ni yu-ne-le-i ni-u-tli-s-de si-la-si-nv-hi-quo i-gi-dv, a-se-no u-du-i-s-da-ne-le na-s-gi u-tse-li i-yu-wa-ne-di-yi a-le na-s-gi u-ne-tlv-ta-nv-dv ge-sv a-si-quo u-we-tsi ne-hv-na ge-se-i.

050706.english: And God spake on this wise, That his seed should sojourn in a strange land; and that they should bring them into bondage, and entreat them evil four hundred years.
050706.cherokee: ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; "ᏣᏁᏢᏔᏅᏛ ᎠᏁᏙᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᎾᏤ-ᎵᎪᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏁᎯ ᏙᏓᎬᏩᏂᎾᏢᏂ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏲ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏅᏁᎵ ᏅᎩᏧᏈ ᏧᏕᏘᏴᏛ.
050706.phonetic: U-ne-la-nv-hi-no hi-a nu-we-se-i; "Tsa-ne-tlv-ta-nv-dv a-ne-do-hi ge-se-s-di na-hna u-na-tse--li-go-hi ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i, a-le na-hna a-ne-hi do-da-gv-wa-ni-na-tlv-ni a-le u-yo nv-da-gv-wa-nv-ne-li nv-gi-tsu-qui tsu-de-ti-yv-dv.

050707.english: And the nation to whom they shall be in bondage will I judge, said God: and after that shall they come forth, and serve me in this place.
050707.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏑᎾᏓᎴᎩ ᏴᏫ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᎾᏢᏅᎯ ᏙᏓᎦᏥᏳᎪᏓᏁᎵ, ᎣᏂᏃ ᎤᏂᎾᏄᎪᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎬᏆᏛᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ," ᎤᏛᏁ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ.
050707.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no su-na-da-le-gi yv-wi di-gv-wa-ni-na-tlv-nv-hi do-da-ga-tsi-yu-go-da-ne-li, o-ni-no u-ni-na-nu-go-i-s-di ge-se-s-di a-le a-ni go-hu-s-di gv-qua-dv-ne-di ge-se-s-di," u-dv-ne U-ne-la-nv-hi.

050708.english: And he gave him the covenant of circumcision: and so Abraham begat Isaac, and circumcised him the eighth day; and Isaac begat Jacob; and Jacob begat the twelve patriarchs.
050708.cherokee: ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗᏃ ᎤᏁᎴ ᎠᎱᏍᏕᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ; ᎡᏆᎭᎻᏃ ᎤᏕᏁᎴ ᎡᏏᎩ, ᎤᎱᏍᏕᏎᎴᏃ ᏧᏁᎵᏁ ᎢᎦ, ᎡᏏᎩᏃ ᏤᎦᏈ, ᏤᎦᏈᏃ ᏔᎳᏚ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ.
050708.phonetic: Di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di-no u-ne-le a-hu-s-de-s-di ge-sv u-gv-wa-li; E-qua-ha-mi-no u-de-ne-le E-si-gi, u-hu-s-de-se-le-no tsu-ne-li-ne i-ga, E-si-gi-no Tse-ga-qui, Tse-ga-qui-no ta-la-du i-ya-ni-dv di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge-i.

050709.english: And the patriarchs, moved with envy, sold Joseph into Egypt: but God was with him,
050709.cherokee: ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨᏃ ᎠᏅᏳᏤᎲ ᏅᏗᎤᎵᏍᏙᏔᏁ ᎤᏂᎾᏗᏅᏎ ᏦᏩ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ ᏩᎦᏘᏅᏍᏙᏗᏱ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏚᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎮᎢ,
050709.phonetic: Di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge-no a-nv-yu-tse-hv nv-di-u-li-s-do-ta-ne u-ni-na-di-nv-se Tso-wa I-tsi-qui-yi wa-ga-ti-nv-s-do-di-yi; a-se-no U-ne-la-nv-hi du-ka-hna-wa-di-do-he-i,

050710.english: And delivered him out of all his afflictions, and gave him favour and wisdom in the sight of Pharaoh king of Egypt; and he made him governor over Egypt and all his house.
050710.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏭᏓᎴᏍᎨ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᏲ ᏄᎵᏍᏓᏁᎵᏕᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎴ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎠᎦᏓᏅᏖᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎦᏔᎿᎭᎢᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎦᏔᎲ ᏇᎵᏲ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᏄᏩᏁᎴ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎦᏁᎸᎢ.
050710.phonetic: A-le u-wu-da-le-s-ge ni-ga-dv u-yo nu-li-s-da-ne-li-de-gv-i, a-le u-ne-le o-si-yu a-ga-da-nv-te-di-yi a-le a-ga-ta-hna-i-yu ge-sv a-ga-ta-hv Que-li-yo u-gv-wi-yu-hi I-tsi-qui-yi, a-le nu-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi nu-wa-ne-le I-tsi-qui-yi, a-le ni-ga-dv ga-ne-lv-i.

050711.english: Now there came a dearth over all the land of Egypt and Chanaan, and great affliction: and our fathers found no sustenance.
050711.cherokee: ᏂᎬᎾᏛᏃ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎨᎾᏂ ᏚᎪᏄᎶᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎠᎩᎵᏯ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨᏃ ᎤᎾᏠᎨ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ.
050711.phonetic: Ni-gv-na-dv-no I-tsi-qui-yi a-le Ge-na-ni du-go-nu-lo-se-i, a-le u-tsa-ti a-gi-li-ya nu-li-s-ta-ne-i, di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge-no u-na-tlo-ge u-na-li-s-da-yv-di.

050712.english: But when Jacob heard that there was corn in Egypt, he sent out our fathers first.
050712.cherokee: ᏤᎦᏈᏃ ᎤᏛᎦᏅ ᎤᏣᎴᏍᏗ ᎡᎲ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ, ᏕᎤᏅᏎ ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᎩᎳ ᎾᎨᏎᎢ.
050712.phonetic: Tse-ga-qui-no u-dv-ga-nv u-tsa-le-s-di e-hv I-tsi-qui-yi, de-u-nv-se di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge gi-la na-ge-se-i.

050713.english: And at the second time Joseph was made known to his brethren; and Joseph's kindred was made known unto Pharaoh.
050713.cherokee: ᏔᎵᏁᏃ ᏦᏩ ᎠᎾᎵᏅᏟ ᎬᏬᎵᏤᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏇᎵᏲ ᏕᎤᎦᏙᎥᏎ ᏦᏩ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏧᏩᎿᎭ
050713.phonetic: Ta-li-ne-no Tso-wa a-na-li-nv-tli gv-wo-li-tse-i, a-le Que-li-yo de-u-ga-do-v-se Tso-wa go-hu-s-di tsu-wa-hna.

050714.english: Then sent Joseph, and called his father Jacob to him, and all his kindred, threescore and fifteen souls.
050714.cherokee: ᏦᏩᏃ ᎤᏓᏅᏎ ᏭᏯᏅᎮ ᎤᏙᏓ ᏤᎦᏈ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏧᏩᎿᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎵᏆᏍᎪᎯ ᎯᏍᎩᎦᎵ ᎢᏯᏂᎢ.
050714.phonetic: Tso-wa-no u-da-nv-se wu-ya-nv-he u-do-da Tse-ga-qui a-le ni-ga-dv u-wa-sv go-hu-s-di tsu-wa-hna, na-s-gi ga-li-qua-s-go-hi hi-s-gi-ga-li i-ya-ni-i.

050715.english: So Jacob went down into Egypt, and died, he, and our fathers,
050715.cherokee: ᏤᎦᏈᏃ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ ᏭᎶᏎᎢ, ᎤᏲᎱᏎᏃ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ.
050715.phonetic: Tse-ga-qui-no I-tsi-qui-yi wu-lo-se-i, u-yo-hu-se-no, a-le na-s-quo di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge-i.

050716.english: And were carried over into Sychem, and laid in the sepulchre that Abraham bought for a sum of money of the sons of Emmor the father of Sychem.
050716.cherokee: ᏌᎩᎻᏃ ᏫᏗᎨᏥᏅᏍᏔᏁᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏕᎨᏥᏂᏌᏁ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᏆᎭᎻ ᎠᏕᎸ ᏧᎫᏴᏔᏁ ᏥᏚᏩᏎᎴ ᎡᎹ ᏧᏪᏥ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏌᎩᎻ ᎤᏙᏓ.
050716.phonetic: Sa-gi-mi-no wi-di-ge-tsi-nv-s-ta-ne-i a-le de-ge-tsi-ni-sa-ne a-tse-li-s-dv na-s-gi E-qua-ha-mi a-de-lv tsu-gu-yv-ta-ne tsi-du-wa-se-le E-ma tsu-we-tsi, na-s-gi Sa-gi-mi u-do-da.

050717.english: But when the time of the promise drew nigh, which God had sworn to Abraham, the people grew and multiplied in Egypt,
050717.cherokee: ᎠᏚᎢᏍᏛᏃ ᎤᏍᏆᎸᎯᏗᏎᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏎᎵᏔᏅᎯ ᏧᏁᏤᎴ ᎡᏆᎭᎻ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᏁᏉᏤ ᏴᏫ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏔᏅᎯ ᏄᎾᎵᏍᏔᏁ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ.
050717.phonetic: A-du-i-s-dv-no u-s-qua-lv-hi-di-se-i, na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-se-li-ta-nv-hi tsu-ne-tse-le E-qua-ha-mi, na-s-gi u-ni-ne-quo-tse yv-wi a-le u-ni-tsa-ta-nv-hi nu-na-li-s-ta-ne I-tsi-qui-yi.

050718.english: Till another king arose, which knew not Joseph.
050718.cherokee: ᎬᏂ ᏅᏩᏓᎴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᎾᏄᎪᏨ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᎦᏔᎲᎾ ᏦᏩ.
050718.phonetic: Gv-ni nv-wa-da-le u-gv-wi-yu-hi u-na-nu-go-tsv na-s-gi nu-ga-ta-hv-na Tso-wa.

050719.english: The same dealt subtilly with our kindred, and evil entreated our fathers, so that they cast out their young children, to the end they might not live.
050719.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᏏᎾᏌᏅᏤᎮ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏗᎬᎿᎭ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏲ ᏂᏚᏩᏁᎴ ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎨᎦᏓᎢᏅᏗᏱ ᏧᎾᏍᏗ ᏧᏁᏥ, ᏗᎬᏩᎾᏛᎯᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
050719.phonetic: Na-s-gi da-si-na-sa-nv-tse-he go-hu-s-di di-gv-hna, a-le u-yo ni-du-wa-ne-le di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge-i, na-s-gi di-ge-ga-da-i-nv-di-yi tsu-na-s-di tsu-ne-tsi, di-gv-wa-na-dv-hi-s-di ni-ge-sv-na i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi.

050720.english: In which time Moses was born, and was exceeding fair, and nourished up in his father's house three months:
050720.cherokee: ᎾᎯᏳ ᎼᏏ ᎤᏕᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏔᏅᎯ ᎤᏬᏚᎯᏳ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏥᏍᏆᏂᎪᏔᏁ ᎤᏙᏓ ᎦᏁᎸᎢ ᏦᎢ ᎢᏯᏅᏙ.
050720.phonetic: Na-hi-yu Mo-si u-de-ne-i, a-le u-wa-ta-nv-hi u-wo-du-hi-yu ge-se-i, a-le a-tsi-s-qua-ni-go-ta-ne u-do-da ga-ne-lv-i tso-i i-ya-nv-do.

050721.english: And when he was cast out, Pharaoh's daughter took him up, and nourished him for her own son.
050721.cherokee: ᎠᎦᏓᎢᏅᏃ ᏇᎵᏲ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᎨᏴ ᎤᏁᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏔᏁᎢ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᏰᎸᏁᎢ.
050721.phonetic: A-ga-da-i-nv-no Que-li-yo u-we-tsi a-ge-yv u-ne-se-i, a-le u-s-qua-ni-go-ta-ne-i u-wa-sv u-we-tsi u-ye-lv-ne-i.

050722.english: And Moses was learned in all the wisdom of the Egyptians, and was mighty in words and in deeds.
050722.cherokee: ᏱᏏᏃ ᎠᎨᏲᏅᎯ ᎨᏎ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎿᎭᎢᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏗᏳ ᎨᏎ ᎤᏁᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᏧᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ.
050722.phonetic: Yi-si-no a-ge-yo-nv-hi ge-se ni-ga-v a-ni-ga-ta-hna-i-yu ge-sv I-tsi-qui-yi a-ne-hi, a-le u-li-ni-gi-di-yu ge-se u-ne-i-s-di-yi a-le tsu-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi.

050723.english: And when he was full forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his brethren the children of Israel.
050723.cherokee: ᏅᎦᏍᎪᎯᏃ ᎢᏳᏕᏘᏴᏛ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ, ᎤᏓᏅᏖᎴ ᏧᏩᏛᎲᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎾᎵᏅᏟ ᎢᏏᎵ ᏧᏪᏥ.
050723.phonetic: Nv-ga-s-go-hi-no i-yu-de-ti-yv-dv nu-li-s-ta-nv, u-da-nv-te-le tsu-wa-dv-hv-s-di-yi a-na-li-nv-tli I-si-li tsu-we-tsi.

050724.english: And seeing one of them suffer wrong, he defended him, and avenged him that was oppressed, and smote the Egyptian:
050724.cherokee: ᎤᎪᎲᏃ ᎠᏏᏴᏫ ᎦᏰᏥᏐᏢᏛᎢ, ᎤᏍᏕᎸᎮᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏞᏤᎴ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏯᎬᏁᎸᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎴ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ ᎡᎯ.
050724.phonetic: U-go-hv-no a-si-yv-wi ga-ye-tsi-so-tlv-dv-i, u-s-de-lv-he-i, a-le u-tle-tse-le u-yo i-ya-gv-ne-lv-hi, a-le u-le I-tsi-qui-yi e-hi.

050725.english: For he supposed his brethren would have understood how that God by his hand would deliver them: but they understood not.
050725.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏁᎵᏍᎨᎢ, ᏛᏃᎵᏥ ᎣᏣᎵᏅᏟ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏴ ᎠᏉᏰᏂ ᏗᎬᏔᏂᏒ ᏙᏗᏍᏕᎸᎯᏒᎢ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏳᏃᎵᏤᎢ.
050725.phonetic: A-le hi-a ne-li-s-ge-i, dv-no-li-tsi o-tsa-li-nv-tli U-ne-la-nv-hi a-yv a-quo-ye-ni di-gv-ta-ni-sv do-di-s-de-lv-hi-sv-i; a-se-no v-tla yu-no-li-tse-i.

050726.english: And the next day he shewed himself unto them as they strove, and would have set them at one again, saying, Sirs, ye are brethren; why do ye wrong one to another?
050726.cherokee: ᎤᎩᏨᏛᏃ ᏚᎾᏄᎪᏤᎴ ᎠᎾᎵᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᎪᎯᏍᏓᏁᎮ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; "ᎢᏍᏗᏍᎦᏯ, ᏗᏍᏓᏓᏅᏟᏉ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᎤᏲ ᏂᏙᏍᏓᏓᏛᏁᎭ?"
050726.phonetic: U-gi-tsv-dv-no du-na-nu-go-tse-le a-na-li-hv-i, a-le ga-go-hi-s-da-ne-he hi-a nu-we-se-i; "I-s-di-s-ga-ya, di-s-da-da-nv-tli-quo; ga-do-no u-yo ni-do-s-da-da-dv-ne-ha?"

050727.english: But he that did his neighbour wrong thrust him away, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a judge over us?
050727.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏣᏘᏂ ᎢᏳᏛᏁᎸᎯ ᎾᎥ ᎢᏧᎾᏓᎳ ᏗᎤᏪᏌᏙᏰᏉ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎦᎪ ᏂᏣᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏍᎩᏳᎪᏓᏁᎯ ᎢᏣᎧᏅ ᏂᎯ?
050727.phonetic: A-se-no u-tsa-ti-ni i-yu-dv-ne-lv-hi na-v i-tsu-na-da-la di-u-we-sa-do-ye-quo, hi-a nu-we-se-i; Ga-go ni-tsa-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi a-le di-s-gi-yu-go-da-ne-hi i-tsa-ka-nv ni-hi?

050728.english: Wilt thou kill me, as thou diddest the Egyptian yesterday?
050728.cherokee: ᏥᎪ ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏣᏚᎵ ᏍᎩᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎤᏒᎯ ᏥᎸᎩ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ ᎡᎯ?
050728.phonetic: Tsi-go a-yv na-s-quo tsa-du-li s-gi-hi-s-di-yi na-s-gi-ya u-sv-hi tsi-lv-gi I-tsi-qui-yi e-hi?

050729.english: Then fled Moses at this saying, and was a stranger in the land of Madian, where he begat two sons.
050729.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏪᏒ ᎼᏏ ᎤᎵᏘᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎻᏗᏂ ᎦᏓ ᎠᎲ ᎡᏙᎯ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᏧᏪᏥ ᎬᏩᏕᏁᎴᎢ.
050729.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nu-we-sv Mo-si u-li-ti-se-i, a-le Mi-di-ni ga-da a-hv e-do-hi nu-li-s-ta-ne-i, na-hna-no a-ni-ta-li a-ni-s-ga-ya tsu-we-tsi gv-wa-de-ne-le-i.

050730.english: And when forty years were expired, there appeared to him in the wilderness of mount Sina an angel of the Lord in a flame of fire in a bush.
050730.cherokee: ᏅᎦᏍᎪᎯᏃ ᏄᏕᏘᏴᎲ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏄᏛᏁᎴ ᎢᎾᎨ ᏌᎾᏱ ᎣᏓᎸ ᎤᏪᏲᏓᏛ ᎠᏓᏪᎵᎩᏍᎬᎢ.
050730.phonetic: Nv-ga-s-go-hi-no nu-de-ti-yv-hv di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi U-gv-wi-yu-hi u-tse-li-ga gv-ni-ge-sv nu-dv-ne-le i-na-ge sa-na-yi o-da-lv u-we-yo-da-dv a-da-we-li-gi-s-gv-i.

050731.english: When Moses saw it, he wondered at the sight: and as he drew near to behold it, the voice of the Lord came unto him,
050731.cherokee: ᎼᏏᏃ ᎤᎪᎲ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎠᎪᏩᏘᏍᎬᎢ.  ᎤᎪᎵᏰᏗᏱᏃ ᎤᏰᎸᏅ ᎾᎥ ᏭᎷᏨ ᏗᎪᎵᏰᏎᎢ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎧᏁᎬ ᎤᏛᎦᏁ [ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎨᎢ;]
050731.phonetic: Mo-si-no u-go-hv u-s-qua-ni-go-se nu-s-dv a-go-wa-ti-s-gv-i.  U-go-li-ye-di-yi-no u-ye-lv-nv na-v wu-lu-tsv di-go-li-ye-se-i, U-gv-wi-yu-hi ka-ne-gv u-dv-ga-ne [hi-a ni-ga-we-s-ge-i;]

050732.english: Saying, I am the God of thy fathers, the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. Then Moses trembled, and durst not behold.
050732.cherokee: "ᎠᏴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏗᏣᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎡᏆᎭᎻ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎡᏏᎩ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏤᎦᏈ ᏅᏤᎵᎦ."  ᎣᏏᏃ ᎤᏪᎿᎭᏪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᎦᎴ ᎤᎪᎵᏰᏗᏱ.
050732.phonetic: "A-yv U-ne-la-nv-hi di-tsa-ga-yv-li-ge u-na-tse-li-ga, U-ne-la-nv-hi E-qua-ha-mi u-tse-li-ga, a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi E-si-gi u-tse-li-ga, a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi Tse-ga-qui nv-tse-li-ga."  O-si-no u-we-hna-we-i, a-le u-s-ga-le u-go-li-ye-di-yi.

050733.english: Then said the Lord to him, Put off thy shoes from thy feet: for the place where thou standest is holy ground.
050733.cherokee: ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; "ᏔᎳᏑᎳᎩ, ᎾᎿᎭᏰᏃ ᏥᏙᎦ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎦᏙᎯ.
050733.phonetic: U-gv-wi-yu-hi-no hi-a nu-we-se-le-i; "Ta-la-su-la-gi, na-hna-ye-no tsi-do-ga ga-lv-quo-di-yu ga-do-hi.

050734.english: I have seen, I have seen the affliction of my people which is in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning, and am come down to deliver them. And now come, I will send thee into Egypt.
050734.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎠᎩᎪᎲ ᎤᏲ ᏄᎾᎵᏍᏓᏁᎵᏕᎬ ᏴᏫ ᏗᏆᏤᎵ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ ᏣᏁᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏆᏛᎦᏅ ᏚᏂᎵᏰᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏆᏠᎣᏏᎸ ᏕᎫᏓᎴᏏᎸ; Ꭷ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏓᎬᏅᏏ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ."
050734.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya a-gi-go-hv u-yo nu-na-li-s-da-ne-li-de-gv yv-wi di-qua-tse-li I-tsi-qui-yi tsa-ne-ha, a-le a-qua-dv-ga-nv du-ni-li-ye-di-s-gv-i, a-le a-qua-tlo-o-si-lv de-gu-da-le-si-lv; ka hna-quo da-gv-nv-si I-tsi-qui-yi."

050735.english: This Moses whom they refused, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a judge? the same did God send to be a ruler and a deliverer by the hand of the angel which appeared to him in the bush.
050735.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎼᏏ ᏧᏂᏲᎢᏎᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ, ᎦᎪ ᏂᏣᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏚᎪᏗᏍᎩ ᏣᎧᏅ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏅᏎ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏄᏓᎴᏍᎩ ᏄᏩᏁᎴ ᎤᏩᏔᏁ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏛᏁᎸᎯ ᎤᏪᏲᏓᏛᎢ.
050735.phonetic: Na-s-gi hi-a Mo-si tsu-ni-yo-i-se-le hi-a tsi-nu-ni-we-se-i, Ga-go ni-tsa-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi a-le du-go-di-s-gi tsa-ka-nv, na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-nv-se nu-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi a-le u-nu-da-le-s-gi nu-wa-ne-le u-wa-ta-ne di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi gv-ni-ge-sv i-yu-dv-ne-lv-hi u-we-yo-da-dv-i.

050736.english: He brought them out, after that he had shewed wonders and signs in the land of Egypt, and in the Red sea, and in the wilderness forty years.
050736.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᏘᎿᎭᏫᏛᎮᎢ ᏂᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎶ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏰᎸᏛ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎹ-ᎩᎦᎨᏍᏛᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎢᎾᎨ ᏅᎦᏍᎪᎯ ᏧᏕᏘᏴᏛ.
050736.phonetic: Na-s-gi du-ti-hna-wi-dv-he-i ni-du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lo u-s-qua-ni-go-di a-le u-ye-lv-dv I-tsi-qui-yi a-le A-ma--gi-ga-ge-s-dv-yi a-le i-na-ge nv-ga-s-go-hi tsu-de-ti-yv-dv.

050737.english: This is that Moses, which said unto the children of Israel, A prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me; him shall ye hear.
050737.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎼᏏ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴ ᎢᏏᎵ ᏧᏪᏥ; ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎢᏣᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏓᏥᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᎵ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎢᏣᎵᏅᏟ ᎨᏒ ᏓᎦᎾᏄᎪᏥ ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᏰᏣᏛᎦᏁᎵ.
050737.phonetic: Hi-a na-s-gi Mo-si na-s-gi hi-a tsi-ni-du-we-se-le I-si-li tsu-we-tsi; U-gv-wi-yu-hi I-tsa-ne-la-nv-hi da-tsi-na-nu-go-wi-se-li a-do-le-ho-s-gi i-tsa-li-nv-tli ge-sv da-ga-na-nu-go-tsi a-yv na-s-gi-ya-i; na-s-gi da-ye-tsa-dv-ga-ne-li.

050738.english: This is he, that was in the church in the wilderness with the angel which spake to him in the mount Sina, and with our fathers: who received the lively oracles to give unto us:
050738.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏥᎨᎳᏗᏙᎮ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᏩᏕᎬ ᎢᎾᎨᎢ, ᏔᎵ ᏧᏛᏗᏕᎨ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏁᏤᎴ ᎣᏓᎸ ᏌᎾᏱ, ᏥᎨᎳᏗᏙᎮ ᎠᏁᏙᎲ ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏥᏓᏥᏲᎯᏎᎴ ᎠᏛᏂᏗᏍᏙᏗ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎠᏴ ᎢᎩᏁᏗ.
050738.phonetic: Hi-a na-s-gi na tsi-ge-la-di-do-he u-na-da-tlu-wa-de-gv i-na-ge-i, ta-li tsu-dv-di-de-ge di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi na-s-gi tsu-ne-tse-le o-da-lv Sa-na-yi, tsi-ge-la-di-do-he a-ne-do-hv di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge-i, na-s-gi na-s-quo tsi-da-tsi-yo-hi-se-le a-dv-ni-di-s-do-di ka-no-he-dv a-yv i-gi-ne-di.

050739.english: To whom our fathers would not obey, but thrust him from them, and in their hearts turned back again into Egypt,
050739.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᎥᏝ ᏳᎾᏚᎵᏍᎨ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏗᏱ, ᎢᏴᏛᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᏄᏅᏁᏔᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛ ᎢᏥᏈᏱᏉ ᏫᏂᏚᏅᏁᎢ,
050739.phonetic: Na-s-gi di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge v-tla yu-na-du-li-s-ge u-no-hi-yu-di-yi, i-yv-dv-quo-s-gi-ni nu-nv-ne-ta-ne-i, a-le du-na-da-nv-dv I-tsi-qui-yi-quo wi-ni-du-nv-ne-i,

050740.english: Saying unto Aaron, Make us gods to go before us: for as for this Moses, which brought us out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become of him.
050740.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎴ ᎡᎳᏂ; ᏗᏍᎩᏲᏢᎾᏏ ᏦᎦᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏗᎪᎦᏘᏂᏙᎯ, ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᎼᏏ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ ᏂᏙᏓᏲᎦᏘᏅᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎥᏝ ᏲᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᏄᎵᏍᏓᏁᎸᎢ.
050740.phonetic: Hi-a nu-ni-we-se-le E-la-ni; Di-s-gi-yo-tlv-na-si tso-ga-ne-la-nv-hi di-go-ga-ti-ni-do-hi, hi-a-ye-no Mo-si I-tsi-qui-yi ni-do-da-yo-ga-ti-nv-s-ta-nv-hi v-tla yo-tsi-ga-ta-ha nu-li-s-da-ne-lv-i.

050741.english: And they made a calf in those days, and offered sacrifice unto the idol, and rejoiced in the works of their own hands.
050741.cherokee: ᎾᎯᏳᏃ ᎤᏃᏢᏁ ᏩᎦ ᎠᎩᎾ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏗᏟᎶᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎳᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᏲᎮᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᎵᎮᎵᏤᎴ ᎤᏅᏒ ᏧᏃᏰᏂ ᏧᏅᏔᏅᎯ ᎤᏃᏢᏅᎯ.
050741.phonetic: Na-hi-yu-no u-no-tlv-ne wa-ga a-gi-na, na-s-gi-no di-tli-lo-s-ta-nv-hi a-tsi-lv--ge-la-s-di u-ni-yo-he-le-i, a-le u-na-li-he-li-tse-le u-nv-sv tsu-no-ye-ni tsu-nv-ta-nv-hi u-no-tlv-nv-hi.

050742.english: Then God turned, and gave them up to worship the host of heaven; as it is written in the book of the prophets, O ye house of Israel, have ye offered to me slain beasts and sacrifices by the space of forty years in the wilderness?
050742.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎦᏔᎲᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏁᎳᎩ ᏫᏓᏂᎸᏉᏓ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎦᎸᎶ ᎠᏁᎯ ᏚᏪᎵᏎᎴᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᎬᏅ ᏥᎪᏪᎳ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᏧᏃᏪᎳᏅᎯ; "ᏂᎯ ᎢᏏᎵ ᏏᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏥᎪ ᏕᏍᎩᏃᎮᎸ ᏗᎸᎯ ᎦᎾᏝᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎳᏍᏗ ᏅᎦᏍᎪᎯ ᏥᎾᏕᏘᏴ ᎢᎾᎨᎢ?
050742.phonetic: Hna-quo-no U-ne-la-nv-hi u-ga-ta-hv-se-i, a-le u-na-ne-la-gi wi-da-ni-lv-quo-da u-ni-tsa-ti ga-lv-lo a-ne-hi du-we-li-se-le-i, na-s-gi-ya hi-a tsi-ni-gv-nv tsi-go-we-la a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi tsu-no-we-la-nv-hi; "Ni-hi I-si-li si-da-ne-lv-hi ge-sv-i, tsi-go de-s-gi-no-he-lv di-lv-hi ga-na-tla-i a-le a-tsi-lv--ge-la-s-di nv-ga-s-go-hi tsi-na-de-ti-yv i-na-ge-i?

050743.english: Yea, ye took up the tabernacle of Moloch, and the star of your god Remphan, figures which ye made to worship them: and I will carry you away beyond Babylon.
050743.cherokee: ᎢᏥᎲᏒᏰᏃ ᎦᎵᏦᏙᏗ ᎼᎳᎩ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎢᏥᏰᎸᎯ ᎴᎻᏈᏂ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᏃᏈᏏ, ᏗᏟᎶᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏦᏢᏅᎯ ᏗᏣᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ; ᏓᏗᎶᏂᏃ ᎤᏗᏗᏢ ᏫᏙᏓᏨᏯᎧᏂ."
050743.phonetic: I-tsi-hv-sv-ye-no ga-li-tso-do-di Mo-la-gi u-tse-li-ga a-le u-ne-la-nv-hi i-tsi-ye-lv-hi le-mi-qui-ni u-tse-li-ga no-qui-si, di-tli-lo-s-ta-nv-hi na-s-gi di-tso-tlv-nv-hi di-tsa-da-do-li-s-da-ne-di; Da-di-lo-ni-no u-di-di-tlv wi-do-da-tsv-ya-ka-ni."

050744.english: Our fathers had the tabernacle of witness in the wilderness, as he had appointed, speaking unto Moses, that he should make it according to the fashion that he had seen.
050744.cherokee: ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᎢᎾᎨ ᎤᏂᎮ ᎦᎵᏦᏛ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏙᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎤᏁᏨᎢ ᎼᏏ ᏧᏁᏤᎴ ᎤᏬᏢᏗᏱ ᏧᏟᎶᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎤᎪᎲᎢ.
050744.phonetic: Di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge i-na-ge u-ni-he ga-li-tso-dv a-do-le-ho-hi-s-do-di, na-s-gi-ya u-ne-tsv-i Mo-si tsu-ne-tse-le u-wo-tlv-di-yi tsu-tli-lo-s-do-di-yi nu-s-dv u-go-hv-i.

050745.english: Which also our fathers that came after brought in with Jesus into the possession of the Gentiles, whom God drave out before the face of our fathers, unto the days of David;
050745.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᏗᎨᏥᏲᎯᏎᎸᎯ ᎨᏒ ᏦᏑᏩ ᏧᎾᏖᏉᎶᏎ ᏧᏂᏲᎴ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏥᏚᎨᎯᏕᎴ ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᏕᏫ ᏤᎮ ᎢᏯᏍᏗ.
050745.phonetic: Na-s-gi na-s-quo di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge di-ge-tsi-yo-hi-se-lv-hi ge-sv Tso-su-wa tsu-na-te-quo-lo-se tsu-ni-yo-le tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi u-na-tse-li-go-hi na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi tsi-du-ge-hi-de-le di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge De-wi tse-he i-ya-s-di.

050746.english: Who found favour before God, and desired to find a tabernacle for the God of Jacob.
050746.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎤᏓᏅᏖᏍᎩ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏔᏲᎴ ᎤᏩᏛᏗᏱ ᎤᏁᎳᏗᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏤᎦᏈ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ.
050746.phonetic: Na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi o-si-yu u-da-nv-te-s-gi nu-li-s-ta-ne-i, a-le na-s-gi u-ta-yo-le u-wa-dv-di-yi u-ne-la-di-s-di-yi U-ne-la-nv-hi Tse-ga-qui u-tse-li-ga.

050747.english: But Solomon built him an house.

050748.english: Howbeit the most High dwelleth not in temples made with hands; as saith the prophet,
050748.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᏩᏍᏛ ᎦᎸᎳᏗᏳ ᎡᎯ ᏱᎦᏁᎸ ᏓᏓᏁᎸ ᎩᎶ ᏧᏃᏰᏂ ᏧᏅᏔᏅᎯ ᏧᎾᏁᏍᎨᎲᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᎦᏪᎠ;
050748.phonetic: V-tla-s-gi-ni-no-nv wa-s-dv ga-lv-la-di-yu e-hi yi-ga-ne-lv da-da-ne-lv gi-lo tsu-no-ye-ni tsu-nv-ta-nv-hi tsu-na-ne-s-ge-hv-hi, na-s-gi-ya a-do-le-ho-s-gi hi-a tsi-ni-ga-we-a;

050749.english: Heaven is my throne, and earth is my footstool: what house will ye build me? saith the Lord: or what is the place of my rest?
050749.cherokee: "ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎠᎩᏗᏱ ᎡᎶᎯᏃ ᏗᏆᎳᏏᏗᏱ.  ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏕᏍᏗ ᎦᎵᏦᏕ ᏓᏍᎩᏯᏁᏍᎨᎵ?  ᎠᏗᎭ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ; ᎠᎴ ᎭᏢ ᎠᏆᎵᏙᎯᏍᏙᏗᏱ;
050749.phonetic: "Ga-lv-la-di a-gi-di-yi e-lo-hi-no di-qua-la-si-di-yi.  Ga-do u-s-de-s-di ga-li-tso-de da-s-gi-ya-ne-s-ge-li?  a-di-ha U-gv-wi-yu-hi; a-le ha-tlv a-qua-li-do-hi-s-do-di-yi;

050750.english: Hath not my hand made all these things?
050750.cherokee: ᏝᏍᎪ ᎠᏉᏰᏂ ᎠᏋᏔᏅᎯ ᎠᏉᏢᏅᎯ ᏱᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏥᏄᏍᏗᏓᎾ?"
050750.phonetic: Tla-s-go a-quo-ye-ni a-quv-ta-nv-hi a-quo-tlv-nv-hi yi-gi hi-a ni-ga-dv tsi-nu-s-di-da-na?"

050751.english: Ye stiffnecked and uncircumcised in heart and ears, ye do always resist the Holy Ghost: as your fathers did, so do ye.
050751.cherokee: ᏧᏩᏗᏔᏯ ᏗᏥᎩᎵᎨᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᏕᏥᎤᏍᏕᏎᎸᎾ ᏗᏥᎾᏫ ᎠᎴ ᏕᏥᎵᎷᎬᎢ, ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᎡᏣᏡᏗᏍᎪ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ; ᏗᏥᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᏄᎾᏛᏁᎸ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏂᎯ ᏂᏣᏛᏁᎰᎢ.
050751.phonetic: Tsu-wa-di-ta-ya di-tsi-gi-li-ge-ni, a-le ni-de-tsi-u-s-de-se-lv-na di-tsi-na-wi a-le de-tsi-li-lu-gv-i, ni-go-hi-lv e-tsa-tlu-di-s-go Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do; di-tsi-ga-yv-li-ge nu-na-dv-ne-lv na-s-gi-ya ni-hi ni-tsa-dv-ne-ho-i.

050752.english: Which of the prophets have not your fathers persecuted? and they have slain them which shewed before of the coming of the Just One; of whom ye have been now the betrayers and murderers:
050752.cherokee: ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎨᏒ ᏗᏥᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏳᏅᏁᎸᎯ ᏱᎩ?  ᎠᎴ ᏕᎤᏂᎸ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᏒᎯ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏅᏁᎸᎯ ᎤᎷᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎦᏅᎾ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎯ ᎡᏥᎶᏄᎡᎸᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎡᏥᎸᎯ ᏥᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ;
050752.phonetic: Ga-do u-s-di na-s-gi na a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi ge-sv di-tsi-ga-yv-li-ge v-tla u-yo i-yu-nv-ne-lv-hi yi-gi?  A-le de-u-ni-lv u-na-do-le-ho-sv-hi gv-ni-ge-sv i-yu-nv-ne-lv-hi u-lu-hi-s-di-yi na-s-ga-nv-na na-s-gi ni-hi e-tsi-lo-nu-e-lv-hi a-le e-tsi-lv-hi tsi-nu-li-s-ta-nv;

050753.english: Who have received the law by the disposition of angels, and have not kept it.
050753.cherokee: ᏤᏥᏲᎯᏎᎸᎩ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏗᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏄᏅᎿᎭᏕᎬᎢ ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᎢᏥᏍᏆᏂᎪᏔᏅᎯ ᏱᎩ.
050753.phonetic: Tse-tsi-yo-hi-se-lv-gi di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di di-ni-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi o-s-dv nu-nv-hna-de-gv-i a-se-no v-tla i-tsi-s-qua-ni-go-ta-nv-hi yi-gi.

050754.english: When they heard these things, they were cut to the heart, and they gnashed on him with their teeth.
050754.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ ᏚᏂᏘᎴ ᏧᏂᎾᏫᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᏄᏙᎬ ᏕᎬᏩᏄᏓᎨᎴᎢ.
050754.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-na-dv-ga-nv du-ni-ti-le tsu-ni-na-wi-yi, a-le di-ni-nu-do-gv de-gv-wa-nu-da-ge-le-i.

050755.english: But he, being full of the Holy Ghost, looked up stedfastly into heaven, and saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing on the right hand of God,
050755.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᎧᎵᏨᎯ ᎨᏎ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᏯᏅᏒᎯ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏫᏚᎧᎿᎭᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏭᎪᎮ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᏌ ᎦᏙᎨ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎦᏘᏏᏗᏢ;
050755.phonetic: A-se-no u-ka-li-tsv-hi ge-se Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do u-ya-nv-sv-hi ga-lv-la-di wi-du-ka-hna-ne-i, a-le wu-go-he ga-lv-quo-di-yu ge-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga, a-le Tsi-sa ga-do-ge U-ne-la-nv-hi a-ga-ti-si-di-tlv;

050756.english: And said, Behold, I see the heavens opened, and the Son of man standing on the right hand of God.
050756.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎤᎵᏍᏚᎢᏛ ᏥᎪᏩᏘᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎦᏙᎦ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎦᏘᏏᏗᏢ.
050756.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-we-se-i; Gv-ni-yu-quo ga-lv-la-di u-li-s-du-i-dv tsi-go-wa-ti-ha, a-le Yv-wi U-we-tsi ga-do-ga U-ne-la-nv-hi a-ga-ti-si-di-tlv.

050757.english: Then they cried out with a loud voice, and stopped their ears, and ran upon him with one accord,
050757.cherokee: ᎠᏍᏓᏯᏃ ᎤᏁᎷᏅ ᏗᏏᎴᏂ ᏚᏂᏍᏚᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎴᏃᏅᎯ ᎤᎾᏁᎷᎩᎡᎴᎢ;
050757.phonetic: A-s-da-ya-no u-ne-lu-nv di-si-le-ni du-ni-s-du-ne-i, a-le u-le-no-nv-hi u-na-ne-lu-gi-e-le-i;

050758.english: And cast him out of the city, and stoned him: and the witnesses laid down their clothes at a young man's feet, whose name was Saul.
050758.cherokee: ᎦᏚᎲᏃ ᎤᏂᏄᎪᏫᏒ ᏅᏯ ᏚᏅᏂᏍᏔᏁᎢ.  ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎯᏃ ᏧᎾᏅᏬ ᏚᏂᏅᏁ ᏚᎳᏍᎬ ᎾᎥ ᎠᏫᏅ ᏐᎳ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ.
050758.phonetic: Ga-du-hv-no u-ni-nu-go-wi-sv nv-ya du-nv-ni-s-ta-ne-i.  A-ni-ga-ta-hi-no tsu-na-nv-wo du-ni-nv-ne du-la-s-gv na-v a-wi-nv So-la tsu-do-i-dv.

050759.english: And they stoned Stephen, calling upon God, and saying, Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.
050759.cherokee: ᏅᏯᏃ ᏚᏅᏂᏍᏔᏁ ᏍᏗᏫ ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗᏍᎨᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎨᎢ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᏥᏌ, ᏔᏓᏂᎸᎩ ᎠᏆᏓᏅᏙᎩ.
050759.phonetic: Nv-ya-no du-nv-ni-s-ta-ne S-di-wi a-da-do-li-s-di-s-ge-i, hi-a ni-ga-we-s-ge-i; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, Tsi-sa, ta-da-ni-lv-gi a-qua-da-nv-do-gi.

050760.english: And he kneeled down, and cried with a loud voice, Lord, lay not this sin to their charge. And when he had said this, he fell asleep.
050760.cherokee: ᏚᎵᏂᏆᏅᏅᏃ ᎠᏍᏓᏯ ᎤᏪᎷᏁᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᎯᎠ ᎤᏂᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᏱᎦᎯᏍᏔᏁᏍᏗ.  ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏪᏒ ᎤᎸᏁᎢ.
050760.phonetic: Du-li-ni-qua-nv-nv-no a-s-da-ya u-we-lu-ne-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, tle-s-di hi-a u-ni-s-ga-nv-tsv yi-ga-hi-s-ta-ne-s-di.  Na-s-gi-no nu-we-sv u-lv-ne-i.

050801.english: And Saul was consenting unto his death. And at that time there was a great persecution against the church which was at Jerusalem; and they were all scattered abroad throughout the regions of Judaea and Samaria, except the apostles.
050801.cherokee: ᏐᎳᏃ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎠᏰᎸᏍᎨ ᎠᏥᎸᎢ.  ᎾᎯᏳᏃ ᎤᎶᏘ ᎤᏲ ᏂᎨᎬᎾᏕᎨ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏈᎬ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ; ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᎾᏗᎦᎴᏲᏤ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᏧᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᏌᎺᎵᏱ, ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛ ᎤᏅᏒ.
050801.phonetic: So-la-no o-si-yu a-ye-lv-s-ge a-tsi-lv-i.  Na-hi-yu-no u-lo-ti u-yo ni-ge-gv-na-de-ge tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-qui-gv Tsi-lu-si-li-mi; a-le ni-ga-dv u-na-di-ga-le-yo-tse ni-gv-na-dv Tsu-di-yi a-le Sa-me-li-yi, ge-tsi-nv-si-dv u-nv-sv.

050802.english: And devout men carried Stephen to his burial, and made great lamentation over him.
050802.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏂᎾᏰᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᎾᏫᏛᎮ ᏍᏗᏫ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏔᏅᎯ ᎤᏂᏍᎪᏂᎴᎢ.
050802.phonetic: A-ni-s-ga-ya-no U-ne-la-nv-hi a-ni-na-ye-s-gi u-ni-na-wi-dv-he S-di-wi, a-le u-tsa-ta-nv-hi u-ni-s-go-ni-le-i.

050803.english: As for Saul, he made havock of the church, entering into every house, and haling men and women committed them to prison.
050803.cherokee: ᏐᎳᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᏲ ᏂᏕᎬᏁᎮ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏈᎬᎢ, ᏓᏴᎵᏙᎮ ᏓᏓᏁᎳᏗᏒᎢ, ᏕᎦᎾᏌᏁᏏᏙᎮ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏗᏱ ᏓᏴᏗᏍᎨᎢ.
050803.phonetic: So-la-s-gi-ni u-tsa-ti u-yo ni-de-gv-ne-he tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-qui-gv-i, da-yv-li-do-he da-da-ne-la-di-sv-i, de-ga-na-sa-ne-si-do-he a-ni-s-ga-ya a-le a-ni-ge-yv, a-le di-da-s-du-di-yi da-yv-di-s-ge-i.

050804.english: Therefore they that were scattered abroad went every where preaching the word.

050805.english: Then Philip went down to the city of Samaria, and preached Christ unto them.
050805.cherokee: ᏈᎵᎩᏃ ᏗᎦᏚᎲ ᏌᎺᎵᏱ ᏭᎶᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎵᏥᏙᏁᎴ ᏚᏃᎮᎮᎴ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ.
050805.phonetic: Qui-li-gi-no di-ga-du-hv Sa-me-li-yi wu-lo-se-i, a-le du-li-tsi-do-ne-le du-no-he-he-le Ga-lo-ne-dv.

050806.english: And the people with one accord gave heed unto those things which Philip spake, hearing and seeing the miracles which he did.
050806.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᏌᏉ ᎢᎦᎦᏛ ᎤᎾᎦᏌᏯᏍᏔᏁ ᏈᎵᎩ ᎧᏃᎮᏍᎬᎢ, ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᎪᎲ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲᎢ.
050806.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-ti-no sa-quo i-ga-ga-dv u-na-ga-sa-ya-s-ta-ne Qui-li-gi ka-no-he-s-gv-i, u-na-dv-ga-nv a-le u-ni-go-hv u-s-qua-ni-go-di du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv-i.

050807.english: For unclean spirits, crying with loud voice, came out of many that were possessed with them: and many taken with palsies, and that were lame, were healed.
050807.cherokee: ᎠᏂᎦᏓᎭᏰᏃ ᏗᏓᏅᏙ ᎠᏍᏓᏯ ᎠᏁᎷᎲᏍᎨ ᏕᎬᏩᏂᏄᎪᎨ ᎤᏂᏣᏖ ᎬᏩᏂᏯᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏤᏖ ᏧᏂᎾᏫᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᏲᎤᎵ ᏚᎾᏗᏩᏍᎨᎢ.
050807.phonetic: A-ni-ga-da-ha-ye-no di-da-nv-do a-s-da-ya a-ne-lu-hv-s-ge de-gv-wa-ni-nu-go-ge u-ni-tsa-te gv-wa-ni-ya-i, a-le u-ni-tse-te tsu-ni-na-wi-s-gi a-le di-ni-yo-u-li du-na-di-wa-s-ge-i.

050808.english: And there was great joy in that city.

050809.english: But there was a certain man, called Simon, which beforetime in the same city used sorcery, and bewitched the people of Samaria, giving out that himself was some great one:
050809.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎾᎿᎭᎡᎮ ᏌᏩᏂ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᎿᎭᎦᏚᎲ ᎠᏙᏅᏗ ᏧᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏧᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏎ ᏌᎺᎵᏱ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗ ᎠᏤᎸᏍᎨᎢ.
050809.phonetic: Gi-lo-s-gi-ni-no-nv i-yu-s-di a-s-ga-ya na-hna e-he Sa-wa-ni tsu-do-i-dv na-s-gi na-hna ga-du-hv a-do-nv-di tsu-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv-hi ge-se-i, a-le u-s-qua-ni-go-di tsu-da-nv-da-di-s-ta-nv-hi ge-se Sa-me-li-yi a-ne-hi, gi-lo i-yu-s-di a-tsi-lv-quo-di a-tse-lv-s-ge-i.

050810.english: To whom they all gave heed, from the least to the greatest, saying, This man is the great power of God.
050810.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎬᏬᎯᏳᎲᏍᎨ ᎾᏂᎥ ᎨᏥᎸᏉᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎨᏥᎸᏉᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ; ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏗᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏪᎭ.
050810.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no gv-wo-hi-yu-hv-s-ge na-ni-v ge-tsi-lv-quo-di a-le ge-tsi-lv-quo-di ni-ge-sv-na, hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i; U-tsa-ti u-li-ni-gi-di-yu ge-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga hi-a a-s-ga-ya u-we-ha.

050811.english: And to him they had regard, because that of long time he had bewitched them with sorceries.
050811.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎬᏬᎯᏳᎲᏍᎨᎢ, ᎪᎯᏗᏳᏰᏃ ᎬᏩᎴᏅᏛ ᎨᏎ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏓᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏗᏍᎨ ᎠᏙᏂᏍᎬᎢ.
050811.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no gv-wo-hi-yu-hv-s-ge-i, go-hi-di-yu-ye-no gv-wa-le-nv-dv ge-se u-s-qua-ni-go-di da-da-nv-da-di-s-di-s-ge a-do-ni-s-gv-i.

050812.english: But when they believed Philip preaching the things concerning the kingdom of God, and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.
050812.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏅ ᏈᎵᎩ ᎠᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎬ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏕᎤᏙᎥ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ, ᏕᎨᎦᏬᏍᎨ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ.
050812.phonetic: A-se-no u-no-hi-yu-nv Qui-li-gi a-li-tsi-do-hv-s-gv o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv a-le de-u-do-v Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv u-gv-wa-li, de-ge-ga-wo-s-ge a-ni-s-ga-ya a-le a-ni-ge-yv.

050813.english: Then Simon himself believed also: and when he was baptized, he continued with Philip, and wondered, beholding the miracles and signs which were done.
050813.cherokee: ᏌᏩᏂᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏬᎯᏳᏁᎢ, ᎠᎦᏬᎥᏃ ᎤᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎴ ᏈᎵᎩ; ᎠᎪᏩᏗᏍᎬᏃ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏰᎸᏛ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲᎢ, ᎠᏍᏆᏂᎪᏍᎨᎢ.
050813.phonetic: Sa-wa-ni-no na-s-quo u-wo-hi-yu-ne-i, a-ga-wo-v-no u-s-da-wa-di-do-le Qui-li-gi; a-go-wa-di-s-gv-no u-s-qua-ni-go-di a-le u-ye-lv-dv du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv-i, a-s-qua-ni-go-s-ge-i.

050814.english: Now when the apostles which were at Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent unto them Peter and John:
050814.cherokee: ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛᏃ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎠᏂᏂ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ ᏌᎺᎵᏱ ᏕᎤᎾᏓᏂᎸᏨ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ, ᏚᏂᏅᏎ ᏈᏓ ᎠᎴ ᏣᏂ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏂᏩᏛᎲᏍᏗᏱ.
050814.phonetic: Ge-tsi-nv-si-dv-no Tsi-lu-si-li-mi a-ni-ni u-na-dv-ga-nv Sa-me-li-yi de-u-na-da-ni-lv-tsv ka-no-he-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga, du-ni-nv-se Qui-da a-le Tsa-ni na-s-gi tsu-ni-wa-dv-hv-s-di-yi.

050815.english: Who, when they were come down, prayed for them, that they might receive the Holy Ghost:
050815.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏭᏂᎷᏨ ᎤᎾᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏁ ᎨᎦᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ.
050815.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no wu-ni-lu-tsv u-na-da-do-li-s-ta-ne ge-ga-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-di-yi Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do.

050816.english: (For as yet he was fallen upon none of them: only they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.)
050816.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎠᏏ ᏳᏓᏑᏰ ᎩᎶ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎷᏤᎸᎯ, ᏗᎨᎦᏬᏍᏔᏅᎯᏉ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎨᏎ ᏕᎤᏙᎥ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ.
050816.phonetic: V-tla-ye-no a-si yu-da-su-ye gi-lo na-s-gi u-lu-tse-lv-hi, di-ge-ga-wo-s-ta-nv-hi-quo u-wa-sv ge-se de-u-do-v U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa.

050817.english: Then laid they their hands on them, and they received the Holy Ghost.
050817.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏚᎾᏏᏔᏕᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎨᏥᏁᎴ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ.
050817.phonetic: Hna-quo-no du-na-si-ta-de-i, a-le na-s-gi ge-tsi-ne-le Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do.

050818.english: And when Simon saw that through laying on of the apostles' hands the Holy Ghost was given, he offered them money,
050818.cherokee: ᏌᏩᏂᏃ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎨᏥᏁᎲᎢ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᏓᎾᏏᏔᏗᏍᎬ ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏕᎤᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᎴ ᎠᏕᎸ,
050818.phonetic: Sa-wa-ni-no u-do-le-ho-sv Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do ge-tsi-ne-hv-i nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv da-na-si-ta-di-s-gv ge-tsi-nv-si-dv, na-s-gi de-u-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-le a-de-lv,

050819.english: Saying, Give me also this power, that on whomsoever I lay hands, he may receive the Holy Ghost.
050819.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏍᎩᏂᎥᏏ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎩᎶ ᏥᏯᏏᏔᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᏥᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ.
050819.phonetic: Hi-a nu-we-se-i; A-yv na-s-quo s-gi-ni-v-si hi-a na-s-gi di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di, na-s-gi gi-lo tsi-ya-si-ta-di-s-ge-s-di a-tsi-ne-di ge-se-s-di Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do.

050820.english: But Peter said unto him, Thy money perish with thee, because thou hast thought that the gift of God may be purchased with money.
050820.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏈᏓ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎠᏕᎸ ᏗᏣᏤᎵ ᏫᏓᎵᏗᏛᏓ ᏨᏒ ᎭᎵᏛᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏰᎵᏉ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎬᏩᎯᏍᏙᏗ ᏤᎳ.
050820.phonetic: A-se-no Qui-da hi-a nu-we-se-le-i; A-de-lv di-tsa-tse-li wi-da-li-di-dv-da tsv-sv ha-li-dv-di-s-gv-i, nv-da-ga-li-s-do-da U-ne-la-nv-hi u-da-ne-di ge-sv ye-li-quo a-de-lv gv-wa-hi-s-do-di tse-la.

050821.english: Thou hast neither part nor lot in this matter: for thy heart is not right in the sight of God.
050821.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏣᏤᎵ ᏳᏓᏑᏯ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏂ, ᏝᏰᏃ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᏱᎩ ᏣᎾᏫ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎦᏔᎲᎢ.
050821.phonetic: V-tla go-hu-s-di tsa-tse-li yu-da-su-ya hi-a a-ni, tla-ye-no du-yu-go-dv yi-gi tsa-na-wi U-ne-la-nv-hi a-ga-ta-hv-i.

050822.english: Repent therefore of this thy wickedness, and pray God, if perhaps the thought of thine heart may be forgiven thee.
050822.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏲ ᎯᏰᎸᎾ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏍᎦᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᏯᏙᏓᎵᏍᏓᏏ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᎦᏰᏣᏙᎵᏍᏗᏉ ᏱᏂᎦᎩ ᏄᏍᏛ ᏣᎾᏫᏱ ᏣᏓᏅᏖᎸᎢ.
050822.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di u-yo hi-ye-lv-na hi-a na-s-gi tsi-s-ga-nv-gi, a-le hi-ya-do-da-li-s-da-si U-ne-la-nv-hi, ga-ye-tsa-do-li-s-di-quo yi-ni-ga-gi nu-s-dv tsa-na-wi-yi tsa-da-nv-te-lv-i.

050823.english: For I perceive that thou art in the gall of bitterness, and in the bond of iniquity.
050823.cherokee: ᎦᏙᎴᎣᏍᎦᏰᏃ ᏣᏚᎲ ᎤᏴᏍᏗ ᎠᏓᎪᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏍᎦᏂ ᏣᏓᎸᏍᏛᎢ.
050823.phonetic: Ga-do-le-o-s-ga-ye-no tsa-du-hv u-yv-s-di a-da-go-hi, a-le a-s-ga-ni tsa-da-lv-s-dv-i.

050824.english: Then answered Simon, and said, Pray ye to the Lord for me, that none of these things which ye have spoken come upon me.
050824.cherokee: ᏌᏩᏂᏃ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏂᎯ ᎡᏍᏗᏔᏲᏏ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᎩᏙᎵᏍᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏌᏉᎤᏅ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏍᏗᏁᎩ ᏯᎩᎷᏤᎴᏍᏗ.
050824.phonetic: Sa-wa-ni-no u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-se-i; Ni-hi e-s-di-ta-yo-si U-gv-wi-yu-hi a-gi-do-li-s-di-yi, na-s-gi-no tle-s-di sa-quo-u-nv hi-a tsi-s-di-ne-gi ya-gi-lu-tse-le-s-di.

050825.english: And they, when they had testified and preached the word of the Lord, returned to Jerusalem, and preached the gospel in many villages of the Samaritans.
050825.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᎸ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᎵᏥᏙᏅ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᎤᎾᏨᏎ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏭᏂᎶᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏖ ᏌᎺᎵᏱ ᎠᏁᎯ ᏚᏂᏚᎲ ᎤᎾᎵᏥᏙᏁ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ.
050825.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-ni-no-he-lv a-le u-na-li-tsi-do-nv ka-no-he-dv U-gv-wi-yu-hi u-tse-li-ga u-na-tsv-se Tsi-lu-si-li-mi wu-ni-lo-se-i, a-le u-tsa-te Sa-me-li-yi a-ne-hi du-ni-du-hv u-na-li-tsi-do-ne o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv.

050826.english: And the angel of the Lord spake unto Philip, saying, Arise, and go toward the south unto the way that goeth down from Jerusalem unto Gaza, which is desert.
050826.cherokee: ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎤᏁᏤᎴ ᏈᎵᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏔᎴᎲᎦ ᏧᎦᎾᏮ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏫᎶᎯ ᏂᎦᏅᏅᎢ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎨᏏᏃ, ᎾᎿᎭᎢᎾᎨ ᏥᎩ.
050826.phonetic: Di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi-no U-gv-wi-yu-hi u-tse-li u-ne-tse-le Qui-li-gi, hi-a nu-we-se-i; Ta-le-hv-ga tsu-ga-na-wv i-di-tlv wi-lo-hi ni-ga-nv-nv-i Tsi-lu-si-li-mi Ge-si-no, na-hna i-na-ge tsi-gi.

050827.english: And he arose and went: and, behold, a man of Ethiopia, an eunuch of great authority under Candace queen of the Ethiopians, who had the charge of all her treasure, and had come to Jerusalem for to worship,
050827.cherokee: ᏚᎴᏅᏃ ᎤᏪᏅᏎᎢ; ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎢᏘᏱᏈ ᎡᎯ ᎠᏳᎾᎦ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗ ᎤᏁᏤᎸᎯ ᎬᏗᏏ ᎠᎨᏴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎢᏗᎣᏈ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎤᎦᏘᏕᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏂᎸᎯ ᎨᏎ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ,
050827.phonetic: Du-le-nv-no u-we-nv-se-i; gv-ni-yu-quo-no a-s-ga-ya I-ti-yi-qui e-hi a-yu-na-ga a-tsi-lv-quo-di u-ne-tse-lv-hi Gv-di-si a-ge-yv u-gv-wi-yu-hi I-di-o-qui a-ne-hi u-na-tse-li-ga, na-s-gi ni-ga-dv a-de-lv u-ga-ti-de-hi, na-s-gi u-da-do-li-s-ta-ni-lv-hi ge-se Tsi-lu-si-li-mi,

050828.english: Was returning, and sitting in his chariot read Esaias the prophet.
050828.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᏨᏏᏗᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᏆᎴᎷᎯ ᎤᏣᎡᎢ, ᎠᎪᎵᏰᏍᎨ ᎢᏌᏯ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ.
050828.phonetic: Hna-quo-no u-tsv-si-di-se-i, a-le da-qua-le-lu-hi u-tsa-e-i, a-go-li-ye-s-ge I-sa-ya a-do-le-ho-s-gi.

050829.english: Then the Spirit said unto Philip, Go near, and join thyself to this chariot.
050829.cherokee: ᎠᏓᏅᏙᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᏈᎵᎩ; ᎾᎥ ᏫᎷᎩ ᏫᏯᎵᎪᎲᏏ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏓᏆᎴᎷ.
050829.phonetic: A-da-nv-do-no hi-a nu-we-se-le Qui-li-gi; Na-v wi-lu-gi wi-ya-li-go-hv-si na-s-gi na da-qua-le-lu.

050830.english: And Philip ran thither to him, and heard him read the prophet Esaias, and said, Understandest thou what thou readest?
050830.cherokee: ᏈᎵᎩᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᏭᏗᏠᎸ ᎤᏛᎦᏁ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎢᏌᏯ ᎠᎪᎵᏰᏍᎬᎢ, ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎰᎵᎦᎨᎵ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᎪᎵᏰᎭ?
050830.phonetic: Qui-li-gi-no na-hna wu-di-tlo-lv u-dv-ga-ne a-do-le-ho-s-gi I-sa-ya a-go-li-ye-s-gv-i, hi-a-no nu-we-se-i; Ho-li-ga-ge-li na-s-gi tsi-go-li-ye-ha?

050831.english: And he said, How can I, except some man should guide me? And he desired Philip that he would come up and sit with him.
050831.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎦᏙ ᏱᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎾᏆᏎᎮᎲᎾ ᏱᎩ?  ᎤᏔᏲᎴᏃ ᏈᎵᎩ ᎤᏦᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᏧᏂᏗᏱ.
050831.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hi-a nu-we-se-i; Ga-do yi-ga-li-s-do-da, i-yu-no gi-lo na-qua-se-he-hv-na yi-gi?  U-ta-yo-le-no Qui-li-gi u-tso-di-yi a-le i-tsu-la-ha tsu-ni-di-yi.

050832.english: The place of the scripture which he read was this, He was led as a sheep to the slaughter; and like a lamb dumb before his shearer, so opened he not his mouth:
050832.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎪᏪᎸ ᎠᎪᎵᏰᏍᎨᎢ; "ᎠᎦᏘᏃᎸᎩ ᎠᏫ ᎤᏃᏕᎾ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏗᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎠᎩᎾ ᎠᏫ ᎡᎳᏪ ᏥᎨᏐ ᎬᏩᏍᏰᏍᎩ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎥᏝ ᏳᏍᏚᎢᏎ ᎠᎰᎵ.
050832.phonetic: Hi-a na-s-gi go-we-lv a-go-li-ye-s-ge-i; "A-ga-ti-no-lv-gi a-wi u-no-de-na na-s-gi-ya di-hi-s-di-yi, a-le na-s-gi-ya a-gi-na a-wi e-la-we tsi-ge-so gv-wa-s-ye-s-gi a-ni-ga-ta-hv-i, na-s-gi-ya v-tla yu-s-du-i-se a-ho-li.

050833.english: In his humiliation his judgment was taken away: and who shall declare his generation? for his life is taken from the earth.
050833.cherokee: ᎤᏲ ᏄᏛᎿᎭᏕᎬᎢ ᎠᏥᎩᎡᎸᎩ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎨᏒ ᏚᏳᎪᏛᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎦᎪ ᏙᏓᎧᏃᎮᎵ ᏴᏫ ᎾᎯᏳ ᏤᎮ ᎠᏁᎯ?  ᎬᏅᏰᏃ ᎤᏂᎩᏒᎩ ᎡᎶᎯ."
050833.phonetic: U-yo nu-dv-hna-de-gv-i a-tsi-gi-e-lv-gi u-tse-li ge-sv du-yu-go-dv-i; a-le ga-go do-da-ka-no-he-li yv-wi na-hi-yu tse-he a-ne-hi?  gv-nv-ye-no u-ni-gi-sv-gi e-lo-hi."

050834.english: And the eunuch answered Philip, and said, I pray thee, of whom speaketh the prophet this? of himself, or of some other man?
050834.cherokee: ᎠᏳᎾᎦᏃ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᏈᎵᎩ; ᎦᎪ ᎧᏃᎮᎭ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏂᎦᏪᏎ?  ᎤᏩᏒᏍᎪ, ᏅᏩᏓᎴᎨ ᎩᎶᎢ?
050834.phonetic: A-yu-na-ga-no u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-se-le Qui-li-gi; Ga-go ka-no-he-ha a-do-le-ho-s-gi hi-a na-s-gi tsi-ni-ga-we-se?  U-wa-sv-s-go, nv-wa-da-le-ge gi-lo-i?

050835.english: Then Philip opened his mouth, and began at the same scripture, and preached unto him Jesus.
050835.cherokee: ᏈᎵᎩᏃ ᎤᏍᏚᎢᏒ ᎠᎰᎵ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᎿᎭᎪᏪᎸ ᎤᎴᏅᎮᎢ ᎤᏃᎮᎮᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏥᏌ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ.
050835.phonetic: Qui-li-gi-no u-s-du-i-sv a-ho-li, na-s-gi na-hna go-we-lv u-le-nv-he-i u-no-he-he-le o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv Tsi-sa u-gv-wa-li.

050836.english: And as they went on their way, they came unto a certain water: and the eunuch said, See, here is water; what doth hinder me to be baptized?
050836.cherokee: ᎠᎾᎢᏒᏃ ᏅᏃᎯ, ᎠᎹᏱ ᎤᏂᎷᏤᎢ.  ᎠᏳᎾᎦᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎠᏂ ᎠᎹ, ᎦᏙ ᎠᎩᏲᏍᏙᏓᏁ ᎥᏆᏬᏍᏗᏱ?
050836.phonetic: A-na-i-sv-no nv-no-hi, a-ma-yi u-ni-lu-tse-i.  A-yu-na-ga-no hi-a nu-we-se-i; A-ni a-ma, ga-do a-gi-yo-s-do-da-ne v-qua-wo-s-di-yi?

050837.english: And Philip said, If thou believest with all thine heart, thou mayest. And he answered and said, I believe that Jesus Christ is the Son of God.
050837.cherokee: ᏈᎵᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏰᎵᏉ ᎢᏳᏃ ᏂᎦᎥ ᏣᎾᏫ ᏴᏗᎭ ᏲᎯᏳᎲᏍᎦ.  ᎤᏁᏨᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎪᎢᏳᎲᏍᎦ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎨᏒᎢ.
050837.phonetic: Qui-li-gi-no hi-a nu-we-se-i; ye-li-quo i-yu-no ni-ga-v tsa-na-wi yv-di-ha yo-hi-yu-hv-s-ga.  U-ne-tsv-no hi-a nu-we-se-i; Go-i-yu-hv-s-ga Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi U-we-tsi ge-sv-i.

050838.english: And he commanded the chariot to stand still: and they went down both into the water, both Philip and the eunuch; and he baptized him.
050838.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏤᏃ ᏓᏆᎴᎷ ᎤᎴᏫᏍᏙᏗᏱ.  ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏭᏂᎦᏐᎠᏏᎴ ᎠᎹᏱ ᎢᏧᎳ ᏈᎵᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏳᎾᎦ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᎦᏬᎡᎢ.
050838.phonetic: U-ne-tse-no da-qua-le-lu u-le-wi-s-do-di-yi.  Hna-quo-no wu-ni-ga-so-a-si-le a-ma-yi i-tsu-la Qui-li-gi a-le a-yu-na-ga, na-s-gi-no a-ga-wo-e-i.

050839.english: And when they were come up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord caught away Philip, that the eunuch saw him no more: and he went on his way rejoicing.
050839.cherokee: ᎠᎹᏱᏃ ᏧᎾᏓᏅᏒ ᏧᏂᎿᎭᎷᏒ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᏘᎿᎭᏫᏛᎮ ᏈᎵᎩ, ᎠᏳᎾᎦᏃ Ᏺ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎣᏂ ᏳᎪᎮᎢ, ᎤᏩᏒᏰᏃ ᏩᎦᏛᎢ ᏭᎶᏎ ᎠᎵᎮᎵᎨᎢ.
050839.phonetic: A-ma-yi-no tsu-na-da-nv-sv tsu-ni-hna-lu-sv, U-gv-wi-yu-hi U-da-nv-do u-ti-hna-wi-dv-he Qui-li-gi, a-yu-na-ga-no yo hna-quo o-ni yu-go-he-i, u-wa-sv-ye-no wa-ga-dv-i wu-lo-se a-li-he-li-ge-i.

050840.english: But Philip was found at Azotus: and passing through he preached in all the cities, till he came to Caesarea.
050840.cherokee: ᏈᎵᎩᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᏥᏩᏛᎮ ᎠᏦᏓ; ᎠᎢᏒᏃ ᎠᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎨ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏕᎦᏚᎲ ᎬᏂ ᏏᏌᎵᏱ ᏭᎷᏨ.
050840.phonetic: Qui-li-gi-s-gi-ni a-tsi-wa-dv-he A-tso-da; a-i-sv-no a-li-tsi-do-hv-s-ge o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv ni-ga-dv de-ga-du-hv gv-ni Si-sa-li-yi wu-lu-tsv.

050901.english: And Saul, yet breathing out threatenings and slaughter against the disciples of the Lord, went unto the high priest,
050901.cherokee: ᏐᎳᏃ ᎠᏏᏉ ᎬᏬᎳᏕᏍᎨ ᏕᎦᎾᏰᏓᏁᎮ ᎠᎴ ᏧᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎧᏁᎨ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎶᎯ ᏧᏬᎸ ᏭᎶᏎᎢ,
050901.phonetic: So-la-no a-si-quo gv-wo-la-de-s-ge de-ga-na-ye-da-ne-he a-le tsu-hi-s-di-yi ka-ne-ge U-gv-wi-yu-hi gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi, nu-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--ge-lo-hi tsu-wo-lv wu-lo-se-i,

050902.english: And desired of him letters to Damascus to the synagogues, that if he found any of this way, whether they were men or women, he might bring them bound unto Jerusalem.
050902.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏚᏔᏲᏎᎴ ᎪᏪᎵ ᏧᏅᏍᏗ ᏕᎹᏍᎦ ᏗᎦᏚᎲ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᏳ ᏱᏚᏩᏛᎲ ᎩᎶ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏗᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏕᎩ, ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎨᎦᎸᎢᏛ ᏧᏘᏃᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ.
050902.phonetic: A-le du-ta-yo-se-le go-we-li tsu-nv-s-di De-ma-s-ga di-ga-du-hv di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi, na-s-gi-no i-yu yi-du-wa-dv-hv gi-lo na-s-gi i-yu-s-di di-ni-ka-hna-wa-de-gi, a-ni-s-ga-ya a-le a-ni-ge-yv, na-s-gi di-ge-ga-lv-i-dv tsu-ti-no-hi-s-di-yi Tsi-lu-si-li-mi.

050903.english: And as he journeyed, he came near Damascus: and suddenly there shined round about him a light from heaven:
050903.cherokee: ᎠᎢᏒᏃ ᏕᎹᏍᎦ ᎾᎥ ᏭᎷᏤᎢ, ᎤᏰᎶᎢᏍᏔᏁᏃ ᎢᎦᎦᏘ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏅᏓᏳᎶᏒᎯ ᏚᏚᏫᏍᏔᏁᎢ.
050903.phonetic: A-i-sv-no De-ma-s-ga na-v wu-lu-tse-i, u-ye-lo-i-s-ta-ne-no i-ga-ga-ti ga-lv-la-di nv-da-yu-lo-sv-hi du-du-wi-s-ta-ne-i.

050904.english: And he fell to the earth, and heard a voice saying unto him, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?
050904.cherokee: ᎡᎳᏗᏃ ᎤᏅᏨ ᎤᏛᎦᏁ ᎩᎶ ᎧᏁᎬᎢ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎲᎢ; ᏐᎳ, ᏐᎳ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎤᏲ ᏂᏍᏋᏁᎭ?
050904.phonetic: E-la-di-no u-nv-tsv u-dv-ga-ne gi-lo ka-ne-gv-i hi-a nu-we-se-hv-i; So-la, So-la, ga-do-no u-yo ni-s-quv-ne-ha?

050905.english: And he said, Who art thou, Lord? And the Lord said, I am Jesus whom thou persecutest: it is hard for thee to kick against the pricks.
050905.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎦᎪ ᏂᎯ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ?  ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎠᏴ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏲ ᏥᏂᏴᏁᎭ:  ᎤᏕᏯᏙᏗᏳ ᏗᏣᎲᏖᏍᏗᏱ ᏗᎪᏍᏓᏯ.
050905.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-we-se-i; Ga-go ni-hi, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi?  U-gv-wi-yu-hi-no hi-a nu-we-se-i; A-yv Tsi-sa u-yo tsi-ni-yv-ne-ha:  u-de-ya-do-di-yu di-tsa-hv-te-s-di-yi di-go-s-da-ya.

050906.english: And he trembling and astonished said, Lord, what wilt thou have me to do? And the Lord said unto him, Arise, and go into the city, and it shall be told thee what thou must do.
050906.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏪᎾᏪ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎦᏙ ᏣᏚᎵ ᎠᏆᏛᏁᏗᏱ?  ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏔᎴᎲᎦ, ᏗᎦᏚᎲ ᎮᎾ, ᏓᏰᏣᏃᏁᎵᏃ ᎢᏣᏛᏁᏗᏱ.
050906.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-we-na-we a-le u-s-qua-ni-go-se hi-a nu-we-se-i; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, ga-do tsa-du-li a-qua-dv-ne-di-yi?  U-gv-wi-yu-hi-no hi-a nu-we-se-le-i; Ta-le-hv-ga, di-ga-du-hv he-na, da-ye-tsa-no-ne-li-no i-tsa-dv-ne-di-yi.

050907.english: And the men which journeyed with him stood speechless, hearing a voice, but seeing no man.
050907.cherokee: ᎠᏁᎯᏃ ᎠᏂᏫᏅ ᏧᏅᎨᏫ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏓᏂᏙᎨᎢ, ᎠᎾᏛᎩᏍᎨ ᎧᏁᎬᎢ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᏯᏂᎪᏩᏘᏍᎨᎢ.
050907.phonetic: A-ne-hi-no a-ni-wi-nv tsu-nv-ge-wi na-s-gi-ya da-ni-do-ge-i, a-na-dv-gi-s-ge ka-ne-gv-i, a-se-no v-tla gi-lo ya-ni-go-wa-ti-s-ge-i.

050908.english: And Saul arose from the earth; and when his eyes were opened, he saw no man: but they led him by the hand, and brought him into Damascus.
050908.cherokee: ᏐᎳᏃ ᏕᎤᎴᏅ ᎡᎳᏗ ᎦᏅᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎦᏙᎵ ᏚᎵᏍᏚᎢᏒ ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᏳᎪᎮᎢ, ᎬᏬᏱᏅᏎᏃ ᏕᎹᏍᎦ ᏫᎬᏩᏴᏔᏁᎢ.
050908.phonetic: So-la-no de-u-le-nv e-la-di ga-nv-i, a-le di-ga-do-li du-li-s-du-i-sv v-tla gi-lo yu-go-he-i, gv-wo-yi-nv-se-no De-ma-s-ga wi-gv-wa-yv-ta-ne-i.

050909.english: And he was three days without sight, and neither did eat nor drink.
050909.cherokee: ᏦᎢᏃ ᏧᏒᎯᏛ ᎾᎪᏩᏗᏍᎬᎾ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎬᎾ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏗᏔᏍᎬᎾ ᎨᏎᎢ.
050909.phonetic: Tso-i-no tsu-sv-hi-dv na-go-wa-di-s-gv-na ge-se-i, a-le na-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-gv-na a-le na-di-ta-s-gv-na ge-se-i.

050910.english: And there was a certain disciple at Damascus, named Ananias; and to him said the Lord in a vision, Ananias. And he said, Behold, I am here, Lord.
050910.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏧᏁᎶᏗ ᎡᎮ ᏕᎹᏍᎦ, ᎡᏂᎾᏯ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏫᏎᎲ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎡᏂᎾᏯ.  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎠᏂ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ.
050910.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-s-di tsu-ne-lo-di e-he De-ma-s-ga, E-ni-na-ya tsu-do-i-dv; na-s-gi-no u-ne-la-wi-se-hv U-gv-wi-yu-hi hi-a nu-we-se-le-i; E-ni-na-ya.  Hi-a-no nu-we-se-i; A-ni, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi.

050911.english: And the Lord said unto him, Arise, and go into the street which is called Straight, and inquire in the house of Judas for one called Saul, of Tarsus: for, behold, he prayeth,
050911.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ; ᏔᎴᎲᎦ, ᎮᎾ ᏗᎦᎳᏅᏛ ᎦᏥᏃᏍᏛ ᏥᏚᏙᎥ, ᏧᏓᏏᏃ ᎦᏁᎸ ᏫᏲᎦ ᏐᎳ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᏓᏌ ᎡᎯ.  ᎬᏂᏳᏰᏃ ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗᎭ,
050911.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-we-se-le U-gv-wi-yu-hi; Ta-le-hv-ga, he-na di-ga-la-nv-dv Ga-tsi-no-s-dv tsi-du-do-v, Tsu-da-si-no ga-ne-lv wi-yo-ga So-la tsu-do-i-dv Da-sa e-hi.  Gv-ni-yu-ye-no a-da-do-li-s-di-ha,

050912.english: And hath seen in a vision a man named Ananias coming in, and putting his hand on him, that he might receive his sight.
050912.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏫᏎᎲ ᎤᎪᎲ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎡᏂᎾᏯ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᎤᏴᎵᎸ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏏᏔᏛ ᎥᎤᎪᏩᏛᏗᏱ.
050912.phonetic: A-le u-ne-la-wi-se-hv u-go-hv a-s-ga-ya E-ni-na-ya tsu-do-i-dv u-yv-li-lv a-le u-si-ta-dv v-u-go-wa-dv-di-yi.

050913.english: Then Ananias answered, Lord, I have heard by many of this man, how much evil he hath done to thy saints at Jerusalem:
050913.cherokee: ᎡᏂᎾᏯᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᏥᏯᏛᎦᏁᎸ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏂᏣᏔ ᎬᎩᏃᏁᎸ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᏲ ᏂᏚᏩᏁᎸ ᏗᏣᏤᎵ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ.
050913.phonetic: E-ni-na-ya-no hi-a nu-we-se-i; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, tsi-ya-dv-ga-ne-lv hi-a a-s-ga-ya u-ni-tsa-ta gv-gi-no-ne-lv u-tsa-ti u-yo ni-du-wa-ne-lv di-tsa-tse-li u-na-da-nv-ti Tsi-lu-si-li-mi.

050914.english: And here he hath authority from the chief priests to bind all that call on thy name.
050914.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏃ ᎤᎭ ᎬᏩᏁᏤᎸᎯ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᏧᎸᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏕᏣᏙᎥ ᎠᏂᏁᎢᏍᏗᏍᎩ.
050914.phonetic: A-ni-no u-ha gv-wa-ne-tse-lv-hi nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--a-ne-lo-hi tsu-lv-s-di-yi ni-ga-dv de-tsa-do-v a-ni-ne-i-s-di-s-gi.

050915.english: But the Lord said unto him, Go thy way: for he is a chosen vessel unto me, to bear my name before the Gentiles, and kings, and the children of Israel:
050915.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎮᎾ, ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᏥᏯᏑᏰᏛ ᎠᏖᎵᏙ ᎠᏆᏤᎵᎦ ᏓᏆᏙᎥ ᎠᏱᏙᎯ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏏᎵ ᏧᏪᏥ.
050915.phonetic: A-se-no U-gv-wi-yu-hi hi-a nu-we-se-le-i; He-na, na-s-gi-ye-no tsi-ya-su-ye-dv a-te-li-do a-qua-tse-li-ga da-qua-do-v a-yi-do-hi a-ni-ga-ta-hv tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi a-le u-ni-gv-wi-yu-hi a-le I-si-li tsu-we-tsi.

050916.english: For I will shew him how great things he must suffer for my name's sake.
050916.cherokee: ᎬᏂᎨᏒᏰᏃ ᏅᏓᏥᏴᏁᎵ ᏄᏣᏛ ᎠᏎ ᎤᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏓᏆᏙᎥ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎢ.
050916.phonetic: Gv-ni-ge-sv-ye-no nv-da-tsi-yv-ne-li nu-tsa-dv a-se u-gi-li-yo-i-s-di-yi da-qua-do-v nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-i.

050917.english: And Ananias went his way, and entered into the house; and putting his hands on him said, Brother Saul, the Lord, even Jesus, that appeared unto thee in the way as thou camest, hath sent me, that thou mightest receive thy sight, and be filled with the Holy Ghost.
050917.cherokee: ᎡᏂᎾᏯᏃ ᎤᏪᏅᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᎤᏴᎴᎢ, ᏚᏏᏔᏛᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏐᎳ ᎥᎩᏅᏟ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏥᏂᏣᏛᏁᎴ ᏔᎢᏒᎢ ᏗᎩᏅᏒ ᎥᏣᎪᏩᏛᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᏣᎧᎵᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ.
050917.phonetic: E-ni-na-ya-no u-we-nv-se-i, a-le a-da-ne-lv u-yv-le-i, du-si-ta-dv-no hi-a nu-we-se-i; So-la v-gi-nv-tli, U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa, na-s-gi gv-ni-ge-sv tsi-ni-tsa-dv-ne-le ta-i-sv-i di-gi-nv-sv v-tsa-go-wa-dv-di-yi a-le tsa-ka-li-i-s-di-yi Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do.

050918.english: And immediately there fell from his eyes as it had been scales: and he received sight forthwith, and arose, and was baptized.
050918.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏚᎦᏔᏅᎪᎠᏎ ᎤᏩᏥᏍᎦᎸ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎬᏩᎪᏩᏛᏗ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ; ᏚᎴᏅᏃ ᎠᎦᏬᎡᎢ.
050918.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv du-ga-ta-nv-go-a-se u-wa-tsi-s-ga-lv i-yu-s-di, a-le gi-la-quo i-yv-dv gv-wa-go-wa-dv-di nu-li-s-ta-ne-i; du-le-nv-no a-ga-wo-e-i.

050919.english: And when he had received meat, he was strengthened. Then was Saul certain days with the disciples which were at Damascus.
050919.cherokee: ᎤᎵᏍᏓᏴᏅᏃ ᎤᎵᏂᎪᏎᎢ.  ᏐᎳᏃ ᎢᎸᏍᎩ ᏧᏒᎯᏛ ᏚᏪᎳᏗᏙᎴ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᏕᎹᏍᎦ ᎠᏁᎯ.
050919.phonetic: U-li-s-da-yv-nv-no u-li-ni-go-se-i.  So-la-no i-lv-s-gi tsu-sv-hi-dv du-we-la-di-do-le a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi De-ma-s-ga a-ne-hi.

050920.english: And straightway he preached Christ in the synagogues, that he is the Son of God.
050920.cherokee: ᎢᎬᏪᏅᏛᏃ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏕᎪᏢᏩᏗᏒ ᎤᎵᏥᏙᏁᎢ, ᎤᏃᎮᎴ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎨᏒᎢ.
050920.phonetic: I-gv-we-nv-dv-no di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi de-go-tlv-wa-di-sv u-li-tsi-do-ne-i, u-no-he-le Ga-lo-ne-dv na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-we-tsi ge-sv-i.

050921.english: But all that heard him were amazed, and said; Is not this he that destroyed them which called on this name in Jerusalem, and came hither for that intent, that he might bring them bound unto the chief priests?
050921.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅᎯ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᏝᏍᎪ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏱᎩ ᏥᏓᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎯᎠ ᏕᏅᏙᎥ ᎠᏂᏁᎢᏍᏗᏍᎩ?  ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏂ ᎤᎷᏨᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᎸᏍᏗ ᏫᏚᏘᏃᎮᏗᏱ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ.
050921.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv-no u-na-dv-ga-nv-hi u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se-i, a-le hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; tla-s-go na-s-gi hi-a yi-gi tsi-da-gi-li-yo-i-s-do-di-s-gv Tsi-lu-si-li-mi hi-a de-nv-do-v a-ni-ne-i-s-di-s-gi?  A-le na-s-gi i-yu-s-di a-ni u-lu-tsv-gi, na-s-gi tsu-lv-s-di wi-du-ti-no-he-di-yi nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--a-ne-lo-hi.

050922.english: But Saul increased the more in strength, and confounded the Jews which dwelt at Damascus, proving that this is very Christ.
050922.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏐᎳ ᎧᏁᏉᎨᏉ ᎤᎵᏂᎪᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᏚᎪᎾᏛᏔᏁ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏕᎹᏍᎦ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᎬᏁᎮ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ.
050922.phonetic: A-se-no So-la ka-ne-quo-ge-quo u-li-ni-go-s-gv-i, a-le a-ni-wo-ni-s-gv du-go-na-dv-ta-ne A-ni-tsu-si De-ma-s-ga a-ne-hi, gv-ni-ge-sv ni-gv-ne-he na-s-gi hi-a Ga-lo-ne-dv ge-sv-i.

050923.english: And after that many days were fulfilled, the Jews took counsel to kill him:

050924.english: But their laying await was known of Saul. And they watched the gates day and night to kill him.
050924.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᏄᎪᏔᏅᎢ ᎤᏛᎦᏁ ᏐᎳ.  ᏂᎪᎯᎸᏃ ᎢᎦ ᎠᎴ ᏒᏃᏱ ᏗᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏚᏂᎭᎷᏁ ᎤᏂᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
050924.phonetic: A-se-no na-s-gi du-nu-go-ta-nv-i u-dv-ga-ne So-la.  Ni-go-hi-lv-no i-ga a-le sv-no-yi di-ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi du-ni-ha-lu-ne u-ni-hi-s-di-yi.

050925.english: Then the disciples took him by night, and let him down by the wall in a basket.
050925.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᏂᏯᏅᎮ ᏒᏃᏱ ᎠᎴ ᏔᎷᏣ ᎤᏂᏲᏔᏁ ᎤᎾᏠᎥᏔᏁ ᎠᏐᏴᎢ.
050925.phonetic: A-se-no tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-ni-ya-nv-he sv-no-yi a-le ta-lu-tsa u-ni-yo-ta-ne u-na-tlo-v-ta-ne a-so-yv-i.

050926.english: And when Saul was come to Jerusalem, he assayed to join himself to the disciples: but they were all afraid of him, and believed not that he was a disciple.
050926.cherokee: ᏐᎳᏃ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎤᎷᏨ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᏧᎵᎪᏁᏗᏱ ᎤᏰᎸᏁᎢ.  ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᎬᏩᏍᎦᎢᎮᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏯᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎨ ᏧᏁᎶᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
050926.phonetic: So-la-no Tsi-lu-si-li-mi u-lu-tsv tsu-na-ne-lo-di tsu-li-go-ne-di-yi u-ye-lv-ne-i.  Ni-ga-dv-no gv-wa-s-ga-i-he-i, a-le v-tla ya-no-hi-yu-hv-s-ge tsu-ne-lo-di ge-sv-i.

050927.english: But Barnabas took him, and brought him to the apostles, and declared unto them how he had seen the Lord in the way, and that he had spoken to him, and how he had preached boldly at Damascus in the name of Jesus.
050927.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏆᏂᏆ ᏭᏯᏅᎲ ᏚᏘᏃᎮᎴ ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏃᏁᎴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᎪᎲᎢ ᎠᎢᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏬᏁᏔᏅᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎦᎢᎲᎾ ᏥᏌ ᏕᎤᏙᎥ ᎤᏁᎢᏍᏔᏅᎢ ᏕᎹᏍᎦ.
050927.phonetic: A-se-no Qua-ni-qua wu-ya-nv-hv du-ti-no-he-le ge-tsi-nv-si-dv, a-le du-no-ne-le U-gv-wi-yu-hi u-go-hv-i a-i-sv-i, a-le na-s-gi u-wo-ne-ta-nv-i, a-le na-s-ga-i-hv-na Tsi-sa de-u-do-v u-ne-i-s-ta-nv-i De-ma-s-ga.

050928.english: And he was with them coming in and going out at Jerusalem.

050929.english: And he spake boldly in the name of the Lord Jesus, and disputed against the Grecians: but they went about to slay him.
050929.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎦᎢᎲᎾᏃ ᎧᏁᎢᏍᏗᏍᎨ ᏕᎤᏙᎥ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᎪᎢ ᎤᏂᏬᏂᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏬᏂᏍᎩ ᎠᎾᏗᏒᎯᎮ ᎠᏂᏬᏂᏍᎬᎢ.  ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᎾᏁᎶᏔᏁ ᎬᏩᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
050929.phonetic: Na-s-ga-i-hv-na-no ka-ne-i-s-di-s-ge de-u-do-v U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa, a-le A-ni-go-i u-ni-wo-ni-s-di a-ni-wo-ni-s-gi a-na-di-sv-hi-he a-ni-wo-ni-s-gv-i.  A-se-no u-na-ne-lo-ta-ne gv-wa-hi-s-di-yi.

050930.english: Which when the brethren knew, they brought him down to Caesarea, and sent him forth to Tarsus.
050930.cherokee: ᎠᎾᎵᏅᏟᏍᎩᏂ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ, ᏏᏌᎵᏱ ᏭᎾᏘᏃᎴᎢ, ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏓᏌ ᏭᏂᏅᏎᎢ.
050930.phonetic: A-na-li-nv-tli-s-gi-ni na-s-gi u-na-dv-ga-nv, Si-sa-li-yi wu-na-ti-no-le-i, hna-quo-no Da-sa wu-ni-nv-se-i.

050931.english: Then had the churches rest throughout all Judaea and Galilee and Samaria, and were edified; and walking in the fear of the Lord, and in the comfort of the Holy Ghost, were multiplied.
050931.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏡ Ꭼ ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᏄᎾᎵᏍᏓᏁᎴ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᏧᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎨᎵᎵ ᎠᎴ ᏌᎺᎵᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎨᎦᎵᏂᎪᎯᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᎢᏎ ᎾᎿᎭᎦᎾᏰᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᏂᎦᎵᏍᏓᏗᏍᎬ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ, ᎠᏂᏁᏉᎨᎢ.
050931.phonetic: Hna-quo-no tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-tlu gv nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv nu-na-li-s-da-ne-le ni-gv-na-dv Tsu-di-yi a-le Ge-li-li a-le Sa-me-li-yi, a-le ge-ga-li-ni-go-hi-s-ta-ne-i, a-le a-na-i-se na-hna ga-na-ye-hi-s-di ge-sv U-gv-wi-yu-hi, a-le o-s-dv u-ni-ga-li-s-da-di-s-gv Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do, a-ni-ne-quo-ge-i.

050932.english: And it came to pass, as Peter passed throughout all quarters, he came down also to the saints which dwelt at Lydda.
050932.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᏈᏓ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᎤᏪᏙᎸ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏫᏚᎷᏤᎴ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘ ᎵᏓ ᎠᏁᎯ.
050932.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-ne-i, Qui-da ni-gv-na-dv u-we-do-lv na-s-quo wi-du-lu-tse-le u-na-da-nv-ti Li-da a-ne-hi.

050933.english: And there he found a certain man named Aeneas, which had kept his bed eight years, and was sick of the palsy.
050933.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎤᏩᏛᎮ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎢᏂᏯ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᏧᏁᎳ ᎢᏧᏕᏘᏴᏛ ᎬᏩᏂᏏᏅᎯ ᎨᏎ ᎦᏂᏟ ᎤᎸᏓᎸᎥᏍᎨᎢ.
050933.phonetic: Na-hna-no u-wa-dv-he gi-lo i-yu-s-di a-s-ga-ya I-ni-ya tsu-do-i-dv, tsu-ne-la i-tsu-de-ti-yv-dv gv-wa-ni-si-nv-hi ge-se ga-ni-tli u-lv-da-lv-v-s-ge-i.

050934.english: And Peter said unto him, Aeneas, Jesus Christ maketh thee whole: arise, and make thy bed. And he arose immediately.
050934.cherokee: ᏈᏓᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ; ᎢᏂᏯ, ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᏣᏅᏮᎦ; ᏔᎴᎲᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᏰᏍᏛᎢᏌ ᏣᏂᏏᏗᏱ.  ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏚᎴᏁᎢ.
050934.phonetic: Qui-da-no hi-a nu-we-se-le na-s-gi; I-ni-ya, Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv tsa-nv-wv-ga; ta-le-hv-ga, a-le hi-ye-s-dv-i-sa tsa-ni-si-di-yi.  Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv du-le-ne-i.

050935.english: And all that dwelt at Lydda and Saron saw him, and turned to the Lord.
050935.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᎵᏓ ᎠᎴ ᏎᎶᏂ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎬᏩᎪᎮᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏭᎾᎦᏔᎲᏍᏔᏁᎢ.
050935.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv-no Li-da a-le Se-lo-ni a-ne-hi gv-wa-go-he-i, a-le U-gv-wi-yu-hi i-di-tlv wu-na-ga-ta-hv-s-ta-ne-i.

050936.english: Now there was at Joppa a certain disciple named Tabitha, which by interpretation is called Dorcas: this woman was full of good works and almsdeeds which she did.
050936.cherokee: ᏦᏈᏃ ᎡᎮ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᎨᏴ ᏧᏁᎶᏗ, ᏓᏈᏗ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏁᏢᏔᏅᎯ ᏓᎩ ᎦᏛᎦ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎤᎧᎵᏨᎯ ᎨᏎ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᏁᎸᎥᏍᎬᎢ.
050936.phonetic: Tso-qui-no e-he gi-lo i-yu-s-di a-ge-yv tsu-ne-lo-di, Da-qui-di tsu-do-i-dv, na-s-gi a-ne-tlv-ta-nv-hi Da-gi ga-dv-ga, na-s-gi hi-a u-ka-li-tsv-hi ge-se o-s-dv du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv-i a-le a-da-ne-lv-v-s-gv-i.

050937.english: And it came to pass in those days, that she was sick, and died: whom when they had washed, they laid her in an upper chamber.
050937.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁ ᎾᎯᏳᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏢᏨ ᎤᏲᎱᏎᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᎾᏬᎥ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎧᏅᏑᎸ ᎤᏂᏅᏁᎢ.
050937.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-ne na-hi-yu-i, na-s-gi u-tlv-tsv u-yo-hu-se-i, na-s-gi-no u-na-wo-v ga-lv-la-di ka-nv-su-lv u-ni-nv-ne-i.

050938.english: And forasmuch as Lydda was nigh to Joppa, and the disciples had heard that Peter was there, they sent unto him two men, desiring him that he would not delay to come to them.
050938.cherokee: ᎵᏓᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏦᏈ ᎾᎥ ᏂᏚᏓᎴᎢ, ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ ᏈᏓ ᎡᏙᎲ ᎵᏓ, ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᏚᏂᏅᏎ ᏭᏂᏔᏲᏎᏗᏱ ᎤᏙᎯᏗᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏂᏙᏓᏳᏩᏛᎲᏍᏗᏱ.
050938.phonetic: Li-da-no a-le Tso-qui na-v ni-du-da-le-i, a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi-no u-na-dv-ga-nv Qui-da e-do-hv Li-da, a-ni-ta-li a-ni-s-ga-ya du-ni-nv-se wu-ni-ta-yo-se-di-yi u-do-hi-di-s-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na ni-do-da-yu-wa-dv-hv-s-di-yi.

050939.english: Then Peter arose and went with them. When he was come, they brought him into the upper chamber: and all the widows stood by him weeping, and shewing the coats and garments which Dorcas made, while she was with them.
050939.cherokee: ᏈᏓᏃ ᏚᎴᏅ ᏚᏍᏓᏩᏛᏎᎢ; ᎤᎷᏨᏃ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎧᏅᏑᎸ ᏫᎬᏩᏴᏔᏁᎢ; ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ ᏧᏃᏑᎶᏨᎯ ᎦᏙᎬ ᎾᎥ ᎠᏂᏙᎾᎡ ᏓᏂᏴᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᏂᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᎨ ᎭᏫᏂ ᏗᏄᏬ ᎠᎴ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏗᏄᏬ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᎩ ᏧᏪᏰᏫᏒᎯ ᎠᏏ ᎤᏁᎳᏗᏙᎮᎢ.
050939.phonetic: Qui-da-no du-le-nv du-s-da-wa-dv-se-i; u-lu-tsv-no ga-lv-la-di ka-nv-su-lv wi-gv-wa-yv-ta-ne-i; ni-ga-dv-no a-ni-ge-yv tsu-no-su-lo-tsv-hi ga-do-gv na-v a-ni-do-na-e da-ni-yv-ge-i, a-le da-ni-na-nu-go-wi-s-ge ha-wi-ni di-nu-wo a-le tsu-da-le-nv-dv di-nu-wo na-s-gi Da-gi tsu-we-ye-wi-sv-hi a-si u-ne-la-di-do-he-i.

050940.english: But Peter put them all forth, and kneeled down, and prayed; and turning him to the body said, Tabitha, arise. And she opened her eyes: and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
050940.cherokee: ᏈᏓᏃ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏚᏄᎪᏫᏒ ᏚᎵᏂᏆᏅᏁ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᏕᏛᏅᏃ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏭᎦᏔᎲᏍᏔᏅ, ᏓᏈᏗ ᏔᎴᎲᎦ, ᎤᏛᏁᎢ.  ᏚᏍᏚᎢᏎᏃ ᏗᎦᏙᎵ, ᏈᏓᏃ ᎤᎪᎲ, ᎤᏗᏛᎮ ᎤᏪᏁᎢ.
050940.phonetic: Qui-da-no ni-ga-dv du-nu-go-wi-sv du-li-ni-qua-nv-ne a-le u-da-do-li-s-ta-ne-i, de-dv-nv-no i-di-tlv wu-ga-ta-hv-s-ta-nv, Da-qui-di ta-le-hv-ga, u-dv-ne-i.  Du-s-du-i-se-no di-ga-do-li, Qui-da-no u-go-hv, u-di-dv-he u-we-ne-i.

050941.english: And he gave her his hand, and lifted her up, and when he had called the saints and widows, presented her alive.
050941.cherokee: ᎤᏬᏯᏁᏒᏃ ᏕᎤᎴᏔᏁᎢ; ᏫᏚᏯᏅᎲᏃ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘ ᎠᎴ ᏧᏃᏑᎶᏨᎯ, ᏕᎤᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᎴ ᎬᏃᏛ.
050941.phonetic: U-wo-ya-ne-sv-no de-u-le-ta-ne-i; wi-du-ya-nv-hv-no u-na-da-nv-ti a-le tsu-no-su-lo-tsv-hi, de-u-na-nu-go-wi-se-le gv-no-dv.

050942.english: And it was known throughout all Joppa; and many believed in the Lord.
050942.cherokee: ᏦᏈᏃ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏖ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏁ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ.
050942.phonetic: Tso-qui-no ni-gv-na-dv u-na-dv-ga-ne-i, a-le u-ni-tsa-te u-no-hi-yu-ne U-gv-wi-yu-hi.

050943.english: And it came to pass, that he tarried many days in Joppa with one Simon a tanner.
050943.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᏰᎵ ᎤᏬᎯᏤ ᎤᏕᏁ ᏦᏈ, ᏗᏑᏫᏍᎩ ᏌᏩᏂ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᎦᏁᎸᎢ.
050943.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-ne-i, ye-li u-wo-hi-tse u-de-ne Tso-qui, di-su-wi-s-gi Sa-wa-ni tsu-do-i-dv ga-ne-lv-i.

051001.english: There was a certain man in Caesarea called Cornelius, a centurion of the band called the Italian band,
051001.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎡᎮ ᏏᏌᎵᏱ, ᎧᏂᎵᏯ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ, ᎨᎳ ᎢᏓᎵ ᏧᎾᏙᎢᏛ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬ.
051001.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-s-di a-s-ga-ya e-he Si-sa-li-yi, Ka-ni-li-ya tsu-do-i-dv, a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui di-ti-ni-do-hi, ge-la I-da-li tsu-na-do-i-dv u-na-da-tlu-gv.

051002.english: A devout man, and one that feared God with all his house, which gave much alms to the people, and prayed to God always.
051002.cherokee: ᎤᏓᏅᏘ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎦᎾᏰᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎦᏁᎸ ᎠᏂᎮ, ᎤᏣᏘ ᏗᏁᎸᎥᏍᎩ ᏴᏫ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎯ.
051002.phonetic: U-da-nv-ti a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi ga-na-ye-s-gi, a-le na-s-quo ni-ga-dv ga-ne-lv a-ni-he, u-tsa-ti di-ne-lv-v-s-gi yv-wi, a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi ni-go-hi-lv a-da-do-li-s-da-ne-hi.

051003.english: He saw in a vision evidently about the ninth hour of the day an angel of God coming in to him, and saying unto him, Cornelius.
051003.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏦᎢᎭ ᎢᏳᏟᎶᏛ ᎧᎳᏩᏗᏒ ᎬᏂᎨᏒᎢᏳ ᎤᎪᎮ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎤᎷᏤᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎧᏂᎵᏯ.
051003.phonetic: Na-s-gi tso-i-ha i-yu-tli-lo-dv ka-la-wa-di-sv gv-ni-ge-sv-i-yu u-go-he di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li u-lu-tse-le-i, a-le hi-a nu-we-se-le-i; Ka-ni-li-ya.

051004.english: And when he looked on him, he was afraid, and said, What is it, Lord? And he said unto him, Thy prayers and thine alms are come up for a memorial before God.
051004.cherokee: ᏚᎧᎿᎭᏅᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᎦᎸ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎦᏙᎢ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ.  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎭᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᎴ ᎭᏓᏁᎸᎥᏍᎬ ᏚᎵᏌᎳᏓᏅ ᎠᏅᏓᏗᏍᏙᏗ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎦᏔᎲᎢ.
051004.phonetic: Du-ka-hna-nv-no a-le u-s-ga-lv hi-a nu-we-se-i; Ga-do-i, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi.  Hi-a-no nu-we-se-le-i; Ha-da-do-li-s-di-s-gv a-le ha-da-ne-lv-v-s-gv du-li-sa-la-da-nv a-nv-da-di-s-do-di i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi U-ne-la-nv-hi a-ga-ta-hv-i.

051005.english: And now send men to Joppa, and call for one Simon, whose surname is Peter:
051005.cherokee: Ꭷ, ᏦᏈ ᏘᏅᎵ ᎠᏂᏫᏅ, ᏫᏯᏅ ᏌᏩᏂ ᏈᏓ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ,
051005.phonetic: Ka, Tso-qui ti-nv-li a-ni-wi-nv, wi-ya-nv Sa-wa-ni Qui-da tsu-do-i-dv,

051006.english: He lodgeth with one Simon a tanner, whose house is by the sea side: he shall tell thee what thou oughtest to do.
051006.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏬᎳ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎦᏁᎸ ᏌᏩᏂ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᏗᏑᏫᏍᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎹᏳᎶᏗ ᏥᎦᏁᎸ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᏣᏃᏁᎵ ᎢᏣᏛᏁᏗᏱ.
051006.phonetic: Na-s-gi u-wo-la gi-lo i-yu-s-di ga-ne-lv Sa-wa-ni tsu-do-i-dv, di-su-wi-s-gi, na-s-gi a-ma-yu-lo-di tsi-ga-ne-lv, na-s-gi da-tsa-no-ne-li i-tsa-dv-ne-di-yi.

051007.english: And when the angel which spake unto Cornelius was departed, he called two of his household servants, and a devout soldier of them that waited on him continually;
051007.cherokee: ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎤᏓᏅᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎧᏂᎵᏯ ᎤᏬᏁᏔᏅᎯ, ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᏫᏚᏯᏅᎮ ᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᏧᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏌᏉ ᎠᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎯ ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏗᏍᎩ ᎨᏒ ᎨᎳ.
051007.phonetic: Di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi-no u-da-nv-sv na-s-gi Ka-ni-li-ya u-wo-ne-ta-nv-hi, a-ni-ta-li wi-du-ya-nv-he a-da-ne-lv tsu-nv-si-da-s-di a-le sa-quo a-ya-wi-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi a-da-do-li-s-da-ne-hi ni-go-hi-lv gv-wa-ga-ti-di-s-gi ge-sv ge-la.

051008.english: And when he had declared all these things unto them, he sent them to Joppa.

051009.english: On the morrow, as they went on their journey, and drew nigh unto the city, Peter went up upon the housetop to pray about the sixth hour:
051009.cherokee: ᎤᎩᏨᏛᏃ ᎠᎾᎢᏎ ᎠᎴ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎾᎥᏂᎨ ᎤᏂᎷᎴ ᏗᎦᏚᎲᎢ, ᏈᏓ ᎤᎩᎳᏫᏎ ᎦᏌᎾᎵ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏅᏎ ᏔᎳᏚ ᎢᏳᏟᎶᏛ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎧᎳᏩᏗᏒᎢ.
051009.phonetic: U-gi-tsv-dv-no a-na-i-se a-le hna-quo na-v-ni-ge u-ni-lu-le di-ga-du-hv-i, Qui-da u-gi-la-wi-se ga-sa-na-li u-da-do-li-s-ta-nv-se ta-la-du i-yu-tli-lo-dv i-yv-dv ka-la-wa-di-sv-i.

051010.english: And he became very hungry, and would have eaten: but while they made ready, he fell into a trance,
051010.cherokee: ᎤᏣᏘᏃ ᎤᏲᏏᏌᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎨ ᎤᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏏᏉ ᎠᎾᏛᏅᎢᏍᏗᏍᎨ ᏣᎦᎵᎰ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏄᎵᏍᏓᏁᎴᎢ.
051010.phonetic: U-tsa-ti-no u-yo-si-sa-ne-i, a-le u-du-li-s-ge u-li-s-da-yv-di-yi, a-se-no a-si-quo a-na-dv-nv-i-s-di-s-ge tsa-ga-li-ho na-s-gi-ya nu-li-s-da-ne-le-i.

051011.english: And saw heaven opened, and a certain vessel descending unto him, as it had been a great sheet knit at the four corners, and let down to the earth:
051011.cherokee: ᎤᎪᎮᏃ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎤᎵᏍᏚᎢᏛ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏖᎵᏙ ᎡᎳᏗ ᏅᏧᏍᏗᏗᏎ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎡᏆ ᎠᏄᏬ, ᏅᎩ ᏚᏅᏏᏴ ᏥᏕᎦᎸᎣᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎡᎳᏗ ᎢᎬᏁᎸᎯ ᏥᎨᏐ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎢᏴᏛ.
051011.phonetic: U-go-he-no ga-lv-la-di u-li-s-du-i-dv ge-se-i, a-le go-hu-s-di i-yu-s-di a-te-li-do e-la-di nv-tsu-s-di-di-se, na-s-gi-ya e-qua a-nu-wo, nv-gi du-nv-si-yv tsi-de-ga-lv-o-i, na-s-gi-no e-la-di i-gv-ne-lv-hi tsi-ge-so e-lo-hi i-yv-dv.

051012.english: Wherein were all manner of fourfooted beasts of the earth, and wild beasts, and creeping things, and fowls of the air.
051012.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎠᏂᏯᎡ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏅᎩ ᏗᏂᏅᏌᏗ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᎾᎨ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᏓᎾᏏᏂᏙᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᎸᎶ ᎠᏂᏃᎯᎵᏙᎯ.
051012.phonetic: Na-hna-no a-ni-ya-e ni-ga-dv tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv nv-gi di-ni-nv-sa-di e-lo-hi a-ne-hi, a-le i-na-ge a-ne-hi, a-le a-na-da-na-si-ni-do-hi, a-le ga-lv-lo a-ni-no-hi-li-do-hi.

051013.english: And there came a voice to him, Rise, Peter; kill, and eat.
051013.cherokee: ᎠᏥᏁᏤᎴᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏅᏗᎠᏥᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏈᏓ, ᏔᎴᎲᎦ, ᎯᎷᎦ ᎠᎴ ᎭᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᎦ, ᎠᎪᏎᎴᎢ.
051013.phonetic: A-tsi-ne-tse-le-no hi-a nv-di-a-tsi-we-se-le-i; Qui-da, ta-le-hv-ga, hi-lu-ga a-le ha-li-s-da-yv-hv-ga, a-go-se-le-i.

051014.english: But Peter said, Not so, Lord; for I have never eaten any thing that is common or unclean.
051014.cherokee: ᏈᏓᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏞᏍᏗ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᏞᏰᏃ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᏯᎩᎪ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏅᎦᎸᎲᎾ.
051014.phonetic: Qui-da-no hi-a nu-we-se-i; Tle-s-di, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, tle-ye-no i-lv-hi-yu ya-gi-go go-hu-s-di ga-da-ha a-le ni-ga-nv-ga-lv-hv-na.

051015.english: And the voice spake unto him again the second time, What God hath cleansed, that call not thou common.
051015.cherokee: ᏔᎵᏁᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏅᏗᎠᏥᏪᏎᎵᎢ; ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏅᎦᎸᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏞᏍᏗ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏲᏎᎮᏍᏗ.
051015.phonetic: Ta-li-ne-no hi-a nv-di-a-tsi-we-se-li-i; U-ne-la-nv-hi u-nv-ga-lv-dv na-s-gi tle-s-di ga-da-ha yo-se-he-s-di.

051016.english: This was done thrice: and the vessel was received up again into heaven.
051016.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏦᎢ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏖᎵᏙ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏫᎤᎵᏌᎳᏓᏁᎢ.
051016.phonetic: Na-s-gi hi-a tso-i nu-li-s-ta-ne-i, hna-quo-no a-te-li-do ta-li-ne ga-lv-la-di wi-u-li-sa-la-da-ne-i.

051017.english: Now while Peter doubted in himself what this vision which he had seen should mean, behold, the men which were sent from Cornelius had made inquiry for Simon's house, and stood before the gate,
051017.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏉᏃ ᏈᏓ ᎠᏓᏅᏖᏍᎨ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎦᏛᎬ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏫᏎᎸᎢ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎠᏂᏫᏅ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎧᏂᎵᏯ ᏂᏙᏓᏳᏅᏒᎯ ᎤᎾᏓᏛᏛᏅᎯ ᎨᏎ ᏌᏩᏂ ᎦᏁᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏙᎾᎡ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏳᎶᏗ;
051017.phonetic: A-si-quo-no Qui-da a-da-nv-te-s-ge nu-s-dv ga-dv-gv na-s-gi u-ne-la-wi-se-lv-i, gv-ni-yu-quo a-ni-wi-nv na-s-gi Ka-ni-li-ya ni-do-da-yu-nv-sv-hi u-na-da-dv-dv-nv-hi ge-se Sa-wa-ni ga-ne-lv-i, a-le a-ni-do-na-e ga-lo-hi-s-di-yu-lo-di;

051018.english: And called, and asked whether Simon, which was surnamed Peter, were lodged there.
051018.cherokee: ᏭᎾᎵᏍᏔᏅᏃ ᎤᎾᏓᏛᏛᏁᎢ; ᎠᏂᏍᎪ ᎤᏬᎳ ᏌᏩᏂ ᏈᏓ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎤᎾᏛᏁᎢ.
051018.phonetic: Wu-na-li-s-ta-nv-no u-na-da-dv-dv-ne-i; A-ni-s-go u-wo-la Sa-wa-ni Qui-da tsu-do-i-dv, u-na-dv-ne-i.

051019.english: While Peter thought on the vision, the Spirit said unto him, Behold, three men seek thee.
051019.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏉᏃ ᏈᏓ ᎠᏓᏅᏖᏍᎨ ᎤᏁᎳᏫᏎᎸᎢ, ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎠᏂᏦᎢ ᎠᏂᏫᏅ ᎨᏣᏲᎭ.
051019.phonetic: A-si-quo-no Qui-da a-da-nv-te-s-ge u-ne-la-wi-se-lv-i, A-da-nv-do hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, gv-ni-yu-quo a-ni-tso-i a-ni-wi-nv ge-tsa-yo-ha.

051020.english: Arise therefore, and get thee down, and go with them, doubting nothing: for I have sent them.
051020.cherokee: Ꭷ, ᏔᎴᎲᎦ, ᎭᏠᎠᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏘᏍᏓᏩᏚᎦ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏂᏗᏣᏢᏫᏎᎲᎾ, ᎠᏴᏰᏃ ᏗᏥᏅᏏᏛ.
051020.phonetic: Ka, ta-le-hv-ga, ha-tlo-a-hi, a-le ti-s-da-wa-du-ga go-hu-s-di ni-di-tsa-tlv-wi-se-hv-na, a-yv-ye-no di-tsi-nv-si-dv.

051021.english: Then Peter went down to the men which were sent unto him from Cornelius; and said, Behold, I am he whom ye seek: what is the cause wherefore ye are come?
051021.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏈᏓ ᎤᏠᎠᏒ ᏫᏚᎷᏤᎸ ᎠᏂᏫᏅ ᎧᏂᎵᏯ ᏧᏅᏏᏛ ᎬᏩᏯᏅᎯᎸᎯ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏤᏥᏲᎭ.  ᎦᏙ ᏧᎵᏰᎢᎸᏍᏔᏅ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏥᎷᎩ?
051021.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Qui-da u-tlo-a-sv wi-du-lu-tse-lv a-ni-wi-nv Ka-ni-li-ya tsu-nv-si-dv gv-wa-ya-nv-hi-lv-hi, hi-a nu-we-se-i; Gv-ni-yu-quo a-yv na-s-gi tse-tsi-yo-ha.  Ga-do tsu-li-ye-i-lv-s-ta-nv hi-a tsi-tsi-lu-gi?

051022.english: And they said, Cornelius the centurion, a just man, and one that feareth God, and of good report among all the nation of the Jews, was warned from God by an holy angel to send for thee into his house, and to hear words of thee.
051022.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᎧᏂᎵᏯ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ, ᏚᏳᎯᏛ ᎢᏯᏛᏁᎯ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎦᎾᏰᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎬᏩᏃᎮᏍᎩ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏑᎾᏓᎴᎩ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ, ᎤᏁᏤᎸᎩ ᎦᎸᏉᏗ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎤᏓᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᏧᏪᏅᏒ ᏫᏣᎷᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎯᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᎤᏛᎪᏗᏱ.
051022.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-ni-we-se-i; Ka-ni-li-ya a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui di-ti-ni-do-hi, du-yu-hi-dv i-ya-dv-ne-hi a-s-ga-ya a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi ga-na-ye-s-gi, a-le o-s-dv gv-wa-no-he-s-gi na-ni-v su-na-da-le-gi ge-sv A-ni-tsu-si, u-ne-tse-lv-gi ga-lv-quo-di di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi, u-da-nv-s-di-yi tsu-we-nv-sv wi-tsa-lu-hi-s-di-yi a-le hi-wo-ni-s-gv u-dv-go-di-yi.

051023.english: Then called he them in, and lodged them. And on the morrow Peter went away with them, and certain brethren from Joppa accompanied him.
051023.cherokee: ᏚᏔᏲᏎᎸᏃ ᎤᏂᏴᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏍᏆᏂᎪᏔᏁᎢ.  ᎤᎩᏨᏛᏃ ᏈᏓ ᏕᎤᏍᏓᏩᏛᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᎾᎵᏅᏟ ᏦᏈ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎬᏩᎵᎪᏁᎴᎢ.
051023.phonetic: Du-ta-yo-se-lv-no u-ni-yv-s-di-yi, a-le du-s-qua-ni-go-ta-ne-i.  U-gi-tsv-dv-no Qui-da de-u-s-da-wa-dv-se-i, a-le gi-lo i-yu-na-s-di a-na-li-nv-tli tso-qui a-ne-hi gv-wa-li-go-ne-le-i.

051024.english: And the morrow after they entered into Caesarea. And Cornelius waited for them, and had called together his kinsmen and near friends.
051024.cherokee: ᏫᎤᎩᏨᏅᏃ ᏏᏌᎵᏱ ᏭᏂᏴᎴᎢ.  ᎧᏂᎵᏯᏃ ᎤᏂᎦᏘᏰᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏪᏟᏌᏅᎯ ᎨᏎ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏧᏩᏂ ᎠᎴ ᏧᎵᎢ.
051024.phonetic: Wi-u-gi-tsv-nv-no Si-sa-li-yi wu-ni-yv-le-i.  Ka-ni-li-ya-no u-ni-ga-ti-ye-i, a-le tsu-we-tli-sa-nv-hi ge-se go-hu-s-di tsu-wa-ni a-le tsu-li-i.

051025.english: And as Peter was coming in, Cornelius met him, and fell down at his feet, and worshipped him.
051025.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏈᏓ ᏗᏴᎵᏎ ᎧᏂᎵᏯ ᏕᎤᏠᏎᎢ, ᎡᎳᏗᏃ ᎤᏅᏨ ᏈᏓ ᏚᎳᏍᎬ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎴᎢ.
051025.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Qui-da di-yv-li-se Ka-ni-li-ya de-u-tlo-se-i, e-la-di-no u-nv-tsv Qui-da du-la-s-gv u-da-do-li-s-da-ne-le-i.

051026.english: But Peter took him up, saying, Stand up; I myself also am a man.
051026.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏈᏓ ᏕᎤᎴᏔᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏔᎴᎲᎦ, ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏴᏫᏉ.
051026.phonetic: A-se-no Qui-da de-u-le-ta-ne hi-a nu-we-se-i; Ta-le-hv-ga, a-yv na-s-quo yv-wi-quo.

051027.english: And as he talked with him, he went in, and found many that were come together.
051027.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏉᏃ ᎠᎵᏃᎮᏗᏍᎨ, ᏭᏴᎴᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᏚᏩᏛᎮ ᎤᏂᏣᏖ ᏓᏂᎳᏫᎡᎢ.
051027.phonetic: A-se-quo-no a-li-no-he-di-s-ge, wu-yv-le-i; a-le du-wa-dv-he u-ni-tsa-te da-ni-la-wi-e-i.

051028.english: And he said unto them, Ye know how that it is an unlawful thing for a man that is a Jew to keep company, or come unto one of another nation; but God hath shewed me that I should not call any man common or unclean.
051028.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎢᏥᎦᏔᎭᏉ ᎤᏓᏅᏍᏙᏒᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏧᏏ ᎤᏁᏓᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏛᏗᏱ ᎩᎶ ᏅᏩᏓᎴ ᏴᏫ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎾᏋᏁᎸ ᎠᏴ ᎩᎶ ᏴᏫ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏥᏅᎦᎸᎥᎾ ᏥᏲᏎᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
051028.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i; I-tsi-ga-ta-ha-quo u-da-nv-s-do-sv-hi ge-sv gi-lo A-tsu-si u-ne-da-s-di-yi a-le u-wa-dv-di-yi gi-lo nv-wa-da-le yv-wi; a-se-no U-ne-la-nv-hi gv-ni-ge-sv na-quv-ne-lv a-yv gi-lo yv-wi ga-da-ha a-le na-tsi-nv-ga-lv-v-na tsi-yo-se-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i.

051029.english: Therefore came I unto you without gainsaying, as soon as I was sent for: I ask therefore for what intent ye have sent for me?
051029.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎪᏁᎶᏍᎬᎾ ᏛᏇᏅᏒᎩ ᏮᎩᏯᏅᎰᎸᏉ.  ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏨᏯᏛᏛᎲᎦ ᏅᏧᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅ ᏫᏍᎩᏯᏅᎲᎢ.
051029.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no i-yu-s-di go-hu-s-di na-s-go-ne-lo-s-gv-na dv-que-nv-sv-gi wv-gi-ya-nv-ho-lv-quo.  Na-s-gi-no i-yu-s-di i-tsv-ya-dv-dv-hv-ga nv-tsu-li-s-do-ta-nv wi-s-gi-ya-nv-hv-i.

051030.english: And Cornelius said, Four days ago I was fasting until this hour; and at the ninth hour I prayed in my house, and, behold, a man stood before me in bright clothing,
051030.cherokee: ᎧᏂᎵᏯᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏅᎩ ᏄᏒᎭ ᎠᎹᏟ ᎬᏍᎬᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎢᏳ ᎧᎳᏩᏗᏒ ᎢᏯᏍᏗ, ᏦᎢᏃ ᎢᏳᏟᎶᏛ ᎧᎳᏩᏗᏒ ᎦᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᏥᏁᎸᎢ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏗᎬᏩᎸᏌᏓ ᏧᏄᏫ ᎢᎬᏱᏢ ᎠᏆᎴᏁᎸᎩ.
051030.phonetic: Ka-ni-li-ya-no hi-a nu-we-se-i; Nv-gi nu-sv-ha a-ma-tli gv-s-gv-gi hi-a i-yu ka-la-wa-di-sv i-ya-s-di, tso-i-no i-yu-tli-lo-dv ka-la-wa-di-sv ga-da-do-li-s-di-s-gv tsi-ne-lv-i, gv-ni-yu-quo-no a-s-ga-ya di-gv-wa-lv-sa-da tsu-nu-wi i-gv-yi-tlv a-qua-le-ne-lv-gi.

051031.english: And said, Cornelius, thy prayer is heard, and thine alms are had in remembrance in the sight of God.
051031.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎧᏂᎵᏯ, ᎭᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᎡᏣᏛᎦᏁᎸ ᎠᎴ ᎭᏓᏁᎸᎢᏍᎬ ᎠᏅᏔ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎦᏔᎲᎢ.
051031.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-we-sv-gi; Ka-ni-li-ya, ha-da-do-li-s-di-s-gv e-tsa-dv-ga-ne-lv a-le ha-da-ne-lv-i-s-gv a-nv-ta U-ne-la-nv-hi a-ga-ta-hv-i.

051032.english: Send therefore to Joppa, and call hither Simon, whose surname is Peter; he is lodged in the house of one Simon a tanner by the sea side: who, when he cometh, shall speak unto thee.
051032.cherokee: Ꭷ, ᏦᏈ ᎭᏓᏅᎵ, ᎠᎴ ᏫᏯᏅ ᏌᏩᏂ ᏈᏓ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏬᎳ ᏌᏩᏂ ᏗᏑᏫᏍᎩ ᎦᏁᎸᎢ ᎠᎹᏳᎶᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎷᏨᎭ ᏓᏣᏬᏁᏔᏂ.
051032.phonetic: Ka, Tso-qui ha-da-nv-li, a-le wi-ya-nv Sa-wa-ni Qui-da tsu-do-i-dv; na-s-gi u-wo-la Sa-wa-ni di-su-wi-s-gi ga-ne-lv-i a-ma-yu-lo-di, na-s-gi ga-lu-tsv-ha da-tsa-wo-ne-ta-ni.

051033.english: Immediately therefore I sent to thee; and thou hast well done that thou art come. Now therefore are we all here present before God, to hear all things that are commanded thee of God.
051033.cherokee: ᎰᏩᏃ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎠᏆᏓᏅᏒᎩ ᏪᏣᏯᏅᏗᏱ; ᎣᏏᏳᏃ ᏂᏣᏛᏁᎸ ᏥᎷᎩ.  ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏂ ᎣᏤᏙᎭ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎦᏔᎲᎢ, ᎣᎦᏛᏅᎢᏍᏗ ᎣᎦᏛᎪᏗᏱ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏣᏁᏤᎸᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
051033.phonetic: Ho-wa-no gi-la-quo i-yv-dv a-qua-da-nv-sv-gi we-tsa-ya-nv-di-yi; o-si-yu-no ni-tsa-dv-ne-lv tsi-lu-gi.  Hna-quo-no ni-ga-dv a-ni o-tse-do-ha U-ne-la-nv-hi a-ga-ta-hv-i, o-ga-dv-nv-i-s-di o-ga-dv-go-di-yi ni-ga-v U-ne-la-nv-hi tsa-ne-tse-lv-hi ge-sv-i.

051034.english: Then Peter opened his mouth, and said, Of a truth I perceive that God is no respecter of persons:
051034.cherokee: ᏈᏓᏃ ᎤᏍᏚᎢᏒ ᎠᎰᎵ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎦᏙᎴᎣᏍᎦ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎩᎶ ᏧᎸᏉᏙᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ;
051034.phonetic: Qui-da-no u-s-du-i-sv a-ho-li, hi-a nu-we-se-i; U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya ga-do-le-o-s-ga U-ne-la-nv-hi gi-lo tsu-lv-quo-do-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i;

051035.english: But in every nation he that feareth him, and worketh righteousness, is accepted with him.
051035.cherokee: ᎾᏂᎥᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᎠᏁᎲᎢ ᎩᎶ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏳᎾᏰᏍᎦ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᏱᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᎦᏓᏂᎸᎪᎢ.
051035.phonetic: Na-ni-v-quo-s-gi-ni tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi a-ne-hv-i gi-lo na-s-gi yu-na-ye-s-ga a-le du-yu-go-dv yi-du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-ha na-s-gi da-ga-da-ni-lv-go-i.

051036.english: The word which God sent unto the children of Israel, preaching peace by Jesus Christ: (he is Lord of all:)
051036.cherokee: ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏧᏓᏅᏎ ᎢᏏᎵ ᏧᏪᏥ ᎤᎾᏛᎪᏗ ᏥᎧᏃᎮᏍᎨ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎢᏳᏩᏂᏌᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎾᏂᎥ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ,
051036.phonetic: Ka-no-he-dv tsu-da-nv-se I-si-li tsu-we-tsi u-na-dv-go-di tsi-ka-no-he-s-ge o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv u-gv-wa-li Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv i-yu-wa-ni-sa-dv na-s-gi U-gv-wi-yu-hi na-ni-v u-na-tse-li-ga,

051037.english: That word, I say, ye know, which was published throughout all Judaea, and began from Galilee, after the baptism which John preached;
051037.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎢᏥᎦᏔᎭ, ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏥᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᏧᏗᏱ, ᎨᎵᎵ ᏨᏓᏳᏓᎴᏅᎲ ᏗᏓᏬᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ ᏄᎵᏥᏙᏃ ᏣᏂ^
051037.phonetic: Na-s-gi ka-no-he-dv i-tsi-ga-ta-ha, gv-ni-ge-sv tsi-nu-li-s-ta-nv ni-gv-na-dv Tsu-di-yi, Ge-li-li tsv-da-yu-da-le-nv-hv di-da-wo-s-di ge-sv u-gv-wa-li nu-li-tsi-do-no Tsa-ni^

051038.english: How God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Ghost and with power: who went about doing good, and healing all that were oppressed of the devil; for God was with him.
051038.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛ ᎨᏒ ᏥᏚᎶᏁᏔᏁ ᏥᏌ ᎾᏎᎵᏗ ᎡᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏤᏙᎲ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᎧᏅᏫᏍᎬ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏳᏅᎿᎭᏕᎩ ᎠᏍᎩᎾ; ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏰᏃ ᏚᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎲᎩ.
051038.phonetic: Na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do a-le u-li-ni-gi-dv ge-sv tsi-du-lo-ne-ta-ne Tsi-sa Na-se-li-di e-hi, na-s-gi tse-do-hv o-s-dv du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv-i, a-le de-ka-nv-wi-s-gv ni-ga-dv u-yo i-yu-nv-hna-de-gi a-s-gi-na; U-ne-la-nv-hi-ye-no du-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hv-gi.

051039.english: And we are witnesses of all things which he did both in the land of the Jews, and in Jerusalem; whom they slew and hanged on a tree:
051039.cherokee: ᎠᏴᏃ ᎣᏥᏃᎮᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏄᏛᏁᎵᏙᎸ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏂᎸᎩ ᎨᏛ ᏧᎾᏛᏅᎩ.
051039.phonetic: A-yv-no o-tsi-no-he-s-gi na-s-gi ni-ga-dv nu-dv-ne-li-do-lv A-ni-tsu-si u-na-tse-li-go-hi a-le Tsi-lu-si-li-mi; na-s-gi tsu-ni-lv-gi ge-dv tsu-na-dv-nv-gi.

051040.english: Him God raised up the third day, and shewed him openly;
051040.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏦᎢᏁ ᎢᎦ ᏕᎤᎴᏔᏅᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᏚᏩᏁᎸᎩ,
051040.phonetic: Na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi tso-i-ne i-ga de-u-le-ta-nv-gi a-le gv-ni-ge-sv ni-du-wa-ne-lv-gi,

051041.english: Not to all the people, but unto witnesses chosen before of God, even to us, who did eat and drink with him after he rose from the dead.
051041.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏴᏫ, ᎠᏂᏃᎮᏍᎩᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎦᏳᎳ ᏗᎬᏩᏑᏰᏛ ᏥᎨᏒᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏴ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎣᎦᎵᏍᏓᏴᏅᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏦᎦᏗᏔᎲᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏲᎱᏒ ᏧᎴᎯᏌᏅᎯ ᏥᏂᎨᏎᎢ.
051041.phonetic: V-tla ni-ga-dv yv-wi, a-ni-no-he-s-gi-quo-s-gi-ni u-nv-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi ga-yu-la di-gv-wa-su-ye-dv tsi-ge-sv-gi, na-s-gi a-yv i-tsu-la-ha o-ga-li-s-da-yv-nv-hi a-le tso-ga-di-ta-hv-hi na-s-gi u-yo-hu-sv tsu-le-hi-sa-nv-hi tsi-ni-ge-se-i.

051042.english: And he commanded us to preach unto the people, and to testify that it is he which was ordained of God to be the Judge of quick and dead.
051042.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎣᎩᏁᏤᎸᎩ ᏴᏫ ᏦᏣᎵᏥᏙᏁᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᎩᏃᎮᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᎧᏅ ᏗᎫᎪᏓᏁᎯ ᏄᏩᏁᎸ ᏗᏅᏃᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏧᏂᏲᎱᏒᎯ.
051042.phonetic: A-le o-gi-ne-tse-lv-gi yv-wi tso-tsa-li-tsi-do-ne-di-yi, a-le o-gi-no-he-di-yi na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-we-ka-nv di-gu-go-da-ne-hi nu-wa-ne-lv di-nv-no-dv a-le tsu-ni-yo-hu-sv-hi.

051043.english: To him give all the prophets witness, that through his name whosoever believeth in him shall receive remission of sins.
051043.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᏃᎮᎭ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᎭ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎩᎶ ᎪᎯᏳᎲᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏕᎤᏙᎥᎢ, ᎠᏥᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎤᏍᎦᏅᏨᎢ.
051043.phonetic: Na-s-gi ni-ga-dv a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi gv-wa-no-he-ha, hi-a na-ni-we-ha; na-s-gi Gi-lo go-hi-yu-hv-s-ge-s-di na-s-gi de-u-do-v-i, a-tsi-do-li-s-di ge-se-s-di u-s-ga-nv-tsv-i.

051044.english: While Peter yet spake these words, the Holy Ghost fell on all them which heard the word.
051044.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏉᏃ ᏈᏓ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎨᎢ, ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎡᎳᏗ ᏄᏛᏁᎴ ᎤᏂᎷᏤᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᎾᏛᎩᏍᎩ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ.
051044.phonetic: A-si-quo-no Qui-da hi-a na-s-gi ni-ga-we-s-ge-i, Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do e-la-di nu-dv-ne-le u-ni-lu-tse-le ni-ga-dv a-na-dv-gi-s-gi ka-no-he-dv.
