052110.english: And as we tarried there many days, there came down from Judaea a certain prophet, named Agabus.
052110.cherokee: ᎤᏬᎯᏨᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᎣᎦᏅᏅ, ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎤᎷᏨᎩ, ᎡᎩᏩ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᏧᏗᏱ ᏅᏓᏳᎶᏒᎯ.
052110.phonetic: U-wo-hi-tsv-no na-hna o-ga-nv-nv, gi-lo i-yu-s-di a-do-le-ho-s-gi u-lu-tsv-gi, E-gi-wa tsu-do-i-dv, Tsu-di-yi nv-da-yu-lo-sv-hi.

052111.english: And when he was come unto us, he took Paul's girdle, and bound his own hands and feet, and said, Thus saith the Holy Ghost, So shall the Jews at Jerusalem bind the man that owneth this girdle, and shall deliver him into the hands of the Gentiles.
052111.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᎷᏨ ᎣᏤᏙᎲ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᏒ ᏉᎳ ᎤᏓᏠᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎸᎸ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏧᏬᏰᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᎳᏏᏕᏂ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏪᎠ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏅᏛᏅᏁᎵ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏛᎾᎸᎵ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏓᏠᏍᏗ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᏫᏙᏛᏂᏲᎯᏎᎵ.
052111.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-lu-tsv o-tse-do-hv, a-le u-ne-sv Quo-la u-da-tlo-s-di, a-le du-lv-lv u-wa-sv tsu-wo-ye-ni, a-le tsu-la-si-de-ni, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Hi-a ni-ga-we-a Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do; na-s-gi hi-a nv-dv-nv-ne-li A-ni-tsu-si Tsi-lu-si-li-mi dv-na-lv-li a-s-ga-ya hi-a a-da-tlo-s-di u-tse-li-ga, a-le tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi wi-do-dv-ni-yo-hi-se-li.

052112.english: And when we heard these things, both we, and they of that place, besought him not to go up to Jerusalem.
052112.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎣᎦᏛᎦᏅ, ᎠᏴ ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏁᎯ ᎣᏥᏍᏗᏰᏔᏅᎩ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎤᏪᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
052112.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hi-a o-ga-dv-ga-nv, a-yv a-le na-hna a-ne-hi o-tsi-s-di-ye-ta-nv-gi Tsi-lu-si-li-mi u-we-nv-s-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na.

052113.english: Then Paul answered, What mean ye to weep and to break mine heart? for I am ready not to be bound only, but also to die at Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus.
052113.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏉᎳ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎦᏙ ᎢᏥᏰᎸᏗ, ᏥᏕᏥᏴᎦ ᎠᎴ ᏥᏥᏓᏬᏗᎭ ᎠᎩᎾᏫ; ᎠᏆᏛᏅᎢᏍᏗᏰᏃ, ᎥᏝ ᎥᏆᎸᏍᏗᏱᏉ ᎤᏩᏒ, ᎾᏍᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᎩᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ, ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᏚᏙᎥ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ.
052113.phonetic: A-se-no Quo-la u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Ga-do i-tsi-ye-lv-di, tsi-de-tsi-yv-ga a-le tsi-tsi-da-wo-di-ha a-gi-na-wi; a-qua-dv-nv-i-s-di-ye-no, v-tla v-qua-lv-s-di-yi-quo u-wa-sv, na-s-quo-s-gi-ni a-gi-yo-hu-hi-s-di-yi Tsi-lu-si-li-mi, nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv du-do-v U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa.

052114.english: And when he would not be persuaded, we ceased, saying, The will of the Lord be done.
052114.cherokee: ᎣᏂᏄᎸᏅᏃ ᎣᏥᏍᏗᏰᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᎣᎩᏑᎵᎪᏨᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏃᎩᏪᏒᎩ; ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬ ᏫᏂᎦᎵᏍᏓ.
052114.phonetic: O-ni-nu-lv-nv-no o-tsi-s-di-ye-di-s-gv-i, o-gi-su-li-go-tsv-gi, hi-a no-gi-we-sv-gi; U-gv-wi-yu-hi a-da-nv-te-s-gv wi-ni-ga-li-s-da.

052115.english: And after those days we took up our carriages, and went up to Jerusalem.
052115.cherokee: ᎾᎯᏳᏃ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎶᏐᏅ, ᎣᎩᏟᏌᏅᎩ ᎣᎩᏱᏓᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏬᎩᎶᏒᎩ.
052115.phonetic: Na-hi-yu-no ge-sv u-lo-so-nv, o-gi-tli-sa-nv-gi o-gi-yi-da-s-di, a-le Tsi-lu-si-li-mi wo-gi-lo-sv-gi.

052116.english: There went with us also certain of the disciples of Caesarea, and brought with them one Mnason of Cyprus, an old disciple, with whom we should lodge.
052116.cherokee: ᎾᏍᏉᏃ ᎥᎪᎩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏒᎩ ᎢᎸᏍᎩ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᏏᏌᎵᏱ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏘᏅᏒᎩ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏁᏌᏂ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᏌᏈ ᎡᎯ, ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᎬᏬᎯᏳᏅᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏁᎸ ᎣᎦᏅᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏒᎩ.
052116.phonetic: Na-s-quo-no v-go-gi-s-da-wa-dv-sv-gi i-lv-s-gi i-ya-ni-dv a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi Si-sa-li-yi a-ne-hi, a-le u-na-ti-nv-sv-gi gi-lo i-yu-s-di Ne-sa-ni tsu-do-i-dv Sa-qui e-hi, i-lv-hi-yu gv-wo-hi-yu-nv-hi, na-s-gi ga-ne-lv o-ga-nv-di-yi u-ni-ye-lv-sv-gi.

052117.english: And when we were come to Jerusalem, the brethren received us gladly.

052118.english: And the day following Paul went in with us unto James; and all the elders were present.
052118.cherokee: ᎣᎩᏨᏛᏃ ᏉᎳ ᏥᎻ ᏧᏬᎸ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎣᎨᏅᏒᎩ, ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲᎩ.
052118.phonetic: O-gi-tsv-dv-no Quo-la Tsi-mi tsu-wo-lv i-tsu-la-ha o-ge-nv-sv-gi, ni-ga-dv-no di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di a-ni-ga-ta-hv-gi.

052119.english: And when he had saluted them, he declared particularly what things God had wrought among the Gentiles by his ministry.
052119.cherokee: ᏕᎤᏲᎵᎸᏃ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᏱᎸᏒᎩ ᎤᏃᎮᎸᎩ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏕᎤᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᏓᏁᏩᏗᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎬᏗᏍᎬᎢ.
052119.phonetic: De-u-yo-li-lv-no o-s-dv u-yi-lv-sv-gi u-no-he-lv-gi nu-s-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi de-u-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi da-ne-wa-di-sv-i, na-s-gi Quo-la a-gv-di-s-gv-i.

052120.english: And when they heard it, they glorified the Lord, and said unto him, Thou seest, brother, how many thousands of Jews there are which believe; and they are all zealous of the law:
052120.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ, ᎤᏂᎸᏉᏔᏅᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ.  ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎢᏓᎵᏅᏟ, ᎭᏙᎴᎰᏍᎦ ᏄᏂᏧᏈᏍᏛ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏅᎯ; ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᏂᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ.
052120.phonetic: U-na-dv-ga-nv-no na-s-gi, u-ni-lv-quo-ta-nv-gi U-gv-wi-yu-hi.  Hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi; I-da-li-nv-tli, ha-do-le-ho-s-ga nu-ni-tsu-qui-s-dv A-ni-tsu-si u-no-hi-yu-nv-hi; ni-ga-dv-no u-tsa-ti u-ni-lv-quo-di-yu di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di.

052121.english: And they are informed of thee, that thou teachest all the Jews which are among the Gentiles to forsake Moses, saying that they ought not to circumcise their children, neither to walk after the customs.
052121.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎨᏣᏛᎦᏁᎸ ᏕᎮᏲᎲᏍᎬᎢ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎢᎠᏂᏧᏏ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᎾᏓᏑᏯ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᏓᏁᏩᏗᏒᎢ, ᏧᏂᏲᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎼᏏ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏪᏍᎬᎢ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏗᏥᎱᏍᏕᏎᎮᏍᏗ ᏗᏂᏲᎵ, ᎠᎴ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏱᏥᏍᏓᏩᏕᎨᏍᏗ.
052121.phonetic: A-le ge-tsa-dv-ga-ne-lv de-he-yo-hv-s-gv-i ni-ga-dv Ia-ni-tsu-si, na-s-gi tsu-na-da-su-ya tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi da-ne-wa-di-sv-i, tsu-ni-yo-hi-s-di-yi Mo-si, hi-a ni-we-s-gv-i; Tle-s-di yi-di-tsi-hu-s-de-se-he-s-di di-ni-yo-li, a-le tle-s-di di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di yi-tsi-s-da-wa-de-ge-s-di.

052122.english: What is it therefore? the multitude must needs come together: for they will hear that thou art come.
052122.cherokee: ᎦᏙᏃ ᎠᏛᏁᏗ?  ᎠᏎ ᎤᎾᏓᏟᏐᏗ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ, ᏛᎾᏛᎦᏂᏰᏃ ᏣᎷᏨᎢ.
052122.phonetic: Ga-do-no a-dv-ne-di?  A-se u-na-da-tli-so-di u-ni-tsa-ti, dv-na-dv-ga-ni-ye-no tsa-lu-tsv-i.

052123.english: Do therefore this that we say to thee: We have four men which have a vow on them;
052123.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏨᏪᏎᎲ ᎿᎭᏛᎦ.  ᏅᎩ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᎪᎨᎳᏗᏙᎭ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏄᎾᏚᎵᏄᏁᎸ.
052123.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di hi-a ni-tsv-we-se-hv hna-dv-ga.  Nv-gi i-ya-ni-dv a-ni-s-ga-ya go-ge-la-di-do-ha U-ne-la-nv-hi nu-na-du-li-nu-ne-lv.

052124.english: Them take, and purify thyself with them, and be at charges with them, that they may shave their heads: and all may know that those things, whereof they were informed concerning thee, are nothing; but that thou thyself also walkest orderly, and keepest the law.
052124.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏔᏘᏄᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᎦᎸ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎢᏣᎫᏴ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏗᏂᏍᎪᎵ ᏕᎠᎾᎵᏍᏙᏰᎲᎭ; ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏒᎭ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎨᏣᏛᎦᏁᎸᎢ ᎠᏎᏉᏉ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏒᎭ ᏨᏒ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎭᎴᏂᏙᎲᎢ, ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎯᏍᏓᏩᏕᎬᎢ.
052124.phonetic: Na-s-gi ta-ti-nu-ga, a-le i-tsu-la-ha i-tsa-da-nv-ga-lv, a-le i-tsu-la-ha i-tsa-gu-yv, na-s-gi-no di-ni-s-go-li de-a-na-li-s-do-ye-hv-ha; a-le ni-ga-dv a-na-do-le-ho-sv-ha nu-s-dv ge-tsa-dv-ga-ne-lv-i a-se-quo-quo ge-sv-i, a-le a-na-do-le-ho-sv-ha tsv-sv na-s-quo du-yu-go-dv ha-le-ni-do-hv-i, di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di hi-s-da-wa-de-gv-i.

052125.english: As touching the Gentiles which believe, we have written and concluded that they observe no such thing, save only that they keep themselves from things offered to idols, and from blood, and from strangled, and from fornication.
052125.cherokee: ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛᏍᎩᏂ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ, ᏙᏦᏪᎳᏁᎸ ᏙᎫᎪᏔᏅ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏧᏁᏯᏙᏤᏗᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏗᏰᎸᎯ ᏗᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᎸᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎩᎬ ᎠᎴ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎬᏬᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᏗᏂᏏᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
052125.phonetic: Tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv-s-gi-ni yv-wi u-no-hi-yu-nv-hi tsi-gi, do-tso-we-la-ne-lv do-gu-go-ta-nv u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-do-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na go-hu-s-di na-s-gi i-yu-s-di tsu-ne-ya-do-tse-di-quo-s-gi-ni u-wa-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi di-ye-lv-hi di-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-lv-hi, a-le gi-gv a-le go-hu-s-di gv-wo-s-ta-nv-hi a-le u-de-li-dv di-ni-si-di ge-sv-i.

052126.english: Then Paul took the men, and the next day purifying himself with them entered into the temple, to signify the accomplishment of the days of purification, until that an offering should be offered for every one of them.
052126.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏉᎳ ᏚᏯᏅᎲᎩ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ, ᎤᎩᏨᏛᏃ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᎦᎸᎲᎩ, ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏭᏂᏴᎸᎩ, ᎧᏃᎲᏍᎬᎩ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᏛ ᎢᎪᎯᏛ ᎠᏓᏅᎦᎸᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᎬᎢ ᎠᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏂᏏᏴᏫᎭ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᎪᎸᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
052126.phonetic: Hna-quo-no quo-la du-ya-nv-hv-gi a-ni-s-ga-ya, u-gi-tsv-dv-no i-tsu-la-ha u-na-da-nv-ga-lv-hv-gi, di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi wu-ni-yv-lv-gi, ka-no-hv-s-gv-gi u-li-s-qua-dv i-go-hi-dv a-da-nv-ga-lv-di ge-sv-i, na-s-gi a-li-s-qua-di-s-gv-i a-da-ne-di ge-sv a-ni-si-yv-wi-ha u-na-li-s-go-lv-do-di ge-sv-i.

052127.english: And when the seven days were almost ended, the Jews which were of Asia, when they saw him in the temple, stirred up all the people, and laid hands on him,
052127.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎵᏉᎩ ᎢᎦ ᏚᎵᏍᏆᏗᏗᏒᎩ, ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎡᏏᏱ ᏅᏓᏳᏂᎶᏒᎯ ᎬᏩᎪᎲ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᏚᏂᏖᎸᏅᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏴᏫ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏂᏴᎲᎩ,
052127.phonetic: Hna-quo-no na-s-gi ga-li-quo-gi i-ga du-li-s-qua-di-di-sv-gi, A-ni-tsu-si E-si-yi nv-da-yu-ni-lo-sv-hi gv-wa-go-hv di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi, du-ni-te-lv-nv-gi ni-ga-dv yv-wi, a-le gv-wa-ni-yv-hv-gi,

052128.english: Crying out, Men of Israel, help: This is the man, that teacheth all men every where against the people, and the law, and this place: and further brought Greeks also into the temple, and hath polluted this holy place.
052128.cherokee: ᎤᏁᎷᏅᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ, ᎢᏏᎵ ᏧᏪᏥ, ᏍᎩᏍᏕᎸ!  ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏕᎨᏲᎲᏍᎦ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏴᏫ ᏂᎬᎾᏛᎢ, ᏥᏓᏡᏗᎭ ᎯᎠ ᏴᏫ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏥᏚᏴᏔᏂᎸ ᎠᏂᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏥᏄᏩᏁᎸ ᎠᏂ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ.
052128.phonetic: U-ne-lu-nv-gi hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; I-tsi-s-ga-ya, I-si-li tsu-we-tsi, s-gi-s-de-lv!  Hi-a na-s-gi a-s-ga-ya na-s-gi tsi-de-ge-yo-hv-s-ga na-ni-v yv-wi ni-gv-na-dv-i, tsi-da-tlu-di-ha hi-a yv-wi a-le di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di a-le a-ni ge-sv-i, a-le na-s-quo di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi tsi-du-yv-ta-ni-lv A-ni-go-i, a-le ga-da-ha tsi-nu-wa-ne-lv a-ni ga-lv-quo-di-yu ge-sv-i.

052129.english: (For they had seen before with him in the city Trophimus an Ephesian, whom they supposed that Paul had brought into the temple.)
052129.cherokee: ᎦᏳᎳᏰᏃ ᎤᏂᎪᎲᎯ ᎨᏒᎩ ᏠᏆᎹ ᎡᏈᏌ ᎡᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏉᎳ ᎠᏁᏙᎲ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏉᎳ ᎤᏴᏔᏂᎸ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᏁᎵᏍᎬᎩ.
052129.phonetic: Ga-yu-la-ye-no u-ni-go-hv-hi ge-sv-gi Tlo-qua-ma E-qui-sa e-hi, na-s-gi Quo-la a-ne-do-hv ga-du-hv-i, na-s-gi Quo-la u-yv-ta-ni-lv di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi a-ne-li-s-gv-gi.

052130.english: And all the city was moved, and the people ran together: and they took Paul, and drew him out of the temple: and forthwith the doors were shut.
052130.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎤᎾᎵᏖᎸᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏴᏫ ᎤᎾᏓᏟᏌᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏉᎳ ᎤᏂᏂᏴᎲᎩ ᎤᏂᎾᏌᎾᏫᏛᎲᎩ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏄᎪᏫᏒᎩ; ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏚᏂᏍᏚᏅᎩ.
052130.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv-no ga-du-hv u-na-li-te-lv-nv-gi, a-le yv-wi u-na-da-tli-sa-nv-gi, a-le Quo-la u-ni-ni-yv-hv-gi u-ni-na-sa-na-wi-dv-hv-gi di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi u-ni-nu-go-wi-sv-gi; gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi du-ni-s-du-nv-gi.

052131.english: And as they went about to kill him, tidings came unto the chief captain of the band, that all Jerusalem was in an uproar.
052131.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏉᏃ ᎤᏂᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬᎩ, ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᏗ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎤᏛᎦᏅᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎠᎾᎵᏖᎸᎲᏍᎬᎢ;
052131.phonetic: A-si-quo-no u-ni-hi-s-di-yi a-na-da-nv-te-s-gv-gi, su-na-da-tlu-gi ge-sv a-ni-ya-wi-s-di di-ti-ni-do-hi u-dv-ga-nv-gi ni-ga-dv Tsi-lu-si-li-mi a-na-li-te-lv-hv-s-gv-i;

052132.english: Who immediately took soldiers and centurions, and ran down unto them: and when they saw the chief captain and the soldiers, they left beating of Paul.
052132.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏫᏚᏯᏅᎲ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᏗᎾᏘᏂᏙᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏁᏙᎲ ᏭᏗᏢᏍᏔᏅᎩ.  ᏚᏂᎪᎲᏃ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᏗᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ, ᎤᏂᏑᎵᎪᏨᎩ ᎠᏂᎸᏂᎲ ᏉᎳ.
052132.phonetic: Na-s-gi na gi-la-quo i-yv-dv wi-du-ya-nv-hv a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi a-le a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui di-na-ti-ni-do-hi, a-le di-ne-do-hv wu-di-tlv-s-ta-nv-gi.  Du-ni-go-hv-no nu-gv-wi-yu-sv di-da-ti-ni-do-hi a-le a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi, u-ni-su-li-go-tsv-gi a-ni-lv-ni-hv Quo-la.

052133.english: Then the chief captain came near, and took him, and commanded him to be bound with two chains; and demanded who he was, and what he had done.
052133.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᏗᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎾᎥ ᎤᎷᏨ, ᎤᏂᏴᎲᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᏨᎩ ᏔᎵ ᏧᏓᏕᏒᏛ ᏣᎦᎸᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏛᏛᏅᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ, ᎠᏍᎦᏯ, ᎠᎴ ᏄᏛᏁᎸᎢ.
052133.phonetic: Hna-quo-no nu-gv-wi-yu-sv di-da-ti-ni-do-hi na-v u-lu-tsv, u-ni-yv-hv-gi, a-le u-ne-tsv-gi ta-li tsu-da-de-sv-dv tsa-ga-lv-s-do-di-yi, a-le u-dv-dv-nv-gi i-yu-s-di ge-sv, a-s-ga-ya, a-le nu-dv-ne-lv-i.

052134.english: And some cried one thing, some another, among the multitude: and when he could not know the certainty for the tumult, he commanded him to be carried into the castle.
052134.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᎤᏁᎷᏅᎩ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛᏉ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ.  ᎤᏄᎸᏅᏃ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᎤᏓᏑᏰᏛ ᎠᎾᎵᏖᎸᎲᏍᎬᎢ, ᎤᏁᏨᎩ ᏗᏐᏴ ᏩᎦᏘᏅᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
052134.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-ti-no u-ne-lu-nv-gi tsu-da-le-nv-dv-quo nu-ni-we-sv-gi.  U-nu-lv-nv-no du-yu-go-dv u-do-le-ho-hi-s-di-yi nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv u-da-su-ye-dv a-na-li-te-lv-hv-s-gv-i, u-ne-tsv-gi di-so-yv wa-ga-ti-nv-s-do-di-yi.

052135.english: And when he came upon the stairs, so it was, that he was borne of the soldiers for the violence of the people.
052135.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᎠᎩᎳᏫᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᎷᏨ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᏅᏒᎩ, ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᎬᏍᎦᎢᏍᏓᎩ ᎾᎾᏛᏁᎲ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ.
052135.phonetic: Quo-la-no a-gi-la-wi-s-di-yi u-lu-tsv a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi gv-wa-nv-sv-gi, nv-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-gi gv-s-ga-i-s-da-gi na-na-dv-ne-hv u-ni-tsa-ti.

052136.english: For the multitude of the people followed after, crying, Away with him.
052136.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏰᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏒᎩ, ᎠᏁᎷᎲᏍᎬᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎬᎩ; ᎯᎷᎦ.
052136.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-ti-ye-no yv-wi gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-sv-gi, a-ne-lu-hv-s-gv-gi hi-a na-ni-we-s-gv-gi; hi-lu-ga.

052137.english: And as Paul was to be led into the castle, he said unto the chief captain, May I speak unto thee? Who said, Canst thou speak Greek?
052137.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏉᎳ ᏗᏐᏴ ᏮᏓᏰᏥᏴᏔᏂᏒᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᏗᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ; ᏥᏌ ᏰᎵ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎦᎬᏲᏎᏗ?  ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏥᎪ ᎠᏂᎪᎢ ᎤᏂᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏰᎵ ᎨᏣᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗ?
052137.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Quo-la di-so-yv wv-da-ye-tsi-yv-ta-ni-sv-i, hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi nu-gv-wi-yu-sv di-da-ti-ni-do-hi; Tsi-sa ye-li go-hu-s-di ga-gv-yo-se-di?  Na-s-gi-no hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Tsi-go A-ni-go-i u-ni-wo-ni-hi-s-di ge-sv ye-li ge-tsa-wo-ni-hi-s-di?

052138.english: Art not thou that Egyptian, which before these days madest an uproar, and leddest out into the wilderness four thousand men that were murderers?
052138.cherokee: ᏝᏍᎪ ᏂᎯ ᏱᎩ Ꮎ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ ᏤᎲᎩ, ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᎤᏓᏑᏰᏛ ᎤᎾᎵᏖᎸᏗᏱ ᏥᏄᏩᏁᎸᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᏚᏘᎿᎭᏫᏛᎲᎩ ᎢᎾᎨ ᏥᏫᏚᏘᏅᏍᏔᏅᎩ ᏅᎩ ᎢᏯᎦᏴᎵ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᏴᏫ ᏗᏂᎯᎯ?
052138.phonetic: Tla-s-go ni-hi yi-gi na I-tsi-qui-yi tse-hv-gi, i-lv-hi-yu u-da-su-ye-dv u-na-li-te-lv-di-yi tsi-nu-wa-ne-lv-gi, a-le tsi-du-ti-hna-wi-dv-hv-gi i-na-ge tsi-wi-du-ti-nv-s-ta-nv-gi nv-gi i-ya-ga-yv-li i-ya-ni-dv a-ni-s-ga-ya yv-wi di-ni-hi-hi?

052139.english: But Paul said, I am a man which am a Jew of Tarsus, a city in Cilicia, a citizen of no mean city: and, I beseech thee, suffer me to speak unto the people.
052139.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎠᏴ ᏥᏧᏏ ᏏᎵᏏᏱ ᎨᎢ ᏓᏌ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᎤᏬᎭᏛ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ ᎨᎢ ᏱᎩ; ᎠᎴ ᎬᏔᏲᏎᎭ ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᏍᏇᎵᏎᏗᏱ ᏗᏥᏬᏁᏙᏗᏱ ᏴᏫ.
052139.phonetic: Quo-la-no hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; A-yv Tsi-tsu-si Si-li-si-yi ge-i Da-sa ga-du-hv-i, v-tla e-s-ga-quo u-wo-ha-dv ga-du-hv-i ge-i yi-gi; a-le gv-ta-yo-se-ha u-ne-la-gi s-que-li-se-di-yi di-tsi-wo-ne-do-di-yi yv-wi.

052140.english: And when he had given him licence, Paul stood on the stairs, and beckoned with the hand unto the people. And when there was made a great silence, he spake unto them in the Hebrew tongue, saying,
052140.cherokee: ᎤᏁᎳᎩᏃ ᎤᏪᎵᏎᎸ, ᏉᎳ ᎤᎴᏅᎩ ᎠᎩᎳᏫᏍᏗᏱ, ᎤᏬᏰᏂ ᏴᏫ ᏚᏖᎸᎾᏁᎸᎩ; ᎤᏣᏘᏃ ᎡᎳᏪ ᎤᏅᏅ, ᏚᏬᏁᏔᏅᎩ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᏂᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗ ᎤᏮᏔᏅᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ;
052140.phonetic: U-ne-la-gi-no u-we-li-se-lv, Quo-la u-le-nv-gi a-gi-la-wi-s-di-yi, u-wo-ye-ni yv-wi du-te-lv-na-ne-lv-gi; u-tsa-ti-no e-la-we u-nv-nv, du-wo-ne-ta-nv-gi A-ni-tsu-si u-ni-wo-ni-hi-s-di u-wv-ta-nv-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi;

052201.english: Men, brethren, and fathers, hear ye my defence which I make now unto you.
052201.cherokee: ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ ᎢᏓᏓᏅᏟ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎩᏙᏓ, ᎢᏣᏛᏓᏍᏓ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏆᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏨᏓᏥᏬᏂᏏ.
052201.phonetic: I-tsi-s-ga-ya i-da-da-nv-tli a-le di-gi-do-da, i-tsa-dv-da-s-da hi-a a-qua-li-s-de-lv-do-di hna-quo tsv-da-tsi-wo-ni-si.

052202.english: (And when they heard that he spake in the Hebrew tongue to them, they kept the more silence: and he saith,)
052202.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅᏃ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᏂᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᎤᏂᏬᏁᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᎤᏟ ᎢᎦᎢ ᎡᎳᏪ ᎤᏅᏅᎩ; ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ;
052202.phonetic: U-na-dv-ga-nv-no A-ni-tsu-si u-ni-wo-ni-hi-s-di ge-sv gv-di-s-gv u-ni-wo-ne-di-s-gv-i, u-tli i-ga-i e-la-we u-nv-nv-gi; hi-a-no nu-we-sv-gi;

052203.english: I am verily a man which am a Jew, born in Tarsus, a city in Cilicia, yet brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, and taught according to the perfect manner of the law of the fathers, and was zealous toward God, as ye all are this day.
052203.cherokee: ᎠᏴ ᏥᏧᏏ ᏏᎵᏏᏱ ᎠᏆᏕᏅᎯ ᏓᏌ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏂ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎠᏆᏛᏒᎯ, ᎨᎺᎵ ᏚᎳᏍᎬᎢ ᎥᏇᏲᏅᎯ ᏭᏂᎫᏛ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᎨᎩᏁᎸᎯ, ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏗᏳᏃ ᎠᏆᏓᏅᏛᎩ ᏕᏥᎦᎿᎭᏩᏗᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏂᏥᎥ ᏂᎯ ᏥᏄᏍᏗ ᏥᏣᏓᏅᏔ ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ ᏥᎩ.
052203.phonetic: A-yv Tsi-tsu-si Si-li-si-yi a-qua-de-nv-hi Da-sa ga-du-hv-i, a-se-no a-ni ga-du-hv a-qua-dv-sv-hi, Ge-me-li du-la-s-gv-i v-que-yo-nv-hi wu-ni-gu-dv di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di ge-sv di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge ge-gi-ne-lv-hi, u-li-ni-gi-di-yu-no a-qua-da-nv-dv-gi de-tsi-ga-hna-wa-di-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi na-s-gi-ya ni-tsi-v ni-hi tsi-nu-s-di tsi-tsa-da-nv-ta go-hi i-ga tsi-gi.

052204.english: And I persecuted this way unto the death, binding and delivering into prisons both men and women.
052204.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏲᎱᏍᎩ ᎦᏥᏯᏕᏯᏙᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎦᏅᏅ ᏗᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏕᎩ, ᏕᎦᏥᏯᎸᎢᎲᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏗᏱ ᏕᎦᏥᏴᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ.
052204.phonetic: A-le gv-ni-yo-hu-s-gi ga-tsi-ya-de-ya-do-di-s-gv-gi hi-a ga-nv-nv di-ni-ka-hna-wa-de-gi, de-ga-tsi-ya-lv-i-hv-gi a-le di-da-s-du-di-yi de-ga-tsi-yv-di-s-gv-gi a-ni-s-ga-ya a-le a-ni-ge-yv.

052205.english: As also the high priest doth bear me witness, and all the estate of the elders: from whom also I received letters unto the brethren, and went to Damascus, to bring them which were there bound unto Jerusalem, for to be punished.
052205.cherokee: ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒᏃ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎶᎯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎾᏋᏁᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎪᏪᎵ ᏕᎬᎩᏅᏁᎸᎩ ᎢᏓᎵᏅᏟ ᏧᏂᎪᏩᏛᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᎹᏍᎦ ᏩᎩᎶᏒᎩ, ᏕᏥᏯᏅᎲᏒ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏁᎯ ᏗᎨᎦᎸᎢᏛ ᏗᎦᏥᏯᏘᏃᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ, ᎨᏥᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
052205.phonetic: Nu-gv-wi-yu-sv-no a-tsi-lv--ge-lo-hi na-s-quo gv-ni-ge-sv na-quv-ne-ha, a-le ni-ga-dv di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di ge-sv-i, na-s-gi na-s-quo go-we-li de-gv-gi-nv-ne-lv-gi i-da-li-nv-tli tsu-ni-go-wa-dv-di, a-le De-ma-s-ga wa-gi-lo-sv-gi, de-tsi-ya-nv-hv-sv na-hna a-ne-hi di-ge-ga-lv-i-dv di-ga-tsi-ya-ti-no-hi-s-di-yi Tsi-lu-si-li-mi, ge-tsi-gi-li-yo-i-s-do-di-yi.

052206.english: And it came to pass, that, as I made my journey, and was come nigh unto Damascus about noon, suddenly there shone from heaven a great light round about me.
052206.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏆᎵᏍᏓᏁᎸᎩ ᎦᎢᏒᎢ, ᏕᎹᏍᎦ ᎾᎥ ᏩᎩᎷᏨ, 'ᎢᎦ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎢᏴᏛ, ᎤᏰᎶᎢᏍᏔᏅᎩ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏚᎸᏌᏓᏛᎩ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏅᏓᏳᎶᏒᎯ ᏓᏆᏚᏫᏍᏔᏅᎩ.
052206.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hi-a na-qua-li-s-da-ne-lv-gi ga-i-sv-i, De-ma-s-ga na-v wa-gi-lu-tsv, '-i-ga a-ye-li i-yv-dv, u-ye-lo-i-s-ta-nv-gi u-tsa-ti du-lv-sa-da-dv-gi ga-lv-la-di nv-da-yu-lo-sv-hi da-qua-du-wi-s-ta-nv-gi.

052207.english: And I fell unto the ground, and heard a voice saying unto me, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?
052207.cherokee: ᎡᎳᏗᏃ ᎠᎩᏅᏨᎩ, ᎧᏁᎬᏃ ᎠᏆᏛᎦᏅᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᎩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏐᎳ, ᏐᎳ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎤᏲ ᏂᏍᏋᏁᎭ?
052207.phonetic: E-la-di-no a-gi-nv-tsv-gi, ka-ne-gv-no a-qua-dv-ga-nv-gi, hi-a na-gi-we-se-lv-gi; So-la, So-la, ga-do-no u-yo ni-s-quv-ne-ha?

052208.english: And I answered, Who art thou, Lord? And he said unto me, I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom thou persecutest.
052208.cherokee: ᎠᏴᏃ ᎠᎩᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᎾᎩᏪᏒᎩ; ᎦᎪ ᏂᎯ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ?  ᎯᎠᏃ ᎾᎩᏪᏎᎸᎩ, ᎠᏴ ᏥᏌ ᎾᏎᎵᏘ ᏤᏍᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏲ ᏥᏂᏴᏁᎭ.
052208.phonetic: A-yv-no a-gi-ne-tsv hi-a na-gi-we-sv-gi; ga-go ni-hi, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi?  Hi-a-no na-gi-we-se-lv-gi, a-yv Tsi-sa Na-se-li-ti tse-s-gi, na-s-gi u-yo tsi-ni-yv-ne-ha.

052209.english: And they that were with me saw indeed the light, and were afraid; but they heard not the voice of him that spake to me.
052209.cherokee: ᎣᏤᎯᏃ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᎠᏂᎪᏩᏘᏍᎬᎩ ᎢᎦ ᎦᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎢᎲᎩ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏳᎾᏛᎦᏁ ᎧᏁᎬ ᎠᎩᏬᏁᏗᏍᎩ.
052209.phonetic: O-tse-hi-no u-do-hi-yu-hi a-ni-go-wa-ti-s-gv-gi i-ga ga-dv-i, a-le a-ni-s-ga-i-hv-gi, a-se-no v-tla yu-na-dv-ga-ne ka-ne-gv a-gi-wo-ne-di-s-gi.

052210.english: And I said, What shall I do, Lord? And the Lord said unto me, Arise, and go into Damascus; and there it shall be told thee of all things which are appointed for thee to do.
052210.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᎾᎩᏪᏒᎩ, ᎦᏙ ᏓᎦᏛᏁᎵ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ?  ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᎩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏔᎴᎲᎦ, ᏕᎹᏍᎦ ᎮᎾ, ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᏓᏰᏣᏆᏁᎵ ᏂᎦᎥ ᏚᏚᎪᏔᏅ ᎢᏣᏛᏁᏗᏱ.
052210.phonetic: Hi-a-no na-gi-we-sv-gi, Ga-do da-ga-dv-ne-li, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi?  U-gv-wi-yu-hi-no hi-a na-gi-we-se-lv-gi; Ta-le-hv-ga, De-ma-s-ga he-na, na-hna-no da-ye-tsa-qua-ne-li ni-ga-v du-du-go-ta-nv i-tsa-dv-ne-di-yi.

052211.english: And when I could not see for the glory of that light, being led by the hand of them that were with me, I came into Damascus.
052211.cherokee: ᎠᎩᏄᎸᏅᏃ ᎠᎩᎪᏩᏛᏗᏱ, ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᏗᎬᏩᎸᏌᏓ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎦ ᎦᏛᎢ, ᎣᏤᎯ ᎬᏉᏱᏁᏅ, ᏕᎹᏍᎦ ᏩᎩᏴᎸᎩ.
052211.phonetic: A-gi-nu-lv-nv-no a-gi-go-wa-dv-di-yi, nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv di-gv-wa-lv-sa-da na-s-gi i-ga ga-dv-i, o-tse-hi gv-quo-yi-ne-nv, De-ma-s-ga wa-gi-yv-lv-gi.

052212.english: And one Ananias, a devout man according to the law, having a good report of all the Jews which dwelt there,
052212.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎡᏂᎾᏯ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎤᏓᏅᏘ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᏓᏩᏕᎩ, ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏁᎯ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎬᏩᏃᎮᏍᎩ,
052212.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-s-di E-ni-na-ya tsu-do-i-dv, u-da-nv-ti di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di a-s-da-wa-de-gi, ni-ga-dv A-ni-tsu-si na-hna a-ne-hi o-s-dv gv-wa-no-he-s-gi,

052213.english: Came unto me, and stood, and said unto me, Brother Saul, receive thy sight. And the same hour I looked up upon him.
052213.cherokee: ᎠᎩᎷᏤᎸ ᎤᎴᏂᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᎩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏗᎾᏓᏅᏟ ᏐᎳ, ᏥᎯᎪᏩᏛ.  ᎾᎯᏳᏉᏃ ᏙᏥᏯᎦᎿᎭᏅᎩ.
052213.phonetic: A-gi-lu-tse-lv u-le-ni-lv-gi, hi-a na-gi-we-se-lv-gi; di-na-da-nv-tli So-la, tsi-hi-go-wa-dv.  Na-hi-yu-quo-no do-tsi-ya-ga-hna-nv-gi.

052214.english: And he said, The God of our fathers hath chosen thee, that thou shouldest know his will, and see that Just One, and shouldest hear the voice of his mouth.
052214.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ ᏣᏑᏰᏒ, ᏣᎦᏙᎥᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎠᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎪᏩᏛᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎾᏍᎦᏅᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎰᎵ ᎦᎾᏄᎪᎬ ᎧᏁᎬ ᏣᏛᎪᏗᏱ.
052214.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-we-sv-gi; U-ne-la-nv-hi di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge u-na-tse-li-ga tsa-su-ye-sv, tsa-ga-do-v-hi-s-di-yi nu-s-dv a-da-nv-te-s-gv-i, a-le hi-go-wa-dv-di-yi na-s-gi na na-s-ga-nv-na, a-le a-ho-li ga-na-nu-go-gv ka-ne-gv tsa-dv-go-di-yi.

052215.english: For thou shalt be his witness unto all men of what thou hast seen and heard.
052215.cherokee: ᎯᏃᎮᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏴᏫ ᎠᏁᎲᎢ ᏄᏍᏛ ᏣᎪᎲᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏣᏛᎦᏅᎢ.
052215.phonetic: Hi-no-he-s-gi-ye-no ge-se-s-di na-ni-v yv-wi a-ne-hv-i nu-s-dv tsa-go-hv-i a-le tsa-dv-ga-nv-i.

052216.english: And now why tarriest thou? arise, and be baptized, and wash away thy sins, calling on the name of the Lord.
052216.cherokee: Ꭷ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎢᎭᏙᎯᏗᎭ?  ᏔᎴᎲᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᏪᏣᏬᏣ, ᎠᎴ ᎰᏑᎵ ᏣᏍᎦᏅᏨᎢ, ᎯᏔᏲᎯᎮᏍᏗ ᎲᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏕᎤᏙᎥ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ.
052216.phonetic: Ka, ga-do-no i-ha-do-hi-di-ha?  Ta-le-hv-ga, a-le we-tsa-wo-tsa, a-le ho-su-li tsa-s-ga-nv-tsv-i, hi-ta-yo-hi-he-s-di hv-di-s-ge-s-di de-u-do-v U-gv-wi-yu-hi.

052217.english: And it came to pass, that, when I was come again to Jerusalem, even while I prayed in the temple, I was in a trance;
052217.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᎾᏆᎵᏍᏓᏁᎸ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎢᎠᎩᎷᏨ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᎤᏛᏅ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᏣᎦᎵᎰ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎾᏆᎵᏍᏓᏁᎸᎩ;
052217.phonetic: Hi-a-no na-qua-li-s-da-ne-lv Tsi-lu-si-li-mi i-a-gi-lu-tsv, na-s-gi ga-da-do-li-s-di-s-gv u-dv-nv di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi, tsa-ga-li-ho i-yu-s-di na-qua-li-s-da-ne-lv-gi;

052218.english: And saw him saying unto me, Make haste, and get thee quickly out of Jerusalem: for they will not receive thy testimony concerning me.
052218.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏥᎪᎥᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎾᎩᏪᏎᎲᎩ; ᎾᏞᎬᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎯᏄᎪᎢ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ; ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᏱᏙᏛᏂᏂᏴᎯ ᎠᏴ ᏍᎩᏃᎮᏍᎬᎢ.
052218.phonetic: A-le tsi-go-v-gi hi-a na-gi-we-se-hv-gi; na-tle-gv-quo i-yv-dv hi-nu-go-i Tsi-lu-si-li-mi; v-tla-ye-no yi-do-dv-ni-ni-yv-hi a-yv s-gi-no-he-s-gv-i.

052219.english: And I said, Lord, they know that I imprisoned and beat in every synagogue them that believed on thee:
052219.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᎾᎩᏪᏒᎩ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎭ ᏕᏥᏍᏚᏂᏙᎸᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏕᏥᎸᏂᎵᏙᎸᎢ ᎨᏦᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏕᎪᏢᏩᏗᏒᎢ.
052219.phonetic: A-le hi-a na-gi-we-sv-gi; Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, a-ni-ga-ta-ha de-tsi-s-du-ni-do-lv-i a-le de-tsi-lv-ni-li-do-lv-i ge-tso-hi-yu-hv-s-gi di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi de-go-tlv-wa-di-sv-i.

052220.english: And when the blood of thy martyr Stephen was shed, I also was standing by, and consenting unto his death, and kept the raiment of them that slew him.
052220.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏣᏃᎮᏍᎩ ᏍᏗᏫ ᎤᎩᎬ ᎤᏤᏬᏨ ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎾᎥ ᏥᏙᎬᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎠᎩᏰᎸᏒᎩ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᏥᎦᏘᏴᎩ ᏧᎾᏄᏬ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᎯᎯ.
052220.phonetic: A-le tsa-no-he-s-gi S-di-wi u-gi-gv u-tse-wo-tsv a-yv na-s-quo na-v tsi-do-gv-gi a-le o-si-yu a-gi-ye-lv-sv-gi u-yo-hu-sv-i, a-le de-tsi-ga-ti-yv-gi tsu-na-nu-wo na-s-gi gv-wa-hi-hi.

052221.english: And he said unto me, Depart: for I will send thee far hence unto the Gentiles.
052221.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᎾᎩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎭᏓᏅᎾ, ᏓᎬᏅᏏᏰᏃ ᎢᏅ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᏗᏁᎲᎢ.
052221.phonetic: Hi-a-no na-gi-we-se-lv-gi; Ha-da-nv-na, da-gv-nv-si-ye-no i-nv tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi di-ne-hv-i.

052222.english: And they gave him audience unto this word, and then lifted up their voices, and said, Away with such a fellow from the earth: for it is not fit that he should live.
052222.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏛᏓᏍᏓᏁᎲᎩᏃ ᎬᏂ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒ, ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᏂᏌᎳᏓᏅᎩ ᎠᏂᏁᎬ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎡᎶᎯ ᏩᎦᏘᎿᎭᏫᏛ ᎾᏍᎩᏉ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏴᏫ; ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᏰᎵ ᎬᏁᏍᏗ ᎦᏰᎵᏍᏗ ᏱᎩ.
052222.phonetic: Gv-wa-dv-da-s-da-ne-hv-gi-no gv-ni na-s-gi hi-a nu-we-sv, hna-quo-no u-ni-sa-la-da-nv-gi a-ni-ne-gv hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; e-lo-hi wa-ga-ti-hna-wi-dv na-s-gi-quo i-yu-s-di yv-wi; v-tla-ye-no ye-li gv-ne-s-di ga-ye-li-s-di yi-gi.

052223.english: And as they cried out, and cast off their clothes, and threw dust into the air,
052223.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏉᏃ ᎤᏁᎷᎬᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏚᎾᏓᎡᎬᎩ ᏧᎾᏄᏬ ᎠᎴ ᎪᏍᏚ ᎤᎾᏕᏏᏙᎲᎩ;
052223.phonetic: A-si-quo-no u-ne-lu-gv-gi a-le du-na-da-e-gv-gi tsu-na-nu-wo a-le go-s-du u-na-de-si-do-hv-gi;

052224.english: The chief captain commanded him to be brought into the castle, and bade that he should be examined by scourging; that he might know wherefore they cried so against him.
052224.cherokee: ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᏗᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎤᏁᏨᎩ ᏗᏐᏴ ᏩᏥᏴᏙᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏥᎵᎥᏂᎲ ᎠᏥᎪᎵᏰᏗᏱ, ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎬᎩ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎠᏁᎷᎲᏍᎬ ᎬᏩᏡᏗᏍᎬᎢ.
052224.phonetic: Nu-gv-wi-yu-sv di-da-ti-ni-do-hi u-ne-tsv-gi di-so-yv wa-tsi-yv-do-di-yi, a-le a-tsi-li-v-ni-hv a-tsi-go-li-ye-di-yi, u-du-li-s-gv-gi u-do-le-ho-hi-s-di-yi nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv ni-ga-v a-ne-lu-hv-s-gv gv-wa-tlu-di-s-gv-i.

052225.english: And as they bound him with thongs, Paul said unto the centurion that stood by, Is it lawful for you to scourge a man that is a Roman, and uncondemned?
052225.cherokee: ᎬᏩᎵᎥᏂᏍᏗᏱᏃ ᎬᏩᏘᏃᎸ, ᏉᎳ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᎾᎥ ᎦᏙᎩ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ; ᏰᎵᏉᏍᎪ ᏗᎧᎾᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏂᎬᏅ ᎦᏰᏥᎵᎥᏂᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᎶᎻ, ᎤᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᏧᏚᎪᏔᏅᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏱᎩ?
052225.phonetic: Gv-wa-li-v-ni-s-di-yi-no gv-wa-ti-no-lv, Quo-la hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi na-v ga-do-gi a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui di-ti-ni-do-hi; Ye-li-quo-s-go di-ka-na-wa-dv-s-di ni-gv-nv ga-ye-tsi-li-v-ni-s-di a-s-ga-ya A-lo-mi, u-s-ga-nv-tsv tsu-du-go-ta-nv-hi ni-ge-sv-na yi-gi?

052226.english: When the centurion heard that, he went and told the chief captain, saying, Take heed what thou doest: for this man is a Roman.
052226.cherokee: ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈᏃ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏛᎦᏅ, ᏭᏃᏁᎸᎩ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᏗᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ, ᎯᎠ ᏫᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎮᏯᏔᎮᏍᏗ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏅᏔᏛᏁᎵ; ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᎶᎻ.
052226.phonetic: A-s-go-hi-tsu-qui-no di-ti-ni-do-hi, na-s-gi u-dv-ga-nv, wu-no-ne-lv-gi nu-gv-wi-yu-sv di-da-ti-ni-do-hi, hi-a wi-nu-we-sv-gi; He-ya-ta-he-s-di hi-a tsi-nv-ta-dv-ne-li; hi-a-ye-no a-s-ga-ya A-lo-mi.

052227.english: Then the chief captain came, and said unto him, Tell me, art thou a Roman? He said, Yea.
052227.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᏗᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎤᎷᏨ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏍᎩᏃᎲᏏ, ᎯᎶᎻᏍᎪ ᏂᎯ?  ᎥᎥ, ᎤᏛᏅᎩ.
052227.phonetic: Hna-quo-no nu-gv-wi-yu-sv di-da-ti-ni-do-hi u-lu-tsv, hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; S-gi-no-hv-si, Hi-lo-mi-s-go ni-hi?  V-v, u-dv-nv-gi.

052228.english: And the chief captain answered, With a great sum obtained I this freedom. And Paul said, But I was free born.
052228.cherokee: ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒᏃ ᏗᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎤᏤᏘ ᎠᏆᎫᏴᏛ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏆᏓᏤᎵᎦᏯ ᎨᏒᎢ.  ᏉᎳᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎠᏴᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᏆᏓᏤᎵᎦᏯ ᎠᏆᏕᏅᎩ.
052228.phonetic: Nu-gv-wi-yu-sv-no di-da-ti-ni-do-hi hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; u-tse-ti a-qua-gu-yv-dv hi-a na-s-gi a-qua-da-tse-li-ga-ya ge-sv-i.  Quo-la-no hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; A-yv-s-gi-ni a-qua-da-tse-li-ga-ya a-qua-de-nv-gi.

052229.english: Then straightway they departed from him which should have examined him: and the chief captain also was afraid, after he knew that he was a Roman, and because he had bound him.
052229.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎬᏩᏓᏅᎡᎸᎩ ᎬᏩᎪᎵᏰᏍᎩ; ᎾᏍᏉᏃ ᏄᎬᏫᏴᏒ ᏗᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎠᏍᎦᎢᎲᎩ, ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎠᎶᎻ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎸᎸᎢ.
052229.phonetic: Hna-quo-no gi-la-quo i-yv-dv gv-wa-da-nv-e-lv-gi gv-wa-go-li-ye-s-gi; na-s-quo-no nu-gv-wi-yv-sv di-da-ti-ni-do-hi a-s-ga-i-hv-gi, u-do-le-ho-sv A-lo-mi ge-sv-i, a-le nv-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-gi na-s-gi u-lv-lv-i.

052230.english: On the morrow, because he would have known the certainty wherefore he was accused of the Jews, he loosed him from his bands, and commanded the chief priests and all their council to appear, and brought Paul down, and set him before them.
052230.cherokee: ᎤᎩᏨᏛᏃ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎬ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎬᏭᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᎤᎸᏒᎲᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᏨᎩ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏗᏂᎳᏫᎩ, ᎤᎾᏓᏟᏐᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏘᏃᎸᎩ ᏉᎳ, ᎠᏂᏅ ᎢᎬᏱᏢ ᎤᏪᎧᏅᎩ.
052230.phonetic: U-gi-tsv-dv-no u-du-li-s-gv i-yu-s-di du-yu-go-dv u-do-le-ho-hi-s-di-yi nv-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv A-ni-tsu-si gv-wu-hi-s-di-s-gv-i, u-lv-sv-hv-gi, a-le u-ne-tsv-gi nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv-a-ne-lo-hi a-le ni-ga-dv di-ni-la-wi-gi, u-na-da-tli-so-di-yi, a-le u-ti-no-lv-gi Quo-la, a-ni-nv-i-gv-yi-tlv u-we-ka-nv-gi.

052301.english: And Paul, earnestly beholding the council, said, Men and brethren, I have lived in all good conscience before God until this day.
052301.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᎤᏯᏅᏒᎯ ᏚᎧᎿᎭᏅ ᏗᏂᎳᏫᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ ᎢᏓᏓᏅᏟ, ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ ᎢᏯᏍᏗ, ᎠᏆᏓᏅᏙ ᎠᏆᏎᎮᎲ ᏓᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲᎩ ᏂᎦᎥ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎦᏔᎲᎢ.
052301.phonetic: Quo-la-no u-ya-nv-sv-hi du-ka-hna-nv di-ni-la-wi-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; I-tsi-s-ga-ya i-da-da-nv-tli, go-hi i-ga i-ya-s-di, a-qua-da-nv-do a-qua-se-he-hv da-gi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv-gi ni-ga-v du-yu-go-dv ge-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi a-ga-ta-hv-i.

052302.english: And the high priest Ananias commanded them that stood by him to smite him on the mouth.
052302.cherokee: ᎡᏂᎾᏯᏃ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎶᎯ ᏚᏂᏤᎸᎩ ᎦᏙᎬ ᎾᎥ ᎠᏂᏙᎾᎢ ᎠᎰᎵ ᎬᏩᏂᏍᏗᏱ.
052302.phonetic: E-ni-na-ya-no nu-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--ge-lo-hi du-ni-tse-lv-gi ga-do-gv na-v a-ni-do-na-i a-ho-li gv-wa-ni-s-di-yi.

052303.english: Then said Paul unto him, God shall smite thee, thou whited wall: for sittest thou to judge me after the law, and commandest me to be smitten contrary to the law?
052303.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏓᏨᏂᎵ ᏂᎯ, ᎠᏐᏱ ᎤᏁᎬ ᎢᎬᏁᎸᎯ; ᏦᎳᏰᏃ ᎨᎵ ᏗᏍᏇᎪᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩᏬ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎬᎢ?  ᏥᎪᏃ ᎢᎯᏁᎦ ᎥᏋᏂᏍᏗᏱ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏙᎯᎦᏘᎴᎦ?
052303.phonetic: Quo-la-no hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; U-ne-la-nv-hi da-tsv-ni-li ni-hi, a-so-yi u-ne-gv i-gv-ne-lv-hi; tso-la-ye-no ge-li di-s-que-go-da-ne-di-yi na-s-gi-wo di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di ni-ga-we-s-gv-i?  Tsi-go-no i-hi-ne-ga v-quv-ni-s-di-yi di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di do-hi-ga-ti-le-ga?

052304.english: And they that stood by said, Revilest thou God's high priest?
052304.cherokee: ᎾᎥᏃ ᎠᏂᏙᎾᎢ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᏥᎪ ᎦᎯᏐᏢᏗᎭ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎶᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ?
052304.phonetic: Na-v-no a-ni-do-na-i hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; tsi-go ga-hi-so-tlv-di-ha nu-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--ge-lo-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga?

052305.english: Then said Paul, I wist not, brethren, that he was the high priest: for it is written, Thou shalt not speak evil of the ruler of thy people.
052305.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎢᏓᏓᏅᏟ, ᎥᏝ ᏱᎨᎵᏍᎨ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎶᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᏥᏂᎬᏅ ᏥᎪᏪᎳ; "ᏞᏍᏗ ᎤᏐᏅ ᏱᏁᏤᎮᏍᏗ ᏣᎦᏁᎶᏗ ᏗᏣᏤᎵ ᏴᏫ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ."
052305.phonetic: Quo-la-no hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; I-da-da-nv-tli, v-tla yi-ge-li-s-ge nu-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--ge-lo-hi ge-sv-i; hi-a-ye-no tsi-ni-gv-nv tsi-go-we-la; "Tle-s-di u-so-nv yi-ne-tse-he-s-di tsa-ga-ne-lo-di di-tsa-tse-li yv-wi u-na-tse-li-ga."

052306.english: But when Paul perceived that the one part were Sadducees, and the other Pharisees, he cried out in the council, Men and brethren, I am a Pharisee, the son of a Pharisee: of the hope and resurrection of the dead I am called in question.
052306.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎢᎦᏛ ᎠᏂᏌᏚᏏ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ, ᎤᏪᎷᏅᎩ ᏕᎦᎳᏫᎥᎢ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ ᎢᏓᏓᏅᏟ, ᎠᏴ ᏥᏆᎵᏏ, ᎠᏆᎵᏏ ᎤᏪᏥ; ᎤᏚᎩ ᎬᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏲᎱᏒ ᏗᎴᎯᏐᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ ᎠᏆᎵᏱᎵᏕᎭ.
052306.phonetic: Quo-la-no u-do-le-ho-sv i-ga-dv A-ni-sa-du-si ge-sv-i, i-ga-dv-no A-ni-qua-li-si, u-we-lu-nv-gi de-ga-la-wi-v-i hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; I-tsi-s-ga-ya i-da-da-nv-tli, a-yv Tsi-qua-li-si, A-qua-li-si u-we-tsi; u-du-gi gv-di ge-sv a-le a-yo-hu-sv di-le-hi-so-di ge-sv nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha a-qua-li-yi-li-de-ha.

052307.english: And when he had so said, there arose a dissension between the Pharisees and the Sadducees: and the multitude was divided.
052307.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏪᏒ, ᎤᏂᏲᏠᎯᏎᎸᎩ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏌᏚᏏ, ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᎨᏒ ᏔᎵ ᏄᎾᏓᏛᎩ.
052307.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nu-we-sv, u-ni-yo-tlo-hi-se-lv-gi A-ni-qua-li-si a-le A-ni-sa-du-si, u-ni-tsa-ti-no ge-sv ta-li nu-na-da-dv-gi.

052308.english: For the Sadducees say that there is no resurrection, neither angel, nor spirit: but the Pharisees confess both.
052308.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏌᏚᏏᏰᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏎᎪᎢ; ᎥᏝ ᏗᎴᎯᏐᏗ ᏱᎩ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᏰᎭ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ; ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏᏍᎩᏂ ᎢᏧᎳ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎦ.
052308.phonetic: A-ni-sa-du-si-ye-no hi-a na-ni-we-se-go-i; V-tla di-le-hi-so-di yi-gi, v-tla a-le ye-ha di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi a-le a-da-nv-do; A-ni-qua-li-si-s-gi-ni i-tsu-la a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-ga.

052309.english: And there arose a great cry: and the scribes that were of the Pharisees' part arose, and strove, saying, We find no evil in this man: but if a spirit or an angel hath spoken to him, let us not fight against God.
052309.cherokee: ᎤᏣᏘᏃ ᎤᏁᎷᏅᎩ; ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎤᎾᎵᎪᏒ ᎤᎾᎵᎪᎯ ᏚᎾᎴᏅ ᎤᎾᏗᏒᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏲ ᏲᏥᏩᏛᎡᎭ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ, ᎢᏳᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎤᏁᏤᎸᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏤᏓᏟᏴᎡᎸᎩ.
052309.phonetic: U-tsa-ti-no u-ne-lu-nv-gi; di-no-we-li-s-gi-no A-ni-qua-li-si u-na-li-go-sv u-na-li-go-hi du-na-le-nv u-na-di-sv-gi, hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; V-tla go-hu-s-di u-yo yo-tsi-wa-dv-e-ha hi-a a-s-ga-ya, i-yu-s-gi-ni a-da-nv-do a-le di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi u-ne-tse-lv-hi ge-se-s-di, tle-s-di U-ne-la-nv-hi tse-da-tli-yv-e-lv-gi.

052310.english: And when there arose a great dissension, the chief captain, fearing lest Paul should have been pulled in pieces of them, commanded the soldiers to go down, and to take him by force from among them, and to bring him into the castle.
052310.cherokee: ᎤᏣᏘᏃ ᎤᏂᏲᏠᎯᏎᎸ, ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᏗᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎠᏍᎦᎢᎲᎢ ᏉᎳ ᏱᎬᏪᏓᎦᎸᎥᎦ ᎡᎵᏍᎬᎢ, ᏚᏁᏤᎸᎩ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᏫᏚᎾᏑᎦᎸᏙᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏴᏔᏂᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏗᏐᏴᎢ.
052310.phonetic: U-tsa-ti-no u-ni-yo-tlo-hi-se-lv, nu-gv-wi-yu-sv di-da-ti-ni-do-hi a-s-ga-i-hv-i Quo-la yi-gv-we-da-ga-lv-v-ga e-li-s-gv-i, du-ne-tse-lv-gi a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi u-ne-nv-s-di-yi a-le wi-du-na-su-ga-lv-do-di-yi, a-le u-ni-yv-ta-ni-hi-s-di-yi di-so-yv-i.

052311.english: And the night following the Lord stood by him, and said, Be of good cheer, Paul: for as thou hast testified of me in Jerusalem, so must thou bear witness also at Rome.
052311.cherokee: ᎾᎯᏳᏃ ᏒᏃᏱ ᏫᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎾᎥ ᎤᎴᏁᎴᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎤᎦᎵᏍᏗ ᏣᏓᏅᏖᏍᏗ, ᏉᎳ, ᎬᏂᎨᏒᏰᏃ ᏥᏂᏍᏋᏁᎸ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ, ᎾᏍᏉ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏅᏓᏍᏋᏁᎵ ᏣᏂ.
052311.phonetic: Na-hi-yu-no sv-no-yi wi-nu-li-s-ta-nv, U-gv-wi-yu-hi na-v u-le-ne-le-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i; U-ga-li-s-di tsa-da-nv-te-s-di, Quo-la, gv-ni-ge-sv-ye-no tsi-ni-s-quv-ne-lv Tsi-lu-si-li-mi, na-s-quo gv-ni-ge-sv nv-da-s-quv-ne-li Tsa-ni.

052312.english: And when it was day, certain of the Jews banded together, and bound themselves under a curse, saying that they would neither eat nor drink till they had killed Paul.
052312.cherokee: ᏑᎾᎴᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᎾᎵᎪᏁᎢ, ᎤᎾᏎᎵᏔᏁ ᏚᎾᏓᏁᏤᎴ ᎨᏥᏍᎩᏅᏗᏍᏗᏱ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏴᎨᏗᎦ ᎠᎴ ᏴᎨᏓᏗᏔ, ᎬᏂ ᏉᎳ ᎡᏗᎸᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
052312.phonetic: Su-na-le-no nu-li-s-ta-nv gi-lo i-yu-na-s-di A-ni-tsu-si u-na-li-go-ne-i, u-na-se-li-ta-ne du-na-da-ne-tse-le ge-tsi-s-gi-nv-di-s-di-yi, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; V-tla go-hu-s-di yv-ge-di-ga a-le yv-ge-da-di-ta, gv-ni Quo-la e-di-lv-hi ge-se-s-di.

052313.english: And they were more than forty which had made this conspiracy.

052314.english: And they came to the chief priests and elders, and said, We have bound ourselves under a great curse, that we will eat nothing until we have slain Paul.
052314.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᎳᏫᎩ, ᏚᏂᎷᏤᎸᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎣᎦᏎᎵᏔᏅ ᏙᎦᏓᏁᏤᎸ ᎣᎩᏍᏛᏗᏍᏗᏱ, ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎣᎦᏅᏍᎦᎶᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎣᎦᏚᎩᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎬᏂ ᏉᎳ ᎣᏥᎸᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
052314.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv a-ne-lo-hi a-le di-ni-la-wi-gi, du-ni-lu-tse-lv-gi hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; O-ga-se-li-ta-nv do-ga-da-ne-tse-lv o-gi-s-dv-di-s-di-yi, go-hu-s-di o-ga-nv-s-ga-lo-di-yi a-le o-ga-du-gi-s-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na gv-ni Quo-la o-tsi-lv-hi ge-se-s-di.

052315.english: Now therefore ye with the council signify to the chief captain that he bring him down unto you to morrow, as though ye would inquire something more perfectly concerning him: and we, or ever he come near, are ready to kill him.
052315.cherokee: Ꭷ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏂᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᎳᏫᎩ ᎢᏣᎵᎪᎲᎦ, ᎡᏥᏔᏂᏲᏏ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᏗᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ, ᎤᎩᏨᏅ ᎢᏣᏘᏃᎮᏗᏱ, ᎤᏟ ᎢᎦᎢ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎢᏣᏛᏛᏗᏱ ᏥᏣᏚᎵᏍᎪ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎫᎢᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᏴᏃ ᎠᏏᏉ ᎾᎥ ᏂᎦᎷᎬᎾ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎣᎦᏛᏅᎢᏍᏗ ᎣᏥᎢᏍᏗᏱ.
052315.phonetic: Ka na-s-gi i-yu-s-di ni-hi a-le di-ni-la-wi-gi i-tsa-li-go-hv-ga, e-tsi-ta-ni-yo-si nu-gv-wi-yu-sv di-da-ti-ni-do-hi, u-gi-tsv-nv i-tsa-ti-no-he-di-yi, u-tli i-ga-i go-hu-s-di i-tsa-dv-dv-di-yi tsi-tsa-du-li-s-go na-s-gi-ya-i hi-a na-s-gi a-gu-i-s-di-s-gv-i, a-yv-no a-si-quo na-v ni-ga-lu-gv-na ge-se-s-di, o-ga-dv-nv-i-s-di o-tsi-i-s-di-yi.

052316.english: And when Paul's sister's son heard of their lying in wait, he went and entered into the castle, and told Paul.
052316.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᎤᏙ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎤᏛᎦᏅ ᎤᏂᎭᎷᎬᎢ, ᎤᏬᏅᏒᎩ, ᏗᏐᏴ ᏭᏴᎸᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏭᏃᏁᎸᎩ ᏉᎳ.
052316.phonetic: Quo-la-no u-do u-we-tsi u-dv-ga-nv u-ni-ha-lu-gv-i, u-wo-nv-sv-gi, di-so-yv wu-yv-lv-gi, a-le wu-no-ne-lv-gi Quo-la.

052317.english: Then Paul called one of the centurions unto him, and said, Bring this young man unto the chief captain: for he hath a certain thing to tell him.
052317.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᏭᏯᏅᎲ ᎠᏏᏴᏫ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎯᎠ ᎠᏫᏅ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᏗᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᏄᏬᎸ ᏫᏯᏘᏅᏍᏓ, ᎪᎱᏍᏗᏰᏃ ᎤᎭ ᎤᏃᏁᏗ.
052317.phonetic: Quo-la-no wu-ya-nv-hv a-si-yv-wi a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui di-ti-ni-do-hi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Hi-a a-wi-nv nu-gv-wi-yu-sv di-da-ti-ni-do-hi nu-wo-lv wi-ya-ti-nv-s-da, go-hu-s-di-ye-no u-ha u-no-ne-di.

052318.english: So he took him, and brought him to the chief captain, and said, Paul the prisoner called me unto him, and prayed me to bring this young man unto thee, who hath something to say unto thee.
052318.cherokee: ᎰᏩᏃ ᎤᏯᏅᎲ, ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᏗᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᏭᏘᏃᎮᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏉᎳ ᎠᏴᎩ ᏩᎩᏯᏅᎲᎩ, ᎠᎩᏔᏲᏎᎸᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏫᏅ ᎬᏯᏘᏃᎮᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᎭ ᏣᏃᏁᏗ.
052318.phonetic: Ho-wa-no u-ya-nv-hv, nu-gv-wi-yu-sv di-da-ti-ni-do-hi wu-ti-no-he-lv-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Quo-la a-yv-gi wa-gi-ya-nv-hv-gi, a-gi-ta-yo-se-lv-gi hi-a a-wi-nv gv-ya-ti-no-he-di-yi, na-s-gi go-hu-s-di u-ha tsa-no-ne-di.

052319.english: Then the chief captain took him by the hand, and went with him aside privately, and asked him, What is that thou hast to tell me?
052319.cherokee: ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒᏃ ᏗᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎤᏬᏯᏁᏒ, ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᏕᎵᏒ ᏭᏘᏅᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᎤᏛᏛᏅᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎦᏙ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏍᎩᏃᏁᏗ ᏥᎩ?
052319.phonetic: Nu-gv-wi-yu-sv-no di-da-ti-ni-do-hi u-wo-ya-ne-sv, i-yv-dv u-de-li-sv wu-ti-nv-s-ta-nv-gi, u-dv-dv-nv-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Ga-do na-s-gi na s-gi-no-ne-di tsi-gi?

052320.english: And he said, The Jews have agreed to desire thee that thou wouldest bring down Paul to morrow into the council, as though they would inquire somewhat of him more perfectly.
052320.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏖᎸ ᎨᏣᏔᏲᏎᏗᏱ ᎤᎩᏨᏅ ᏕᎦᎳᏫᎥ ᏫᏯᏘᏃᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏉᎳ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏟ ᎢᎦᎢ ᏨᏗᎾᏛᏛᏂᏐᎢ.
052320.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-we-sv-gi; A-ni-tsu-si u-na-da-nv-te-lv ge-tsa-ta-yo-se-di-yi u-gi-tsv-nv de-ga-la-wi-v wi-ya-ti-no-hi-s-di-yi Quo-la, na-s-gi-ya go-hu-s-di u-tli i-ga-i tsv-di-na-dv-dv-ni-so-i.

052321.english: But do not thou yield unto them: for there lie in wait for him of them more than forty men, which have bound themselves with an oath, that they will neither eat nor drink till they have killed him: and now are they ready, looking for a promise from thee.
052321.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏙᎯᏳᏅᎩ; ᏅᎦᏍᎪᎯᏰᏃ ᎤᏗᏢᏍᏙᏗ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᎬᏩᎭᎷᎦ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏎᎵᏔᏅᎯ ᏚᎾᏓᏁᏤᎸ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏗᏔᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎬᏂ ᎤᏂᎸᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ; ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᎾᏛᏅᎢᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᎦᏘᏴ ᎤᎾᏛᎪᏗᏱ ᏣᏚᎢᏍᏔᏅᎢ.
052321.phonetic: A-se-no tle-s-di do-hi-yu-nv-gi; nv-ga-s-go-hi-ye-no u-di-tlv-s-do-di i-ya-ni-dv a-ni-s-ga-ya gv-wa-ha-lu-ga, na-s-gi u-na-se-li-ta-nv-hi du-na-da-ne-tse-lv u-na-li-s-da-yv-di-yi a-le u-na-di-ta-s-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na, gv-ni u-ni-lv-hi ge-se-s-di; hna-quo-no u-na-dv-nv-i-s-di a-ni-ga-ti-yv u-na-dv-go-di-yi tsa-du-i-s-ta-nv-i.

052322.english: So the chief captain then let the young man depart, and charged him, See thou tell no man that thou hast shewed these things to me.
052322.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᏗᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎤᏪᎧᏅᎩ ᎠᏫᏅ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏁᏤᎸᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᎯᏃᏁᎸ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᏍᏋᏁᎸᎢ.
052322.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nu-gv-wi-yu-sv di-da-ti-ni-do-hi u-we-ka-nv-gi a-wi-nv a-le a-ne-tse-lv-gi hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tle-s-di gi-lo hi-no-ne-lv na-s-gi hi-a gv-ni-ge-sv ni-s-quv-ne-lv-i.

052323.english: And he called unto him two centurions, saying, Make ready two hundred soldiers to go to Caesarea, and horsemen threescore and ten, and spearmen two hundred, at the third hour of the night;
052323.cherokee: ᏫᏚᏯᏅᎲᏃ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᏗᎾᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏗᏍᏓᏛᏅᎢᏍᏓ ᏔᎵᏧᏈ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᎵᏆᏍᎪᎯ ᏧᎾᎩᎸᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏓᏘᏍᏗ ᏗᏂᏂᏙᎯ ᏔᎵᏧᏈ, ᏏᏌᎵᏱ ᎠᏁᏒᎭ ᏐᎣᏁᎵᏁ ᎢᏳᏟᎶᏛ ᏒᏃᏱ;
052323.phonetic: Wi-du-ya-nv-hv-no a-ni-ta-li a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui di-na-da-ti-ni-do-hi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Di-s-da-dv-nv-i-s-da ta-li-tsu-qui a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi, a-le ga-li-qua-s-go-hi tsu-na-gi-lv-di, a-le di-da-ti-s-di di-ni-ni-do-hi ta-li-tsu-qui, Si-sa-li-yi a-ne-sv-ha so-o-ne-li-ne i-yu-tli-lo-dv sv-no-yi;

052324.english: And provide them beasts, that they may set Paul on, and bring him safe unto Felix the governor.
052324.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏗᎩᎸᏙᏗ ᏗᏍᏓᏛᏅᎢᏍᏓ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎾᎩᎸᏔᏅᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᏩᎾᏘᏃᎮᎸᎭ ᏈᎵᏏ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ.
052324.phonetic: A-le di-gi-lv-do-di di-s-da-dv-nv-i-s-da na-s-gi Quo-la a-na-gi-lv-ta-nv-ha, a-le nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv wa-na-ti-no-he-lv-ha Qui-li-si u-gv-wi-yu-hi.

052325.english: And he wrote a letter after this manner:

052326.english: Claudius Lysias unto the most excellent governor Felix sendeth greeting.
052326.cherokee: "ᎠᏴ ᏠᏗᏯ-ᎵᏏᏯ ᏫᎬᏲᎵᎦ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎰᏍᏛ ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏈᎵᏏ.
052326.phonetic: "A-yv Tlo-di-ya--Li-si-ya wi-gv-yo-li-ga u-tsa-ti ho-s-dv tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi Qui-li-si.

052327.english: This man was taken of the Jews, and should have been killed of them: then came I with an army, and rescued him, having understood that he was a Roman.
052327.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎬᏩᏂᏴᎲᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᎬᏩᎵᏒᎩ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᏕᎦᏘᏅᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᏍᏕᎸᎲᎩ, ᎠᏆᏛᎦᏅᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎶᎻ ᎨᏒᎢ.
052327.phonetic: Hi-a a-s-ga-ya A-ni-tsu-si gv-wa-ni-yv-hv-gi, a-le da-gv-wa-li-sv-gi, hna-quo a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi de-ga-ti-nv-sv-gi, a-le tsi-s-de-lv-hv-gi, a-qua-dv-ga-nv-hi ge-sv A-lo-mi ge-sv-i.

052328.english: And when I would have known the cause wherefore they accused him, I brought him forth into their council:
052328.cherokee: ᎠᏆᏚᎸᎲᏃ ᎠᏆᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᎬᏬᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᏥᏯᏘᏃᎸᎩ ᏓᏂᎳᏫᎥᎢ.
052328.phonetic: A-qua-du-lv-hv-no a-qua-do-le-ho-hi-s-di-yi nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv gv-wo-hi-s-di-s-gv-i, tsi-ya-ti-no-lv-gi da-ni-la-wi-v-i.

052329.english: Whom I perceived to be accused of questions of their law, but to have nothing laid to his charge worthy of death or of bonds.
052329.cherokee: ᎠᏆᏙᎴᎰᏒᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗᏉ ᎧᏃᎮᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᎫᎢᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ ᎬᏩᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎦᏰᎦᎸᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎾᎫᎢᏍᏛᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
052329.phonetic: A-qua-do-le-ho-sv-no na-s-gi tsu-ni-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di-quo ka-no-he-di ge-sv nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv a-gu-i-s-di-s-gv-i gv-wa-yo-hu-hi-s-di-no a-le ga-ye-ga-lv-s-di ge-sv go-hu-s-di na-gu-i-s-dv-na ge-sv-i.

052330.english: And when it was told me how that the Jews laid wait for the man, I sent straightway to thee, and gave commandment to his accusers also to say before thee what they had against him. Farewell.
052330.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎥᎩᏃᏁᎸ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᏂᎭᎷᎬ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ, ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎠᏆᏓᏅᏒ ᏗᏦᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎦᏥᏁᏤᎸ ᎬᏭᎯᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᎯᎦᏔᎲ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᏗᏱ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᏄᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ.  ᏙᎯᏱ ᏂᏣᏛᎿᎭᏕᎨᏍᏗ."
052330.phonetic: A-le v-gi-no-ne-lv a-ni-tsu-si u-ni-ha-lu-gv na-s-gi a-s-ga-ya, gi-la-quo i-yv-dv a-qua-da-nv-sv di-tso-lv-i, a-le na-s-quo ga-tsi-ne-tse-lv gv-wu-hi-s-di-s-gi hi-ga-ta-hv u-ni-no-he-di-yi nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv a-nu-hi-s-di-s-gv-i.  Do-hi-yi ni-tsa-dv-hna-de-ge-s-di."

052331.english: Then the soldiers, as it was commanded them, took Paul, and brought him by night to Antipatris.
052331.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩᏃ, ᏂᎨᏥᏪᏎᎸ ᎨᏥᏁᏤᎸ ᏄᎾᏛᏁᎸ, ᎤᏂᏯᏅᎲᎩ ᏉᎳ, ᎠᎴ ᏒᏃᏱ ᏭᏂᎶᏒ, ᎥᏗᏇᏗ ᏭᎾᏘᏃᎸᎩ.
052331.phonetic: A-ni-ya-wi-s-gi-no, ni-ge-tsi-we-se-lv ge-tsi-ne-tse-lv nu-na-dv-ne-lv, u-ni-ya-nv-hv-gi Quo-la, a-le sv-no-yi wu-ni-lo-sv, V-di-que-di wu-na-ti-no-lv-gi.

052332.english: On the morrow they left the horsemen to go with him, and returned to the castle:
052332.cherokee: ᎤᎩᏨᏛᏃ ᏧᎾᎩᎸᏗ ᎤᎾᏘᏅᏍᏗ ᏄᏅᏁᎸ, ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏨᏒᎩ, ᏗᏐᏴ ᏭᏂᎶᏒᎩ.
052332.phonetic: U-gi-tsv-dv-no tsu-na-gi-lv-di u-na-ti-nv-s-di nu-nv-ne-lv, a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi u-na-tsv-sv-gi, di-so-yv wu-ni-lo-sv-gi.

052333.english: Who, when they came to Caesarea, and delivered the epistle to the governor, presented Paul also before him.
052333.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏧᎾᎩᎸᏗ ᏏᏌᎵᏱ ᏭᏂᎷᏨ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎪᏪᎵ ᎤᏂᏅᏁᎸ, ᎾᏍᏉ ᏉᎳ ᏚᏂᏲᎯᏎᎸᎩ.
052333.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no tsu-na-gi-lv-di Si-sa-li-yi wu-ni-lu-tsv, a-le u-gv-wi-yu-hi go-we-li u-ni-nv-ne-lv, na-s-quo Quo-la du-ni-yo-hi-se-lv-gi.

052334.english: And when the governor had read the letter, he asked of what province he was. And when he understood that he was of Cilicia;
052334.cherokee: ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏃ ᎤᎪᎵᏰᎥ, ᎤᏛᏛᏅᎩ ᎾᎿᎭᏍᎦᏚᎩ ᎡᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᏛᎦᏅᏃ ᏏᎵᏏᏱ ᎡᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ,
052334.phonetic: U-gv-wi-yu-hi-no u-go-li-ye-v, u-dv-dv-nv-gi na-hna s-ga-du-gi e-hi ge-sv-i, u-dv-ga-nv-no Si-li-si-yi e-hi ge-sv-i,

052335.english: I will hear thee, said he, when thine accusers are also come. And he commanded him to be kept in Herod's judgment hall.
052335.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏓᎬᏯᏛᎦᏁᎵ ᎨᏧᎯᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏂᎷᏨᎭ.  ᎤᏁᏨᎩᏃ ᎠᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗᏱ ᎡᎶᏛ ᎤᏤᎵ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎠᏓᏁᎸᎢ.
052335.phonetic: Hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Da-gv-ya-dv-ga-ne-li ge-tsu-hi-s-di-s-gi na-s-quo a-ni-lu-tsv-ha.  U-ne-tsv-gi-no a-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di-yi E-lo-dv u-tse-li di-gu-go-do-di-yi a-da-ne-lv-i.

052401.english: And after five days Ananias the high priest descended with the elders, and with a certain orator named Tertullus, who informed the governor against Paul.
052401.cherokee: ᎯᏍᎩᏃ ᏫᏄᏒᎳ ᎡᏂᎾᏯ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎶᎯ ᎤᎷᏨᎩ, ᎠᏁᎲ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏗᏘᏲᎯᎯ, ᏔᏓᎳ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᏃᏁᎸᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎠᏄᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᏉᎳ.
052401.phonetic: Hi-s-gi-no wi-nu-sv-la E-ni-na-ya nu-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--ge-lo-hi u-lu-tsv-gi, a-ne-hv di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di, a-le gi-lo i-yu-s-di di-ti-yo-hi-hi, Ta-da-la tsu-do-i-dv, na-s-gi u-ni-no-ne-lv-gi u-gv-wi-yu-hi, a-nu-hi-s-di-s-gv Quo-la.

052402.english: And when he was called forth, Tertullus began to accuse him, saying, Seeing that by thee we enjoy great quietness, and that very worthy deeds are done unto this nation by thy providence,
052402.cherokee: ᏩᏥᏯᏅᎲᏃ ᏔᏓᎳ ᎤᎴᏅᎲᎩ ᎤᏬᎯᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; "ᏂᎯ ᎢᏣᏂᏌᏅᎯ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᏦᏤᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏥᏄᎾᎵᏍᏓᏁᎭ ᏴᏫ ᎣᎦᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᏣᎦᏌᏯᏍᏗᏳ ᎨᏒ ᏥᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ,
052402.phonetic: Wa-tsi-ya-nv-hv-no Ta-da-la u-le-nv-hv-gi u-wo-hi-s-ta-nv-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; "Ni-hi i-tsa-ni-sa-nv-hi u-tsa-ti nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv tso-tse-ha, a-le o-s-dv tsi-nu-na-li-s-da-ne-ha yv-wi o-ga-tse-li-go-hi tsa-ga-sa-ya-s-di-yu ge-sv tsi-nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha,

052403.english: We accept it always, and in all places, most noble Felix, with all thankfulness.
052403.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎰᏍᏛ ᏈᎵᏏ, ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎠᎵᎮᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎣᏣᏓᏂᎸᎪᎢ.
052403.phonetic: na-s-gi i-yu-s-di, ho-s-dv qui-li-si, ni-go-hi-lv a-le ni-gv-na-dv ni-ga-v a-li-he-li-s-di ge-sv o-tsa-da-ni-lv-go-i.

052404.english: Notwithstanding, that I be not further tedious unto thee, I pray thee that thou wouldest hear us of thy clemency a few words.
052404.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏏ ᎤᏟ ᎢᎦᎢ ᏂᏗᎬᏯᏪᎢᏍᏔᏅᎾ, ᎬᏔᏲᏎᎭ ᏣᏓᏅᏘᏳ ᎨᏒ ᏨᏙᏗᏱ ᏍᎩᏯᏛᎦᏁᏗᏱ ᎢᎸᏍᎩ ᎢᎧᏁᏨᎯ.
052404.phonetic: A-se-no a-si u-tli i-ga-i ni-di-gv-ya-we-i-s-ta-nv-na, gv-ta-yo-se-ha tsa-da-nv-ti-yu ge-sv tsv-do-di-yi s-gi-ya-dv-ga-ne-di-yi i-lv-s-gi i-ka-ne-tsv-hi.

052405.english: For we have found this man a pestilent fellow, and a mover of sedition among all the Jews throughout the world, and a ringleader of the sect of the Nazarenes:
052405.cherokee: ᎣᎦᏙᎴᎰᏒᏰᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏣᏔᏅᎯ ᎤᏕᏯᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏂᎳᏗᏍᎩ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎠᏁᎲ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᎡᎶᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᏗᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎤᎾᎵᎪᏒ ᎾᏎᎵᏂ ᏧᎾᏙᎢᏛ.
052405.phonetic: O-ga-do-le-ho-sv-ye-no hi-a na-s-gi a-s-ga-ya u-tsa-ta-nv-hi u-de-ya-do-di ge-sv-i, a-le ga-ni-la-di-s-gi tsu-da-le-nv-dv ni-ga-dv A-ni-tsu-si a-ne-hv ni-gv-na-dv e-lo-hi, a-le nu-gv-wi-yu-sv di-da-ti-ni-do-hi u-na-li-go-sv Na-se-li-ni tsu-na-do-i-dv.

052406.english: Who also hath gone about to profane the temple: whom we took, and would have judged according to our law.
052406.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏛᏅ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏐᏢᎢᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎤᏰᎸᏅᎩ.  ᎾᏍᎩ ᎣᏥᏂᏴᎲᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎣᎦᏚᎵᏍᎬᎩ ᏦᎩᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎣᎬᏙᏗᏱ ᎣᏥᏱᎵᏓᏍᏗᏱ.
052406.phonetic: Na-s-gi na-s-quo u-dv-nv di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi u-so-tlv-i-s-do-di-yi u-ye-lv-nv-gi.  Na-s-gi o-tsi-ni-yv-hv-gi a-le o-ga-du-li-s-gv-gi tso-gi-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di o-gv-do-di-yi o-tsi-yi-li-da-s-di-yi.

052407.english: But the chief captain Lysias came upon us, and with great violence took him away out of our hands,
052407.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎵᏏᏯ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᏗᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎤᎷᏨ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎬᏍᎦᎢᏍᏓᎩᏯ ᏙᎦᏑᎦᎸᏔᏅᎩ,
052407.phonetic: A-se-no Li-si-ya nu-gv-wi-yu-sv di-da-ti-ni-do-hi u-lu-tsv u-tsa-ti gv-s-ga-i-s-da-gi-ya do-ga-su-ga-lv-ta-nv-gi,

052408.english: Commanding his accusers to come unto thee: by examining of whom thyself mayest take knowledge of all these things, whereof we accuse him.
052408.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏚᏁᏤᎸᎩ ᎬᏭᎯᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᎨᏣᎷᏤᏗᏱ; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎪᎵᏰᏍᎬᎢ ᏨᏒ ᎨᏣᎦᏙᎥᎯᏍᏗ ᏱᏂᎦᎵᏍᏓ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏦᏧᎢᏍᏗᎭ."
052408.phonetic: A-le du-ne-tse-lv-gi gv-wu-hi-s-di-s-gi ge-tsa-lu-tse-di-yi; na-s-gi-no hi-go-li-ye-s-gv-i tsv-sv ge-tsa-ga-do-v-hi-s-di yi-ni-ga-li-s-da hi-a ni-ga-dv tso-tsu-i-s-di-ha."

052409.english: And the Jews also assented, saying that these things were so.
052409.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏧᏏᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏂᏍᏓᏱᏛᎩ, ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏍᏗ, ᎤᎾᏛᏅᎩ.
052409.phonetic: A-ni-tsu-si-no na-s-quo u-ni-s-da-yi-dv-gi, u-do-hi-yu-hi na-s-gi nu-s-di, u-na-dv-nv-gi.

052410.english: Then Paul, after that the governor had beckoned unto him to speak, answered, Forasmuch as I know that thou hast been of many years a judge unto this nation, I do the more cheerfully answer for myself:
052410.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏉᎳ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᎵᏍᎫᏫᏎᎸ ᎤᏁᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᎸ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; "ᏥᎦᏔᎯᏳ ᏥᎩ ᎢᎸᏍᎩ ᏧᏕᏘᏴᏛ ᏚᎪᏓᏁᎯ ᎢᎬᏩᎵᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎯᎠ ᏑᎾᏓᎴᎩ ᏴᏫ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ ᎤᏟ ᎤᎦᎵᏍᏗᏳ ᎦᏓᏅᏓᏓ, ᎠᏋᏒ ᎦᎵᏍᏕᎵᏍᎬ ᎬᎩᏁᎢᏍᏗ ᏥᏂᎦᎵᏍᏓ.
052410.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Quo-la, u-gv-wi-yu-hi u-li-s-gu-wi-se-lv u-ne-i-s-di-yi u-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-lv, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; "Tsi-ga-ta-hi-yu tsi-gi i-lv-s-gi tsu-de-ti-yv-dv du-go-da-ne-hi i-gv-wa-li-s-ta-nv-hi ge-sv hi-a su-na-da-le-gi yv-wi, na-s-gi nv-da-ga-li-s-do-da u-tli u-ga-li-s-di-yu ga-da-nv-da-da, a-quv-sv ga-li-s-de-li-s-gv gv-gi-ne-i-s-di tsi-ni-ga-li-s-da.

052411.english: Because that thou mayest understand, that there are yet but twelve days since I went up to Jerusalem for to worship.
052411.cherokee: ᎨᏣᎦᏙᎥᎯᏍᏗᏰᏃ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏗᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏏᏉ ᏔᎳᏚᏉ ᏄᏒᎭ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎬᏆᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏅᏒᎯ.
052411.phonetic: Ge-tsa-ga-do-v-hi-s-di-ye-no ni-ga-li-s-di-ha na-s-gi a-si-quo ta-la-du-quo nu-sv-ha Tsi-lu-si-li-mi gv-qua-da-do-li-s-ta-nv-sv-hi.

052412.english: And they neither found me in the temple disputing with any man, neither raising up the people, neither in the synagogues, nor in the city:
052412.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏱᎬᎩᏩᏛᎮ ᎩᎶ ᏲᏍᏓᏗᏒᎯᎮ ᎣᏍᏗᏬᏂᏍᎬᎢ ᎤᏛᏅ-ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏗᏥᏖᎸᎲᏍᎨ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏕᎨᏌᏗᏒᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎢᎸᎯᏢ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ.
052412.phonetic: A-le v-tla yi-gv-gi-wa-dv-he gi-lo yo-s-da-di-sv-hi-he o-s-di-wo-ni-s-gv-i u-dv-nv--di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi, a-le v-tla yi-di-tsi-te-lv-hv-s-ge du-na-da-nv-dv yv-wi di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi de-ge-sa-di-sv-i a-le i-lv-hi-tlv ga-du-hv-i.

052413.english: Neither can they prove the things whereof they now accuse me.
052413.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᏰᎵ ᎬᏩᏃᎯᏳᏙᏗ ᏱᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏥᎬᏇᎯᏍᏗᎭ.
052413.phonetic: V-tla a-le ye-li gv-wa-no-hi-yu-do-di yi-gi hi-a na-s-gi hna-quo tsi-gv-que-hi-s-di-ha.

052414.english: But this I confess unto thee, that after the way which they call heresy, so worship I the God of my fathers, believing all things which are written in the law and in the prophets:
052414.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎬᏃᎲᏏ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏓᏤᎵᏛ ᎤᎾᎵᎪᎯ ᏥᏓᏃᏎᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏥᏍᏓᏩᏗᏒ ᏥᏯᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎲ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ, ᎪᎢᏳᎲᏍᎬᎢ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎪᏪᎸ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᏚᏃᏪᎸᎢ;
052414.phonetic: Hi-a-s-gi-ni-no-nv gv-no-hv-si, na-s-gi u-na-da-tse-li-dv u-na-li-go-hi tsi-da-no-se-ha, na-s-gi ga-tsi-s-da-wa-di-sv tsi-ya-da-do-li-s-da-ne-hv U-ne-la-nv-hi di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge u-na-tse-li-ga, go-i-yu-hv-s-gv-i ni-ga-dv go-we-lv di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di-yi a-le a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi du-no-we-lv-i;

052415.english: And have hope toward God, which they themselves also allow, that there shall be a resurrection of the dead, both of the just and unjust.
052415.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏚᎩ ᏥᏴᎠ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᏧᎾᎴᎯᏐᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏧᏂᏲᎱᏒᎯ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏁᎫᏥᏛ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏅᏒ ᏧᏃᎯᏳᎭ.
052415.phonetic: A-le na-s-quo u-du-gi tsi-yv-a U-ne-la-nv-hi, tsu-na-le-hi-so-di ge-sv tsu-ni-yo-hu-sv-hi u-na-da-nv-ti a-le u-ni-ne-gu-tsi-dv, na-s-gi na-s-quo u-nv-sv tsu-no-hi-yu-ha.

052416.english: And herein do I exercise myself, to have always a conscience void of offence toward God, and toward men.
052416.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏛᏁᎰᎢ ᎦᏟᏂᎬᏁᎰ ᎠᏆᏓᏅᏙᎩ ᎠᏆᏎᎮᎲᎢ ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᏂᏥᏍᎦᏅᏤᎲᎾ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏥᏍᎦᏅᏤᎲᎾ ᏴᏫ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
052416.phonetic: A-le na-s-gi hi-a ni-ga-dv-ne-ho-i ga-tli-ni-gv-ne-ho a-qua-da-nv-do-gi a-qua-se-he-hv-i ni-go-hi-lv ni-tsi-s-ga-nv-tse-hv-na U-ne-la-nv-hi a-le ni-ga-tsi-s-ga-nv-tse-hv-na yv-wi i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi.

052417.english: Now after many years I came to bring alms to my nation, and offerings.
052417.cherokee: ᎢᎸᏍᎩᏃ ᏫᏄᏕᏘᏴᎲ ᏗᏆᏤᎵᎦ ᏴᏫ ᏕᏥᏲᎮᎸᎩ ᎨᏥᏁᎸᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎩᏲᎸᎩ ᎠᏥᎸᎨᎳᏍᏗ.
052417.phonetic: I-lv-s-gi-no wi-nu-de-ti-yv-hv di-qua-tse-li-ga yv-wi de-tsi-yo-he-lv-gi ge-tsi-ne-lv-hi, a-le a-gi-yo-lv-gi a-tsi-lv-ge-la-s-di.

052418.english: Whereupon certain Jews from Asia found me purified in the temple, neither with multitude, nor with tumult.
052418.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏂᎦᏛᏁᎲᎢ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎡᏏᏱ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎬᎩᏩᏛᎲᎩ ᎠᏆᏓᏅᎦᎵᏌᏛ ᎨᏒᎩ ᏥᏯᎥᎩ ᎤᏛᏅᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᎥᏝ ᏱᎨᎳᏗᏙᎮ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎠᏁᏙᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏓᏑᏰᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ;
052418.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no ni-ga-dv-ne-hv-i gi-lo i-yu-na-s-di A-ni-tsu-si E-si-yi a-ne-hi gv-gi-wa-dv-hv-gi a-qua-da-nv-ga-li-sa-dv ge-sv-gi tsi-ya-v-gi u-dv-nv-di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi, v-tla yi-ge-la-di-do-he u-ni-tsa-ti a-ne-do-hv-i, a-le u-na-da-su-ye-dv ge-sv-i;

052419.english: Who ought to have been here before thee, and object, if they had ought against me.
052419.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᏂ ᏦᎸᎢ ᏱᎠᏁᏙᎮᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏳᏂᏃᎮᎴᎢ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏱᎬᏇᎯᏍᏗᎭ.
052419.phonetic: Na-s-gi-s-gi-ni a-ni tso-lv-i yi-a-ne-do-he-i, a-le yu-ni-no-he-le-i, i-yu-no go-hu-s-di yi-gv-que-hi-s-di-ha.

052420.english: Or else let these same here say, if they have found any evil doing in me, while I stood before the council,
052420.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠᏉ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏅᏒ ᏳᏂᏃᎮᎸ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎤᏲ ᏓᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸ ᎤᏂᏩᏛᏛ ᏱᎩ ᏕᎦᎳᏫᎥ ᏥᏙᎬᎢ,
052420.phonetic: A-le hi-a-quo na-s-quo u-nv-sv yu-ni-no-he-lv, i-yu-no u-yo da-gi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv u-ni-wa-dv-dv yi-gi de-ga-la-wi-v tsi-do-gv-i,

052421.english: Except it be for this one voice, that I cried standing among them, Touching the resurrection of the dead I am called in question by you this day.
052421.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᏑᏓᎴᎩ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎠᎩᏁᏨᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᎩᏪᏒ ᎠᏇᎷᏅ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲ ᏥᏙᎬᎢ; ᏗᎴᎯᏐᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏲᎱᏒ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᏍᎩᏱᎵᏙᎭ ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ ᏥᎩ."
052421.phonetic: Hi-a s-gi-ni-no-nv su-da-le-gi u-wa-sv a-gi-ne-tsv-i, hi-a na-gi-we-sv a-que-lu-nv a-ni-ga-ta-hv tsi-do-gv-i; Di-le-hi-so-di ge-sv a-yo-hu-sv nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di s-gi-yi-li-do-ha go-hi i-ga tsi-gi."

052422.english: And when Felix heard these things, having more perfect knowledge of that way, he deferred them, and said, When Lysias the chief captain shall come down, I will know the uttermost of your matter.
052422.cherokee: ᏈᎵᏏᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏛᎦᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏟ ᎢᎦᎢ ᎤᎦᏙᎥᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏅᏅᎢ, ᏚᏬᎯᏕᎸᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎵᏏᏯ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᏗᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎦᎷᏨᎭ ᏓᎦᏛᎦᏃᏂ ᏥᏥᏱᎵᏙᎭ.
052422.phonetic: Qui-li-si-no na-s-gi u-dv-ga-nv, a-le u-tli i-ga-i u-ga-do-v-sv na-s-gi ga-nv-nv-i, du-wo-hi-de-lv-gi hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Li-si-ya nu-gv-wi-yu-sv di-da-ti-ni-do-hi ga-lu-tsv-ha da-ga-dv-ga-no-ni tsi-tsi-yi-li-do-ha.

052423.english: And he commanded a centurion to keep Paul, and to let him have liberty, and that he should forbid none of his acquaintance to minister or come unto him.
052423.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏤᎸᎩᏃ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏛ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᏧᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏓᏍᏗᏱ ᏉᎳ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᏪᏓ ᎤᏪᎵᏎᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᏣᏅᏍᏙᏒᎩ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᎾᎵᎢ ᎤᏍᏕᎸᎯᏓᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏛᎯᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
052423.phonetic: U-ne-tse-lv-gi-no a-s-go-hi-tsu-dv di-ti-ni-do-hi tsu-ka-hna-wa-di-da-s-di-yi Quo-la, a-le u-ne-la-gi we-da u-we-li-se-di-yi, a-le hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; tle-s-di tsa-nv-s-do-sv-gi gi-lo i-yu-s-di u-na-li-i u-s-de-lv-hi-da-s-di-yi a-le u-wa-dv-hi-hi-s-di-yi.

052424.english: And after certain days, when Felix came with his wife Drusilla, which was a Jewess, he sent for Paul, and heard him concerning the faith in Christ.
052424.cherokee: ᎢᎸᏍᎩᏃ ᏫᏄᏒᎸ ᏈᎵᏏ ᎤᎷᏨᎩ, ᎤᏘᏃᎸᎩ ᎤᏓᎵᎢ ᏚᏏᎵ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏧᏏ ᎨᏒᎩ, ᎤᏓᏅᏒᎩ ᏩᏥᏯᏅᏗᏱ ᏉᎳ; ᎤᏛᎦᏁᎸᎩᏃ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ.
052424.phonetic: I-lv-s-gi-no wi-nu-sv-lv Qui-li-si u-lu-tsv-gi, u-ti-no-lv-gi u-da-li-i Du-si-li, na-s-gi A-tsu-si ge-sv-gi, u-da-nv-sv-gi wa-tsi-ya-nv-di-yi Quo-la; u-dv-ga-ne-lv-gi-no Ga-lo-ne-dv go-hi-yu-di ge-sv u-gv-wa-li.

052425.english: And as he reasoned of righteousness, temperance, and judgment to come Felix trembled, and answered, Go thy way for this time; when I have a convenient season, I will call for thee.
052425.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏉᏃ ᎧᏃᎮᏍᎬ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎵᏏᎾᎯᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏧᏭᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎤᎵᏱᎶᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏈᎵᏏ ᎤᏪᎾᏮᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎭᏓᏅᎾ ᎪᎯ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏰᎵ ᎢᎬᏆᏛᏁᏗ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏔᏅᎭ ᏫᎬᏯᏅᎲᎭ.
052425.phonetic: A-si-quo-no ka-no-he-s-gv du-yu-go-dv ge-sv-i, a-le a-li-si-na-hi-s-do-di ge-sv-i, a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi tsu-wu-go-do-di-yi u-li-yi-lo-hi-s-di ge-sv-i, Qui-li-si u-we-na-wv-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Ha-da-nv-na go-hi, i-yu-no ye-li i-gv-qua-dv-ne-di ni-ga-li-s-ta-nv-ha wi-gv-ya-nv-hv-ha.

052426.english: He hoped also that money should have been given him of Paul, that he might loose him: wherefore he sent for him the oftener, and communed with him.
052426.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏚᎩ ᎤᏩᏒᎩ ᏉᎳ ᎠᏕᎸ ᏧᏁᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏧᏲᎯᏍᏗᏱ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏟ ᏯᏃᎩᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏍᎬᎩ ᏩᏯᏂᏍᎬᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎵᏃᎮᏗᏍᎬᎩ.
052426.phonetic: A-le na-s-quo u-du-gi u-wa-sv-gi Quo-la a-de-lv tsu-ne-di-yi, na-s-gi-no tsu-yo-hi-s-di-yi; na-s-gi i-yu-s-di u-tli ya-no-gi-yu a-da-nv-s-gv-gi wa-ya-ni-s-gv-gi, a-le a-li-no-he-di-s-gv-gi.

052427.english: But after two years Porcius Festus came into Felix' room: and Felix, willing to shew the Jews a pleasure, left Paul bound.
052427.cherokee: ᏔᎵᏃ ᏫᏄᏕᎢᏴᎲ ᏈᎵᏏ ᎤᏪᏅᎢ ᏉᏏᏯ ᏇᏍᏓ ᎤᏪᏔᏅᎩ; ᏈᎵᏏᏃ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎬᎢ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏧᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎤᏪᎧᎯᏴᎩ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎦᎸᎢᏛ.
052427.phonetic: Ta-li-no wi-nu-de-i-yv-hv Qui-li-si u-we-nv-i Quo-si-ya Que-s-da u-we-ta-nv-gi; Qui-li-si-no u-du-li-s-gv-i A-ni-tsu-si o-s-dv tsu-da-nv-da-di-s-do-di-yi, u-we-ka-hi-yv-gi Quo-la a-ga-lv-i-dv.

052501.english: Now when Festus was come into the province, after three days he ascended from Caesarea to Jerusalem.
052501.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏇᏍᏓ ᎾᎿᎭᏍᎦᏚᎩ ᎠᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏛ ᎤᎷᏨ, ᏦᎢ ᏫᏄᏒᎸ, ᏏᏌᎵᏱ ᎤᏂᎩᏒᎩ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏭᎶᏒᎩ.
052501.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Que-s-da na-hna s-ga-du-gi a-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-dv u-lu-tsv, tso-i wi-nu-sv-lv, Si-sa-li-yi u-ni-gi-sv-gi Tsi-lu-si-li-mi wu-lo-sv-gi.

052502.english: Then the high priest and the chief of the Jews informed him against Paul, and besought him,
052502.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎶᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏧᎾᏤᎦ ᎬᏩᏃᎮᎮᎸᎩ ᎠᏄᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏗᏰᏔᏅᎩ,
052502.phonetic: Hna-quo-no nu-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--ge-lo-hi a-le u-ni-gv-wi-yu-hi A-ni-tsu-si tsu-na-tse-ga gv-wa-no-he-he-lv-gi a-nu-hi-s-di-s-gv Quo-la a-le gv-wa-s-di-ye-ta-nv-gi,

052503.english: And desired favour against him, that he would send for him to Jerusalem, laying wait in the way to kill him.
052503.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏔᏲᏎᎸᎩ ᏧᏓᏅᏖᎮᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏄᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏓᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎠᎦᏘᏃᎯᏍᏗᏱ; ᏭᏂᎭᎷᎨᏰᏃ ᏅᏃᎯ ᎤᏂᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏎᎢ.
052503.phonetic: Gv-wa-ta-yo-se-lv-gi tsu-da-nv-te-he-di-yi na-s-gi a-nu-hi-s-di-s-gv-i, na-s-gi u-da-nv-s-di-yi Tsi-lu-si-li-mi a-ga-ti-no-hi-s-di-yi; wu-ni-ha-lu-ge-ye-no nv-no-hi u-ni-hi-s-di-yi u-ni-ye-lv-se-i.

052504.english: But Festus answered, that Paul should be kept at Caesarea, and that he himself would depart shortly thither.
052504.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏇᏍᏓ ᎤᏁᏨᎩ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗᏱ ᏏᏌᎵᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏂᎪᎯᎸᎾᏉ ᎾᎿ ᎭᏭᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
052504.phonetic: A-se-no Que-s-da u-ne-tsv-gi Quo-la a-ga-ti-di-s-di-yi Si-sa-li-yi, a-le u-wa-sv ni-go-hi-lv-na-quo na-hna wu-lo-hi-s-di-yi.

052505.english: Let them therefore, said he, which among you are able, go down with me, and accuse this man, if there be any wickedness in him.
052505.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏂᎯ ᏱᏣᏓᏑᏯ ᏰᎵ ᎢᎨᏣᏛᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎢᏕᏒᎭ ᏪᏥᏱᎵᏙᎸᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎤᏍᎦᏅᏨᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
052505.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-we-sv-gi; na-s-gi i-yu-s-di ni-hi yi-tsa-da-su-ya ye-li i-ge-tsa-dv-ne-di ge-se-s-di i-de-sv-ha we-tsi-yi-li-do-lv-ha na-s-gi na a-s-ga-ya, i-yu-no u-s-ga-nv-tsv-hi ge-se-s-di.

052506.english: And when he had tarried among them more than ten days, he went down unto Caesarea; and the next day sitting on the judgment seat commanded Paul to be brought.
052506.cherokee: ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏃ ᎤᏗᏢᏍᏙᏗ ᏄᏒᎸ ᏚᏪᎳᏗᏙᎸ, ᏏᏌᎵᏱ ᏭᎶᏒᎩ; ᎤᎩᏨᏛᏃ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎦᏍᎩᎸ ᎤᏬᎸᎢ ᎤᏁᏨᎩ ᏉᎳ ᏩᏥᏯᏅᏗᏱ.
052506.phonetic: A-s-go-hi-no u-di-tlv-s-do-di nu-sv-lv du-we-la-di-do-lv, Si-sa-li-yi wu-lo-sv-gi; u-gi-tsv-dv-no di-gu-go-do-di-yi ga-s-gi-lv u-wo-lv-i u-ne-tsv-gi Quo-la wa-tsi-ya-nv-di-yi.

052507.english: And when he was come, the Jews which came down from Jerusalem stood round about, and laid many and grievous complaints against Paul, which they could not prove.
052507.cherokee: ᎤᎷᏨᏃ, ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏅᏓᏳᎶᏒᎯ ᎬᏩᏚᏫᏛ ᎤᎾᎴᏅᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏛᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎦᎨᏛ ᎠᏄᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᏉᎳ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᏒ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᎬᏩᏅᏁᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎩ.
052507.phonetic: U-lu-tsv-no, A-ni-tsu-si Tsi-lu-si-li-mi nv-da-yu-lo-sv-hi gv-wa-du-wi-dv u-na-le-nv-nv-gi, a-le u-tsa-dv-gi a-le ga-ge-dv a-nu-hi-s-di-s-gv-gi Quo-la, na-s-gi u-do-hi-yu-sv gv-ni-ge-sv i-gv-wa-nv-ne-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-gi.

052508.english: While he answered for himself, Neither against the law of the Jews, neither against the temple, nor yet against Caesar, have I offended any thing at all.
052508.cherokee: ᎤᏩᏒᏃ ᎠᎵᏍᏕᎵᏍᎬ ᎤᏁᏨᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎥᏝ ᎤᏍᏗᎤᏅ ᎠᎩᏍᎦᏅᏨᎯ ᏱᎩ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏧᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᎲᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏛᏅ-ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏥᏍᎦᏅᏤᎸᎯ ᏱᎩ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᏏᏌ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏥᏍᎦᏅᏤᎸᎯ ᏱᎩ.
052508.phonetic: U-wa-sv-no a-li-s-de-li-s-gv u-ne-tsv-gi hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; V-tla u-s-di-u-nv a-gi-s-ga-nv-tsv-hi yi-gi A-ni-tsu-si tsu-ni-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di u-ni-hv-i, v-tla a-le u-dv-nv--di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi tsi-s-ga-nv-tse-lv-hi yi-gi, v-tla a-le Si-sa go-hu-s-di tsi-s-ga-nv-tse-lv-hi yi-gi.

052509.english: But Festus, willing to do the Jews a pleasure, answered Paul, and said, Wilt thou go up to Jerusalem, and there be judged of these things before me?
052509.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏇᏍᏓ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎬᎢ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏧᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᏅᏓᏳᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅ, ᎤᏁᏤᎸᎩ ᏉᎳ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏣᏚᎵᏍᎪ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏤᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎬᏳᎪᏓᏁᏗᏱ?
052509.phonetic: A-se-no Que-s-da u-du-li-s-gv-i A-ni-tsu-si o-s-dv tsu-da-nv-da-di-s-do-di-yi nv-da-yu-li-s-do-ta-nv, u-ne-tse-lv-gi Quo-la hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi; Tsa-du-li-s-go Tsi-lu-si-li-mi tse-nv-s-di-yi a-le na-hna hi-a na-s-gi di-gv-yu-go-da-ne-di-yi?

052510.english: Then said Paul, I stand at Caesar's judgment seat, where I ought to be judged: to the Jews have I done no wrong, as thou very well knowest.
052510.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏏᏌ ᎤᏪᏍᎩᎸ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᏥᏙᎦ, ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏆᎵᏰᎢᎵᏕᏗᏱ.  ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎦᏥᏍᎦᏅᏤᎸᎯ ᏱᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏂᎯ ᏥᎦᏔᎭ.
052510.phonetic: Quo-la-no hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Si-sa u-we-s-gi-lv di-gu-go-do-di-yi tsi-do-ga, na-hna a-qua-li-ye-i-li-de-di-yi.  A-ni-tsu-si v-tla go-hu-s-di ga-tsi-s-ga-nv-tse-lv-hi yi-gi, na-s-gi o-si-yu ni-hi tsi-ga-ta-ha.

052511.english: For if I be an offender, or have committed any thing worthy of death, I refuse not to die: but if there be none of these things whereof these accuse me, no man may deliver me unto them. I appeal unto Caesar.
052511.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏰᏃ ᏥᏍᎦᎾ ᏱᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᎩᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᎢᎦᎢ ᎠᎩᏍᎦᏅᏨᎯ ᏱᎩ, ᎥᏝ ᏱᏙᎬᏆᏢᏫᏏ ᎠᎩᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗᏱ; ᎢᏳᏍᎩᏂ ᏄᏙᎯᏳᏒᎾ ᏱᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᎬᏇᎯᏍᏗᎭ, ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᏰᎵ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᏲᎯᏎᏗ ᏱᎩ ᎠᏴ.  ᏏᏐᎢ ᏫᏥᎥᏍᏓ.
052511.phonetic: I-yu-ye-no tsi-s-ga-na yi-gi, a-le gv-gi-yo-hu-hi-s-di i-ga-i a-gi-s-ga-nv-tsv-hi yi-gi, v-tla yi-do-gv-qua-tlv-wi-si a-gi-yo-hu-hi-s-di-yi; i-yu-s-gi-ni nu-do-hi-yu-sv-na yi-gi hi-a na-s-gi tsi-gv-que-hi-s-di-ha, v-tla gi-lo ye-li di-gv-wa-ni-yo-hi-se-di yi-gi a-yv.  Si-so-i wi-tsi-v-s-da.

052512.english: Then Festus, when he had conferred with the council, answered, Hast thou appealed unto Caesar? unto Caesar shalt thou go.
052512.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏇᏍᏓ ᏚᎵᏃᎮᏔᏅ ᏗᏂᎳᏫᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏏᏐᎢᏧ ᏫᎲᏍᏓ?  ᏏᏐᎢ ᏖᏏ.
052512.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Que-s-da du-li-no-he-ta-nv di-ni-la-wi-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Si-so-i-tsu wi-hv-s-da?  Si-so-i te-si.

052513.english: And after certain days king Agrippa and Bernice came unto Caesarea to salute Festus.
052513.cherokee: ᎢᎸᏍᎩᏃ ᏫᏄᏒᎸ ᏏᏌᎵᏱ ᎤᏂᎷᏨᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎡᎩᎵᏈ ᎠᎴ ᏆᏂᏏ, ᎤᏂᏲᎵᎵᎸᎩ ᏇᏍᏓ.
052513.phonetic: I-lv-s-gi-no wi-nu-sv-lv Si-sa-li-yi u-ni-lu-tsv-gi u-gv-wi-yu-hi E-gi-li-qui a-le Qua-ni-si, u-ni-yo-li-li-lv-gi Que-s-da.

052514.english: And when they had been there many days, Festus declared Paul's cause unto the king, saying, There is a certain man left in bonds by Felix:
052514.cherokee: ᎢᎸᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏬᎯᏨ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏁᏙᎸ, ᏇᏍᏓ ᎤᏃᎮᎮᎸᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏄᏍᏛ ᏉᎳ ᎤᎵᏱᎵᏕᎲᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᏴᎩ ᎠᏥᎧᎭ ᏈᎵᏏ ᎤᏪᎧᎯᏴᎯ.
052514.phonetic: I-lv-s-gi-no nu-wo-hi-tsv na-hna u-ne-do-lv, Que-s-da u-no-he-he-lv-gi u-gv-wi-yu-hi nu-s-dv Quo-la u-li-yi-li-de-hv-i, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Gi-lo i-yu-s-di a-s-ga-ya a-yv-gi a-tsi-ka-ha Qui-li-si u-we-ka-hi-yv-hi.

052515.english: About whom, when I was at Jerusalem, the chief priests and the elders of the Jews informed me, desiring to have judgment against him.
052515.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᎩᏃᎮᎮᎸᎩ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᎳᏫᎩ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏧᎾᏤᎵᎦ, ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏥᏫᎨᏙᎲᎩ, ᎠᏂᏔᏲᎯᎲᎩ ᎤᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᏧᏚᎪᏙᏗᏱ.
052515.phonetic: Na-s-gi gv-gi-no-he-he-lv-gi nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--a-ne-lo-hi a-le di-ni-la-wi-gi A-ni-tsu-si tsu-na-tse-li-ga, Tsi-lu-si-li-mi tsi-wi-ge-do-hv-gi, a-ni-ta-yo-hi-hv-gi u-s-ga-nv-tsv tsu-du-go-do-di-yi.

052516.english: To whom I answered, It is not the manner of the Romans to deliver any man to die, before that he which is accused have the accusers face to face, and have licence to answer for himself concerning the crime laid against him.
052516.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏥᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎠᏂᎶᎻ ᎥᏝ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏱᏄᏅᏅ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏂᏲᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎬᏂ ᎠᎫᎢᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᎬᏭᎯᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᏚᎾᎧᏛ ᏧᎪᎲᎯ ᏥᎨᏐ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎦᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᏥᎨᏐ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎠᎵᏍᏕᎵᏍᎬ ᎤᏁᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏃᎮᏗᏱ ᎤᏍᎦᏅᏨ ᎠᎫᎢᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ.
052516.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hi-a ni-ga-tsi-we-se-lv-gi; A-ni-lo-mi v-tla na-s-gi yi-nu-nv-nv, na-s-gi tsu-ni-yo-hi-s-di-yi gi-lo u-yo-hu-hi-s-di-yi, gv-ni a-gu-i-s-di-s-gi gv-wu-hi-s-di-s-gi du-na-ka-dv tsu-go-hv-hi tsi-ge-so a-le a-ga-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-lv-hi tsi-ge-so u-wa-sv a-li-s-de-li-s-gv u-ne-i-s-di-yi u-no-he-di-yi u-s-ga-nv-tsv a-gu-i-s-di-s-gv u-gv-wa-li.

052517.english: Therefore, when they were come hither, without any delay on the morrow I sat on the judgment seat, and commanded the man to be brought forth.
052517.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏂ ᎤᏂᎷᏥᎸ, ᏄᎵᏍᏛᏉ ᎤᎩᏨᏛ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎦᏍᎩᎸ ᎠᎩᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎩᏁᏨᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏩᏥᏯᏅᏗᏱ.
052517.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di a-ni u-ni-lu-tsi-lv, nu-li-s-dv-quo u-gi-tsv-dv di-gu-go-do-di-yi ga-s-gi-lv a-gi-nv-gi, a-le a-gi-ne-tsv-gi na-s-gi a-s-ga-ya wa-tsi-ya-nv-di-yi.

052518.english: Against whom when the accusers stood up, they brought none accusation of such things as I supposed:
052518.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏭᎯᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᏚᎾᎴᏅ, ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏳᏂᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏯᏂᎾᏄᎪᏩ ᎠᏇᎵᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏄᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ.
052518.phonetic: Na-s-gi gv-wu-hi-s-di-s-gi du-na-le-nv, v-tla go-hu-s-di yu-ni-na-nu-go-wi-se na-s-gi ya-ni-na-nu-go-wa a-que-li-sv na-s-gi a-nu-hi-s-di-s-gv-i.

052519.english: But had certain questions against him of their own superstition, and of one Jesus, which was dead, whom Paul affirmed to be alive.
052519.cherokee: ᎠᏄᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬᏍᎩᏂ ᎪᎱᏍᏗᏉ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎧᏃᎮᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏅᏒᏉ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏧᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏥᏌ, ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᎭ ᎠᏗᏍᎨ ᎨᎵ ᏉᎳ.
052519.phonetic: A-nu-hi-s-di-s-gv-s-gi-ni go-hu-s-di-quo u-s-di na-s-gi ka-no-he-di ge-sv u-nv-sv-quo u-na-tse-li-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi tsu-ni-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di ge-sv-i, a-le gi-lo i-yu-s-di Tsi-sa, u-yo-hu-sv-hi, na-s-gi e-ha a-di-s-ge ge-li Quo-la.

052520.english: And because I doubted of such manner of questions, I asked him whether he would go to Jerusalem, and there be judged of these matters.
052520.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎨᏒ ᏓᏆᏢᏫᏎᎲᎢ ᏅᏓᏳᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏅᏥᏪᏎᎸᎩ [ᏉᎳ]; ᏣᏚᎵᏍᎪ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏤᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏤᏧᎪᏓᏁᏗᏱ.
052520.phonetic: A-le na-s-gi i-yu-s-di ka-no-he-dv ge-sv da-qua-tlv-wi-se-hv-i nv-da-yu-li-s-do-ta-nv-gi, hi-a nv-tsi-we-se-lv-gi [Quo-la]; tsa-du-li-s-go Tsi-lu-si-li-mi tse-nv-s-di-yi na-hna-no hi-a na-s-gi ni-ga-dv tse-tsu-go-da-ne-di-yi.

052521.english: But when Paul had appealed to be reserved unto the hearing of Augustus, I commanded him to be kept till I might send him to Caesar.
052521.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏉᎳ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎠᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᎦᏍᏓ ᏧᏭᎪᏓᏁᏗᏱ, ᎠᎩᏁᏨᎩ ᎠᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗᏱ, ᎬᏂ ᎬᏆᏓᏅᏍᏗ ᏱᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ, ᏏᏐᎢ.
052521.phonetic: A-se-no Quo-la u-ne-tsv a-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di-yi, a-le O-ga-s-da tsu-wu-go-da-ne-di-yi, a-gi-ne-tsv-gi a-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di-yi, gv-ni gv-qua-da-nv-s-di yi-nu-li-s-ta-nv, Si-so-i.

052522.english: Then Agrippa said unto Festus, I would also hear the man myself. To morrow, said he, thou shalt hear him.
052522.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎡᎩᎵᏈ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᏇᏍᏓ; ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏋᏒ ᎠᏆᏚᎵᎭ ᏥᏯᏛᎦᏁᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ.  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎤᎩᏨᏅ ᏘᏯᏛᎦᏁᎵ.
052522.phonetic: Hna-quo-no E-gi-li-qui hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi Que-s-da; a-yv na-s-quo a-quv-sv a-qua-du-li-ha tsi-ya-dv-ga-ne-di-yi na-s-gi a-s-ga-ya.  Hi-a-no nu-we-sv-gi; u-gi-tsv-nv ti-ya-dv-ga-ne-li.

052523.english: And on the morrow, when Agrippa was come, and Bernice, with great pomp, and was entered into the place of hearing, with the chief captains, and principal men of the city, at Festus' commandment Paul was brought forth.
052523.cherokee: ᎰᏩᏃ ᎤᎩᏨᏛ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᎾᏣᏅᎯ ᎤᏂᎷᏨ ᎡᎩᎵᏈ ᎠᎴ ᏆᏂᏏ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏗᏓᏱᎵᏓᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏴᎸ, ᎠᎴ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᏗᎾᏓᏘᏂᏙᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎨᏥᎸᏉᏗ ᎾᎿᎭᎦᏚᎲ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᏇᏍᏓ ᎤᏁᏨ, ᏉᎳ ᏩᏥᏯᏅᎲᎩ.
052523.phonetic: Ho-wa-no u-gi-tsv-dv u-tsa-ti u-na-tsa-nv-hi u-ni-lu-tsv E-gi-li-qui a-le Qua-ni-si, na-s-gi-no di-da-yi-li-da-s-di-yi u-ni-yv-lv, a-le nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv di-na-da-ti-ni-do-hi, a-le ge-tsi-lv-quo-di na-hna ga-du-hv a-ne-hi, Que-s-da u-ne-tsv, Quo-la wa-tsi-ya-nv-hv-gi.

052524.english: And Festus said, King Agrippa, and all men which are here present with us, ye see this man, about whom all the multitude of the Jews have dealt with me, both at Jerusalem, and also here, crying that he ought not to live any longer.
052524.cherokee: ᏇᏍᏓᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎡᎩᎵᏈ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᏂ ᏥᏗᎾ; ᎡᏥᎪᏩᏗᎭ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ, ᎾᏍᎩ; ᎠᏄᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏥᎬᎩᎷᏤᎸᎩ ᏥᎷ ᏏᎵᎻ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏂ, ᎯᎠ ᏥᎾᏂᏪᏍᎬᎩ; ᎥᏝ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏱᎦᎩ ᎠᏏᏉ ᏱᎬᏅ.
052524.phonetic: Que-s-da-no hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi E-gi-li-qui, a-le na-s-quo ni-ga-dv i-tsi-s-ga-ya a-ni tsi-di-na; e-tsi-go-wa-di-ha hi-a a-s-ga-ya, na-s-gi; a-nu-hi-s-di-s-gv ni-ga-dv u-ni-tsa-ti A-ni-tsu-si tsi-gv-gi-lu-tse-lv-gi Tsi-lu-si-li-mi, a-le na-s-quo a-ni, hi-a tsi-na-ni-we-s-gv-gi; v-tla o-si-yu yi-ga-gi a-si-quo yi-gv-nv.

052525.english: But when I found that he had committed nothing worthy of death, and that he himself hath appealed to Augustus, I have determined to send him.
052525.cherokee: ᎠᏆᏙᎴᎰᏒᏃ ᏄᏍᎦᏅᏨᎾ ᎨᏒ ᏰᎵ ᎬᏩᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎣᎦᏍᏙᎢ ᏭᎲᏍᏔᏅ, ᏓᏇᎪᏔᏅ ᎠᎦᏘᏅᏍᏗᏱ.
052525.phonetic: A-qua-do-le-ho-sv-no nu-s-ga-nv-tsv-na ge-sv ye-li gv-wa-yo-hu-hi-s-di ge-sv-i, a-le u-wa-sv O-ga-s-do-i wu-hv-s-ta-nv, da-que-go-ta-nv a-ga-ti-nv-s-di-yi.

052526.english: Of whom I have no certain thing to write unto my lord. Wherefore I have brought him forth before you, and specially before thee, O king Agrippa, that, after examination had, I might have somewhat to write.
052526.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏃᎮᏍᎬᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏯᎩᎭ ᎦᏥᏲᏪᎳᏁᏗ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏨᏯᏘᏃᎯᏏ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎯ Ꮀ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎭ ᎬᏩᏘᏃᎯᏏ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎡᎩᎵᏈ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎡᏗᎪᎵᏰᎥᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏰᎵ ᎬᏉᏪᎶᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
052526.phonetic: Na-s-gi tsi-no-he-s-gv-i, v-tla go-hu-s-di u-s-qua-ni-go-di ya-gi-ha ga-tsi-yo-we-la-ne-di u-gv-wi-yu-hi; na-s-gi i-yu-s-di i-tsv-ya-ti-no-hi-si, a-le ni-hi ho u-gv-wi-yu-ha gv-wa-ti-no-hi-si, tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi E-gi-li-qui, na-s-gi-no e-di-go-li-ye-v-hi ge-se-s-di, go-hu-s-di ye-li gv-quo-we-lo-di ge-se-s-di.

052527.english: For it seemeth to me unreasonable to send a prisoner, and not withal to signify the crimes laid against him.
052527.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᏱᏙᎬᏩᏳᎪᏗ ᎨᎵᎠ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏱᎦᏓᏅᏍᎦ ᎠᎦᏘᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᏴᎩ, ᎾᎩᏁᎢᏍᏔᏅᎾᏃ ᏱᎩ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎠᎫᎢᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ.
052527.phonetic: V-tla-ye-no yi-do-gv-wa-yu-go-di ge-li-a, i-yu-no yi-ga-da-nv-s-ga a-ga-ti-nv-s-di-yi a-yv-gi, na-gi-ne-i-s-ta-nv-na-no yi-gi nu-s-dv a-gu-i-s-di-s-gv-i.

052601.english: Then Agrippa said unto Paul, Thou art permitted to speak for thyself. Then Paul stretched forth the hand, and answered for himself:
052601.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎡᎩᎵᏈ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᏉᎳ; ᏨᏒ ᎭᎵᏍᏕᎵᏍᎬ ᎨᏣᏁᎢᏍᏗ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏓ.  ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏉᎳ ᎤᏙᏯᏅᎯᏛ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎠᎵᏍᏕᎵᏍᎬ ᎤᏬᏂᏒᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ;
052601.phonetic: Hna-quo-no E-gi-li-qui hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi Quo-la; Tsv-sv ha-li-s-de-li-s-gv ge-tsa-ne-i-s-di ni-ga-li-s-da.  Hna-quo-no Quo-la u-do-ya-nv-hi-dv u-wa-sv a-li-s-de-li-s-gv u-wo-ni-sv-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi;

052602.english: I think myself happy, king Agrippa, because I shall answer for myself this day before thee touching all the things whereof I am accused of the Jews:
052602.cherokee: ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎡᎩᎵᏉ, ᎦᎵᎡᎵᎦ ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ ᏥᎩ ᎯᎦᏔᎲ ᎦᎵᏍᏕᎵᏍᎬ ᏨᏓᏥᏁᎢᏍᏔᏂ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎬᏇᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ.
052602.phonetic: Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi E-gi-li-quo, ga-li-e-li-ga go-hi i-ga tsi-gi hi-ga-ta-hv ga-li-s-de-li-s-gv tsv-da-tsi-ne-i-s-ta-ni ni-ga-v gv-que-hi-s-di-s-gv-i A-ni-tsu-si.

052603.english: Especially because I know thee to be expert in all customs and questions which are among the Jews: wherefore I beseech thee to hear me patiently.
052603.cherokee: Ꮀ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎭ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ ᎯᎦᏔᎯᏳ ᏥᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎧᏃᎮᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏥᏚᏁᎭ.  ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎬᏔᏲᏎᎭ ᏂᏗᏣᏯᏪᎬᎾ ᏍᏆᏛᎦᏁᏗᏱ.
052603.phonetic: Ho u-gv-wi-yu-ha nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha hi-ga-ta-hi-yu tsi-gi ni-ga-dv di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di a-le ka-no-he-di ge-sv A-ni-tsu-si tsi-du-ne-ha.  Na-s-gi-no i-yu-s-di gv-ta-yo-se-ha ni-di-tsa-ya-we-gv-na s-qua-dv-ga-ne-di-yi.

052604.english: My manner of life from my youth, which was at the first among mine own nation at Jerusalem, know all the Jews;
052604.cherokee: ᏄᏍᏛ ᎠᏆᎴᏂᏙᎸ ᏥᏫᏄᏣ ᏅᏧᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏓᎴᏅᏛ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎬᏱᏱ ᎦᏥᏰᎳᏗᏙᎸᎩ ᏗᏆᏤᎵ ᏴᏫ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ, ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎭ,
052604.phonetic: Nu-s-dv a-qua-le-ni-do-lv tsi-wi-nu-tsa nv-tsu-li-s-ta-nv nv-da-gv-wa-da-le-nv-dv, na-s-gi i-gv-yi-yi ga-tsi-ye-la-di-do-lv-gi di-qua-tse-li yv-wi Tsi-lu-si-li-mi, ni-ga-dv A-ni-tsu-si a-ni-ga-ta-ha,

052605.english: Which knew me from the beginning, if they would testify, that after the most straitest sect of our religion I lived a Pharisee.
052605.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᎩᎦᏔᎲᎩ ᏗᏓᎴᏂᏍᎬ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏓᎴᏅᏛ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏳᏂᏃᎮᎸ, ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎯᏳᏰᏃ ᎠᏆᎵᏏ ᎤᎴᏂᏓᏍᏗ ᎠᏆᎴᏂᏙᎸᎢ, ᏕᏥᏍᏓᏩᏗᏒ ᎤᎾᏓᏤᎵᏛ ᎤᎾᎵᎪᎯ ᏭᏂᎫᏛ ᏗᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏕᎩ ᎣᎦᏤᎵ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏦᏥᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
052605.phonetic: Na-s-gi gv-gi-ga-ta-hv-gi di-da-le-ni-s-gv nv-da-gv-wa-da-le-nv-dv, i-yu-no yu-ni-no-he-lv, a-ni-ga-ta-hi-yu-ye-no A-qua-li-si u-le-ni-da-s-di a-qua-le-ni-do-lv-i, de-tsi-s-da-wa-di-sv u-na-da-tse-li-dv u-na-li-go-hi wu-ni-gu-dv di-ni-ka-hna-wa-de-gi o-ga-tse-li U-ne-la-nv-hi tso-tsi-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di ge-sv-i.

052606.english: And now I stand and am judged for the hope of the promise made of God unto our fathers:
052606.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏃ ᎢᏥᏙᎦ ᎢᎬᎩᏱᎵᏙᎭ, ᎠᎩᏍᏛᏗᎭ ᎤᏚᎩ ᎬᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏥᏚᏚᎢᏍᏓᏁᎴ ᏦᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ;
052606.phonetic: Go-hi-no i-tsi-do-ga i-gv-gi-yi-li-do-ha, a-gi-s-dv-di-ha u-du-gi gv-di ge-sv na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi tsi-du-du-i-s-da-ne-le tso-gi-ga-yv-li-ge-i;

052607.english: Unto which promise our twelve tribes, instantly serving God day and night, hope to come. For which hope's sake, king Agrippa, I am accused of the Jews.
052607.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎨᏥᏚᎢᏍᏓᏁᎸ ᎤᎾᎵᏱᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏚᎩ ᎤᏅᎭ ᏔᎳᏚ ᏃᏥᎳᏍᏓᎸᎢ, ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏗᏳ ᏥᏓᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏗᎬᏩᎩᏨᏗ.  ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏚᎩ ᎬᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎡᎩᎵᏈ, ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎬᏇᎯᏍᏗᎭ.
052607.phonetic: Na-s-gi ge-tsi-du-i-s-da-ne-lv u-na-li-yi-lo-hi-s-di-yi u-du-gi u-nv-ha ta-la-du no-tsi-la-s-da-lv-i, u-li-ni-gi-di-yu tsi-da-ni-ka-hna-wa-di U-ne-la-nv-hi di-gv-wa-gi-tsv-di.  Na-s-gi u-du-gi gv-di ge-sv nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha, tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi E-gi-li-qui, A-ni-tsu-si gv-que-hi-s-di-ha.

052608.english: Why should it be thought a thing incredible with you, that God should raise the dead?
052608.cherokee: ᎦᏙᏃ ᎦᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎢᏥᏰᎸᏐ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏧᎴᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏧᏂᏲᎱᏒᎯ?
052608.phonetic: Ga-do-no ga-go-hi-yu-di ni-ge-sv-na i-tsi-ye-lv-so U-ne-la-nv-hi tsu-le-do-di ge-sv tsu-ni-yo-hu-sv-hi?

052609.english: I verily thought with myself, that I ought to do many things contrary to the name of Jesus of Nazareth.
052609.cherokee: ᎠᏴ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎨᎵᏍᎬᎩ; ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᏱᏂᎦᏛᏁᎭ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏱᏓᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁ ᎦᏡᏗᏍᎬ ᏕᎤᏙᎥ ᏥᏌ ᎾᏎᎵᏘ ᏤᎲᎩ.
052609.phonetic: A-yv u-do-hi-yu-hi hi-a ni-ge-li-s-gv-gi; du-yu-go-dv yi-ni-ga-dv-ne-ha u-tsa-ti go-hu-s-di yi-da-gi-lv-wi-s-da-ne ga-tlu-di-s-gv de-u-do-v Tsi-sa Na-se-li-ti tse-hv-gi.

052610.english: Which thing I also did in Jerusalem: and many of the saints did I shut up in prison, having received authority from the chief priests; and when they were put to death, I gave my voice against them.
052610.cherokee: ᎰᏩᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏆᏛᏁᎸᎩ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏛᎩ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘ ᏕᏥᏍᏚᏅᎩ ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏗᏱ, ᎬᎩᏁᏤᎸᎯ ᎨᏒᎩ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ; ᏕᎨᏥᎷᎦᏃ ᏥᏁᎬᎩ ᎦᏥᏯᏡᏗᏍᎬᎢ.
052610.phonetic: Ho-wa-no na-s-gi na-qua-dv-ne-lv-gi Tsi-lu-si-li-mi; a-le u-ni-tsa-dv-gi u-na-da-nv-ti de-tsi-s-du-nv-gi di-da-s-du-di-yi, gv-gi-ne-tse-lv-hi ge-sv-gi nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--a-ne-lo-hi; de-ge-tsi-lu-ga-no tsi-ne-gv-gi ga-tsi-ya-tlu-di-s-gv-i.

052611.english: And I punished them oft in every synagogue, and compelled them to blaspheme; and being exceedingly mad against them, I persecuted them even unto strange cities.
052611.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏯᏃᎩᏳ ᎤᏲ ᏂᎦᏥᏴᏁᎲ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏕᎨᏌᏗᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏐᏢᎢᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᏂᎦᏥᏴᏁᎲᎩ; ᎤᏣᏔᏅᎯᏃ ᎦᏥᏂᏆᏘᎲ ᎾᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᎤᏲ ᏂᎦᏥᏴᏁᎲ ᏅᏩᏓᎴ ᏙᏗᎦᏚᎲ ᏫᎦᏥᎨᎰᎢᎲᎩ.
052611.phonetic: A-le ya-no-gi-yu u-yo ni-ga-tsi-yv-ne-hv di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi de-ge-sa-di-sv-i, a-le u-ni-so-tlv-i-s-do-di-yi ni-ga-tsi-yv-ne-hv-gi; u-tsa-ta-nv-hi-no ga-tsi-ni-qua-ti-hv na-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-gi u-yo ni-ga-tsi-yv-ne-hv nv-wa-da-le do-di-ga-du-hv wi-ga-tsi-ge-ho-i-hv-gi.

052612.english: Whereupon as I went to Damascus with authority and commission from the chief priests,

052613.english: At midday, O king, I saw in the way a light from heaven, above the brightness of the sun, shining round about me and them which journeyed with me.
052613.cherokee: ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎦᎢᏒ ᎢᎦ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎠᎩᎪᎲᎩ ᎢᎦᎦᏛᎢ, ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏅᏓᏳᎶᏒᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏍᎪᏒᎢ ᎤᏟ ᎨᏒᎩ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᏅᏙ, ᏓᏆᏚᏫᏍᏔᏅᎩ ᎠᏴ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎣᏤᎯ.
052613.phonetic: Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, ga-i-sv i-ga a-ye-li a-gi-go-hv-gi i-ga-ga-dv-i, ga-lv-la-di nv-da-yu-lo-sv-hi, na-s-gi u-s-go-sv-i u-tli ge-sv-gi e-s-ga-quo nv-do, da-qua-du-wi-s-ta-nv-gi a-yv a-le na-s-quo o-tse-hi.

052614.english: And when we were all fallen to the earth, I heard a voice speaking unto me, and saying in the Hebrew tongue, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me? it is hard for thee to kick against the pricks.
052614.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᎡᎳᏗ ᏙᎩᏅᏨ, ᎧᏁᎬ ᎠᏆᏛᎦᏅᎩ ᎠᎩᏁᏤᎲᎩ, ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᏂᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗ ᎬᏗᏍᎬᎩ; ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎬᎩ ᏐᎳ, ᏐᎳ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎤᏲ ᏂᏍᏋᏁᎭ?  ᎤᏕᏯᏙᏗᏳ ᏗᏣᎲᏖᏍᏗᏱ ᏗᎪᏍᏓᏯ.
052614.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv-no e-la-di do-gi-nv-tsv, ka-ne-gv a-qua-dv-ga-nv-gi a-gi-ne-tse-hv-gi, A-ni-tsu-si u-ni-wo-ni-hi-s-di gv-di-s-gv-gi; hi-a ni-ga-we-s-gv-gi So-la, So-la, ga-do-no u-yo ni-s-quv-ne-ha?  U-de-ya-do-di-yu di-tsa-hv-te-s-di-yi di-go-s-da-ya.

052615.english: And I said, Who art thou, Lord? And he said, I am Jesus whom thou persecutest.
052615.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᎾᎩᏪᏒᎩ; ᎦᎪ ᏂᎯ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ?  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎠᏏ ᏥᏌ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏲ ᏥᏂᏴᏁᎭ.
052615.phonetic: Hi-a-no na-gi-we-sv-gi; Ga-go ni-hi, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi?  Hi-a-no nu-we-sv-gi; A-si Tsi-sa na-s-gi u-yo tsi-ni-yv-ne-ha.

052616.english: But rise, and stand upon thy feet: for I have appeared unto thee for this purpose, to make thee a minister and a witness both of these things which thou hast seen, and of those things in the which I will appear unto thee;
052616.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏔᎴᎲᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎭᎴᎲᎦ, ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏖᎭ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏥᏂᎬᏯᏛᏂᏏ, ᎭᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎩ ᎢᎬᏴᏁᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎯᏃᎮᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᎪᏩᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏏ ᎬᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᏗ ᏥᎩ.
052616.phonetic: A-se-no ta-le-hv-ga, a-le ha-le-hv-ga, hi-a-ye-no nv-di-ga-li-s-do-te-ha gv-ni-ge-sv tsi-ni-gv-ya-dv-ni-si, ha-li-tsi-do-hv-s-gi i-gv-yv-ne-di-yi a-le hi-no-he-s-gi hi-a na-s-gi tsi-go-wa-dv, a-le a-si gv-na-nu-go-wi-se-di tsi-gi.

052617.english: Delivering thee from the people, and from the Gentiles, unto whom now I send thee,
052617.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏓᎴᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎨᏣᏡᏗᏍᎬ ᎯᎠ ᏴᏫ ᎠᎴ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏁᎲ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎬᏅᎵ,
052617.phonetic: Gv-wa-da-le-s-ge-s-di ge-tsa-tlu-di-s-gv hi-a yv-wi a-le tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi, na-s-gi di-ne-hv hna-quo gv-nv-li,

052618.english: To open their eyes, and to turn them from darkness to light, and from the power of Satan unto God, that they may receive forgiveness of sins, and inheritance among them which are sanctified by faith that is in me.
052618.cherokee: ᏘᏍᏚᎢᎡᏗᏱ ᏗᏂᎦᏙᎵ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏏᎬ ᏄᎾᏛᏅ ᏘᎪᎸᏍᏗᏱ ᎢᎦᎦᏛ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏫᏘᎦᏔᎲᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏎᏓᏂ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᏄᎾᏛᏅ ᏘᎪᎸᏍᏗᏱ ᏙᏘᎦᏔᎲᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏫᏘᎦᏔᎲᏍᏙᏗᏱ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎦᎨᏥᏁᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏍᎦᏅᏨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏂᏩᏛᏗ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎨᏥᏅᎦᎸᏛ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᎥᏉᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
052618.phonetic: Ti-s-du-i-e-di-yi di-ni-ga-do-li, a-le u-li-si-gv nu-na-dv-nv ti-go-lv-s-di-yi i-ga-ga-dv i-di-tlv wi-ti-ga-ta-hv-s-di-yi, a-le Se-da-ni u-tse-li-go-hi nu-na-dv-nv ti-go-lv-s-di-yi do-ti-ga-ta-hv-s-di-yi U-ne-la-nv-hi i-di-tlv wi-ti-ga-ta-hv-s-do-di-yi; na-s-gi di-ga-ge-tsi-ne-di-yi u-ni-s-ga-nv-tsv-i, a-le gv-wa-ni-wa-dv-di i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi u-na-tse-li-ga i-yu-li-s-do-di ge-sv ge-tsi-nv-ga-lv-dv nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv v-quo-hi-yu-di ge-sv-i.

052619.english: Whereupon, O king Agrippa, I was not disobedient unto the heavenly vision:
052619.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏏᏗ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎡᎩᎵᏈ, ᎥᏝ ᎾᏉᎯᏳᏅᎾ ᏱᎨᏎ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏅᏓᏳᎶᏒᎯ ᎠᎩᎪᎲᎢ.
052619.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-si-di, tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi E-gi-li-qui, v-tla na-quo-hi-yu-nv-na yi-ge-se ga-lv-la-di nv-da-yu-lo-sv-hi a-gi-go-hv-i.

052620.english: But shewed first unto them of Damascus, and at Jerusalem, and throughout all the coasts of Judaea, and then to the Gentiles, that they should repent and turn to God, and do works meet for repentance.
052620.cherokee: ᎦᏥᏃᏁᎸᎩᏰᏃ ᎢᎬᏱ ᏕᎹᏍᎦ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᏧᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏂᏁᏟᏴᏍᏗᏱ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏭᎾᎦᏔᎲᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏚᏂᏁᏟᏴᏒ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛᎢ.
