052620.phonetic: Ga-tsi-no-ne-lv-gi-ye-no i-gv-yi De-ma-s-ga a-ne-hi, a-le tsi-lu-si-li-mi a-ne-hi, a-le ni-gv-na-dv Tsu-di-yi a-le na-s-quo tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi, na-s-gi tsu-ni-ne-tli-yv-s-di-yi du-na-da-nv-dv-i, a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi i-di-tlv wu-na-ga-ta-hv-s-do-di-yi, a-le tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi a-do-le-ho-hi-s-do-di ge-sv du-ni-ne-tli-yv-sv du-na-da-nv-dv-i.

052621.english: For these causes the Jews caught me in the temple, and went about to kill me.
052621.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏓᏳᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅᎩ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎬᎩᏂᏴᎲᎩ ᎤᏛᏅ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᎬᎩᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏒᎩ.
052621.phonetic: Na-s-gi nv-da-yu-li-s-do-ta-nv-gi A-ni-tsu-si gv-gi-ni-yv-hv-gi u-dv-nv di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi, gv-gi-hi-s-di-yi u-ni-ye-lv-sv-gi.

052622.english: Having therefore obtained help of God, I continue unto this day, witnessing both to small and great, saying none other things than those which the prophets and Moses did say should come:
052622.cherokee: ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏃ ᎠᎩᏍᏕᎸᎲᎢ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ ᎠᏏ ᎦᎴᏂᏙᎭ ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ ᏥᎩ, ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᎦᏥᏴᏁᎭ ᏂᎨᏥᎸᏉᏛᎾ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎨᏥᎸᏉᏗ, ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏂᏥᏃᎮᏍᎬᎾ Ꮎ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎼᏏ ᎤᎾᏄᎪᎢᏍᏗ ᎤᎾᏛᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ;
052622.phonetic: U-ne-la-nv-hi-no a-gi-s-de-lv-hv-i nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha a-si ga-le-ni-do-ha go-hi i-ga tsi-gi, gv-ni-ge-sv ni-ga-tsi-yv-ne-ha ni-ge-tsi-lv-quo-dv-na a-le na-s-quo ge-tsi-lv-quo-di, go-hu-s-di ni-tsi-no-he-s-gv-na na u-wa-sv a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi a-le Mo-si u-na-nu-go-i-s-di u-na-dv-nv-hi tsi-gi;

052623.english: That Christ should suffer, and that he should be the first that should rise from the dead, and should shew light unto the people, and to the Gentiles.
052623.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎤᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎬᏱ ᎤᏲᎱᏒ ᏧᎴᎯᏐᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᎦᎦᏘ ᏧᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᏗᏱ ᎯᎠ ᏴᏫ ᎠᎴ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ.
052623.phonetic: Na-s-gi Ga-lo-ne-dv u-gi-li-yo-i-s-di-yi, a-le na-s-gi i-gv-yi u-yo-hu-sv tsu-le-hi-so-di-yi, a-le i-ga-ga-ti tsu-na-nu-go-wi-se-di-yi hi-a yv-wi a-le tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi.

052624.english: And as he thus spake for himself, Festus said with a loud voice, Paul, thou art beside thyself; much learning doth make thee mad.
052624.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏉᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎬᎩ, ᎤᏩᏒ ᎠᎵᏍᏕᎵᏍᎬᎢ, ᏇᏍᏓ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᏓᏯ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏣᎸᏃᏘᎭ ᏉᎳ, ᎤᏣᏘ ᎪᏪᎵ ᏘᏏᎾᏏᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᎸᏃᏙᏗᎭ.
052624.phonetic: A-si-quo-no na-s-gi ni-ga-we-s-gv-gi, u-wa-sv a-li-s-de-li-s-gv-i, Que-s-da hi-a a-s-da-ya nu-we-sv-gi; tsa-lv-no-ti-ha Quo-la, u-tsa-ti go-we-li ti-si-na-si-yu ge-sv na-s-gi tsa-lv-no-do-di-ha.

052625.english: But he said, I am not mad, most noble Festus; but speak forth the words of truth and soberness.
052625.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎥᏝ ᏯᎩᎸᏃᏘᎭ, ᎰᏍᏛ ᏇᏍᏓ, ᏥᏁᎦᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏃᎸᏃᏘᏍᎬᎾ ᎧᏁᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
052625.phonetic: A-se-no hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; v-tla ya-gi-lv-no-ti-ha, ho-s-dv Que-s-da, tsi-ne-ga-quo-s-gi-ni du-yu-go-dv ge-sv-i a-le no-lv-no-ti-s-gv-na ka-ne-i-s-di ge-sv-i.

052626.english: For the king knoweth of these things, before whom also I speak freely: for I am persuaded that none of these things are hidden from him; for this thing was not done in a corner.
052626.cherokee: ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎠᎦᏔᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏛᏓᏍᏛ ᏂᏥᏍᎦᎢᎲᎾ ᏥᏥᏁᎦ; ᏥᎦᏔᎭᏰᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎠᎬᏍᎦᎳᏁᎸᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏞᏰᏃ ᎤᏅᏏᏴ ᎯᎠ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᏱᎩ.
052626.phonetic: U-gv-wi-yu-hi-ye-no na-s-gi hi-a a-ga-ta-ha, na-s-gi na-s-quo u-dv-da-s-dv ni-tsi-s-ga-i-hv-na tsi-tsi-ne-ga; tsi-ga-ta-ha-ye-no hi-a na-s-gi go-hu-s-di a-gv-s-ga-la-ne-lv-hi ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i, tle-ye-no u-nv-si-yv hi-a di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv-hi yi-gi.

052627.english: King Agrippa, believest thou the prophets? I know that thou believest.
052627.cherokee: ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎡᎩᎵᏈ, ᏕᎰᎯᏳᎲᏍᎦᏍᎪ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ?  ᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᏕᎰᎯᏳᎲᏍᎬᎢ.
052627.phonetic: Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi E-gi-li-qui, de-ho-hi-yu-hv-s-ga-s-go a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi?  Tsi-ga-ta-ha de-ho-hi-yu-hv-s-gv-i.

052628.english: Then Agrippa said unto Paul, Almost thou persuadest me to be a Christian.
052628.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎡᎩᎵᏈ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᏉᎳ; ᎠᎴᏉ ᏂᏍᎩᏎᎪᎩᎠ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᏥᏲᎢᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᎢᏯᏆᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
052628.phonetic: Hna-quo-no E-gi-li-qui hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi Quo-la; a-le-quo ni-s-gi-se-go-gi-a Ga-lo-ne-dv tsi-yo-i-yu-hv-s-gi i-ya-qua-li-s-do-di-yi.

052629.english: And Paul said, I would to God, that not only thou, but also all that hear me this day, were both almost, and altogether such as I am, except these bonds.
052629.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏥᏯᏚᎸᎡᎭ, ᎥᏝ ᏂᎯᏉ ᏨᏒ, ᎾᏍᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏥᎬᏆᏛᎦᏁᎭ ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ ᏥᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴᏉ ᎾᏆᏍᏛᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯᏍᎩᏂ ᎾᏆᏍᏛ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏨᏆᎸᎥ ᎤᏩᏒ.
052629.phonetic: Quo-la-no hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; U-ne-la-nv-hi tsi-ya-du-lv-e-ha, v-tla ni-hi-quo tsv-sv, na-s-quo-s-gi-ni ni-ga-dv tsi-gv-qua-dv-ga-ne-ha go-hi i-ga tsi-gi, na-s-gi, v-tla a-le-quo na-qua-s-dv-i, na-s-gi-ya-s-gi-ni na-qua-s-dv i-tsa-li-s-do-di-yi, hi-a na-s-gi tsv-qua-lv-v u-wa-sv.

052630.english: And when he had thus spoken, the king rose up, and the governor, and Bernice, and they that sat with them:
052630.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏪᏒ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏚᎴᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏆᏂᏏ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏅᏅᎯ,
052630.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nu-we-sv, u-gv-wi-yu-hi du-le-nv-gi, a-le a-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di u-gv-wi-yu-hi, a-le Qua-ni-si, a-le na-s-gi u-na-nv-nv-hi,

052631.english: And when they were gone aside, they talked between themselves, saying, This man doeth nothing worthy of death or of bonds.
052631.cherokee: ᎢᏴᏛᏃ ᏭᏂᎶᏒ, ᎤᏅᏒ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎾᎵᏃᎮᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏱᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎭ ᎬᏩᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎦᏰᎦᎸᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
052631.phonetic: I-yv-dv-no wu-ni-lo-sv, u-nv-sv ge-sv u-na-li-no-he-lv-gi, hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Hi-a a-s-ga-ya v-tla go-hu-s-di yi-du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-ha gv-wa-yo-hu-hi-s-di a-le ga-ye-ga-lv-s-di ge-sv-i.

052632.english: Then said Agrippa unto Festus, This man might have been set at liberty, if he had not appealed unto Caesar.
052632.cherokee: ᎡᎩᎵᏈᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᏇᏍᏓ; ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏗᎦᏰᏥᏲᎯᏍᏗᏉ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏏᏐᎢ ᏫᏄᎲᏍᏔᏅᎾ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ.
052632.phonetic: E-gi-li-qui-no hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi Que-s-da; hi-a a-s-ga-ya di-ga-ye-tsi-yo-hi-s-di-quo yi-ge-se-i, i-yu-no Si-so-i wi-nu-hv-s-ta-nv-na yi-ge-se-i.

052701.english: And when it was determined that we should sail into Italy, they delivered Paul and certain other prisoners unto one named Julius, a centurion of Augustus' band.
052701.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏚᏚᎪᏔᏅ ᎢᏓᎵ ᏭᎩᏅᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᏥᏳ, ᏚᏂᏲᏒᎩ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴ ᎠᏂᏴᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᏂᏲᎯᏎᎸ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ, ᏧᎵᏯᏏ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎨᎳ ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩ ᎨᏒ ᎣᎦᏍᏓ ᏧᏤᎵᎦ.
052701.phonetic: Hna-quo-no du-du-go-ta-nv I-da-li wu-gi-nv-s-do-di-yi tsi-yu, du-ni-yo-sv-gi Quo-la a-le gi-lo i-yu-na-s-di nv-wa-na-da-le a-ni-yv-gi, na-s-gi du-ni-yo-hi-se-lv gi-lo i-yu-s-di a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui di-ti-ni-do-hi, Tsu-li-ya-si tsu-do-i-dv, ge-la su-na-da-tlu-gi ge-sv O-ga-s-da tsu-tse-li-ga.

052702.english: And entering into a ship of Adramyttium, we launched, meaning to sail by the coasts of Asia; one Aristarchus, a Macedonian of Thessalonica, being with us.
052702.cherokee: ᎣᎦᏣᏅᏃ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎡᏟᎻᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎣᎩᎧᏙᏴᎩ, ᎡᏏᏱ ᎤᎶᏗᏢ ᎣᎩᏅᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎣᎩᏰᎸᏒᎩ, ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎡᎵᏍᏓᎦ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎹᏏᏙᏂ ᏕᏌᎶᏂᎦ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎡᎯ, ᎣᏤᎲᎩ.
052702.phonetic: O-ga-tsa-nv-no tsi-yu-hi E-tli-mi-di e-hi o-gi-ka-do-yv-gi, E-si-yi u-lo-di-tlv o-gi-nv-s-do-di-yi o-gi-ye-lv-sv-gi, gi-lo i-yu-s-di, E-li-s-da-ga tsu-do-i-dv, Ma-si-do-ni De-sa-lo-ni-ga ga-du-hv e-hi, o-tse-hv-gi.

052703.english: And the next day we touched at Sidon. And Julius courteously entreated Paul, and gave him liberty to go unto his friends to refresh himself.
052703.cherokee: ᎤᎩᏨᏛᏃ ᏌᏙᏂ ᎣᎩᏃᏠᏨᎩ.  ᏧᎵᏯᏏᏃ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏄᏛᏁᎸᎩ ᏉᎳ, ᎤᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᎸᎩᏰᏃ ᏧᎵᎢ ᏧᏩᏛᎲᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᏍᏆᏂᎪᏙᏗᏱ.
052703.phonetic: U-gi-tsv-dv-no Sa-do-ni o-gi-no-tlo-tsv-gi.  Tsu-li-ya-si-no o-s-dv nu-dv-ne-lv-gi Quo-la, u-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-lv-gi-ye-no tsu-li-i tsu-wa-dv-hv-s-di-yi, a-le na-s-gi gv-wa-s-qua-ni-go-do-di-yi.

052704.english: And when we had launched from thence, we sailed under Cyprus, because the winds were contrary.
052704.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᏫᎣᎩᎧᏙᏴ ᏌᏈ ᎭᏫᏂᏗᏢ ᎣᎩᏅᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᎦᏃᎸᎥᏍᎬᏰᏃ ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢ ᏓᏳᎦᏛᎩ.
052704.phonetic: Na-hna-no wi-o-gi-ka-do-yv Sa-qui ha-wi-ni-di-tlv o-gi-nv-s-ta-nv-gi, ga-no-lv-v-s-gv-ye-no i-gv-yi-di-tlv da-yu-ga-dv-gi.

052705.english: And when we had sailed over the sea of Cilicia and Pamphylia, we came to Myra, a city of Lycia.
052705.cherokee: ᎣᎩᎶᏐᏅᏃ ᎠᎺᏉᎯ, ᏏᎵᏏᏱ ᎠᎴ ᏆᎻᏈᎵᏱ ᏧᏳᎪᏗ, ᎵᏏᏱ ᏬᎩᎷᏨᎩ, ᎹᎵ ᏕᎤᏙᎥ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ.
052705.phonetic: O-gi-lo-so-nv-no a-me-quo-hi, Si-li-si-yi a-le Qua-mi-qui-li-yi tsu-yu-go-di, Li-si-yi wo-gi-lu-tsv-gi, Ma-li de-u-do-v ga-du-hv-i.

052706.english: And there the centurion found a ship of Alexandria sailing into Italy; and he put us therein.
052706.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎤᏴᏩᏛᎲᎩ ᏥᏳ ᎢᏓᎵ ᎠᏂᏂᏒᎩ, ᎡᎵᎩᏱ ᎡᎯ, ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᏙᎩᏯᏅᎩ.
052706.phonetic: Na-hna-no a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui di-ti-ni-do-hi u-yv-wa-dv-hv-gi tsi-yu I-da-li a-ni-ni-sv-gi, E-li-gi-yi e-hi, na-hna-no do-gi-ya-nv-gi.

052707.english: And when we had sailed slowly many days, and scarce were come over against Cnidus, the wind not suffering us, we sailed under Crete, over against Salmone;
052707.cherokee: ᎤᏬᎯᏨᏃ ᎦᏂᎳ ᎣᎩᏁᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏏᏉ ᏃᎩᎶᏐᏅᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏓ, ᏟᏗ ᎭᏫᏂᏗᏢ ᏬᎩᏅᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᏌᎳᎼᏂ ᏧᏳᎪᏗ, ᎦᏃᎸᎥᏍᎬᏰᏃ ᎣᎩᏲᏍᏙ ᏓᏁᎲᎩ.
052707.phonetic: U-wo-hi-tsv-no ga-ni-la o-gi-ne-nv, a-le a-si-quo no-gi-lo-so-nv-na ge-sv Na-da, Tli-di ha-wi-ni-di-tlv wo-gi-nv-s-ta-nv-gi, Sa-la-mo-ni tsu-yu-go-di, ga-no-lv-v-s-gv-ye-no o-gi-yo-s-do da-ne-hv-gi.

052708.english: And, hardly passing it, came unto a place which is called The fair havens; nigh whereunto was the city of Lasea.
052708.cherokee: ᎤᏎᎦᏨᎯᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᎣᎩᎶᏐᏅ, ᏬᎩᎷᏨᎩ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᏔᎳᏗᏍᏗᏱ ᏚᏙᎥᎢ, ᎾᎥ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎴᏏᏱ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ.
052708.phonetic: U-se-ga-tsv-hi-no na-hna o-gi-lo-so-nv, wo-gi-lu-tsv-gi o-s-dv Di-ta-la-di-s-di-yi du-do-v-i, na-v ga-du-hv Le-si-yi tsu-do-i-dv.

052709.english: Now when much time was spent, and when sailing was now dangerous, because the fast was now already past, Paul admonished them,
052709.cherokee: ᎤᏬᎯᏨᏃ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏰᎯᏍᏗᏳ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᏥᏳ ᎦᏂᏓᏍᏗᏱ, ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᎹᏟ ᎬᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎶᏐᏅᎢ, ᏉᎳ ᏚᏪᏯᏔᏅᎩ,
052709.phonetic: U-wo-hi-tsv-no, a-le u-na-ye-hi-s-di-yu nu-li-s-ta-nv tsi-yu ga-ni-da-s-di-yi, nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv a-ma-tli gv-di ge-sv u-lo-so-nv-i, Quo-la du-we-ya-ta-nv-gi,

052710.english: And said unto them, Sirs, I perceive that this voyage will be with hurt and much damage, not only of the lading and ship, but also of our lives.
052710.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ, ᎦᏙᎴᎣᏍᎦ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏳ ᏥᏗᏂ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏗᎦᎵᏍᏓᏁᎵᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏛ ᏗᎩᏲᎱᏎᎵᏒᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᎵᏁᏌᎸᏉ ᎠᎴ ᏥᏳ ᎤᏩᏒ, ᎾᏍᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᏕᏛᏅᎢ.
052710.phonetic: Hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; I-tsi-s-ga-ya, ga-do-le-o-s-ga hi-a na-s-gi tsi-yu tsi-di-ni go-hu-s-di di-ga-li-s-da-ne-li-sv-i, a-le u-tsa-dv di-gi-yo-hu-se-li-sv-i, v-tla a-li-ne-sa-lv-quo a-le tsi-yu u-wa-sv, na-s-quo-s-gi-ni de-dv-nv-i.

052711.english: Nevertheless the centurion believed the master and the owner of the ship, more than those things which were spoken by Paul.
052711.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎤᏟ ᏂᏚᏬᎯᏳᏒᎩ ᎧᏁᏥᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᏥᏳ ᎠᏂᏁᎬᎢ, ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᏉᎳ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎬᎢ.
052711.phonetic: A-se-no a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui di-ti-ni-do-hi u-tli ni-du-wo-hi-yu-sv-gi ka-ne-tsi-do-hi a-le u-tse-li-ga tsi-yu a-ni-ne-gv-i, e-s-ga-quo Quo-la ni-ga-we-s-gv-i.

052712.english: And because the haven was not commodious to winter in, the more part advised to depart thence also, if by any means they might attain to Phenice, and there to winter; which is an haven of Crete, and lieth toward the south west and north west.
052712.cherokee: ᏗᏔᎳᏗᏍᏗᏱᏃ ᏃᏒᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᎪᎳᏗᏴᏗᏱ, ᎤᏟ ᎢᏯᏂᎢ ᎤᏂᏁᏨᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎾᎿ ᎭᎤᏂᏴᏫᏛᏗᏱ, ᏰᎵ ᏈᏂᏏ ᏫᎦᏲᎩᏃᎯᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏫᎦᏲᎦᎪᎳᏗᏴᏗ ᏱᎩ ᎠᏁᎵᏍᎬᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᎿᎭᏟᏗ ᏗᏔᎳᏗᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏭᏕᎵᎬ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏭᎦᏘ.
052712.phonetic: Di-ta-la-di-s-di-yi-no no-sv-na ge-sv na-hna u-go-la-di-yv-di-yi, u-tli i-ya-ni-i u-ni-ne-tsv-gi na-s-quo na-hna u-ni-yv-wi-dv-di-yi, ye-li Qui-ni-si wi-ga-yo-gi-no-hi-s-di a-le wi-ga-yo-ga-go-la-di-yv-di yi-gi a-ne-li-s-gv-gi, na-s-gi na-hna Tli-di di-ta-la-di-s-di-yi, a-le na-s-gi wu-de-li-gv i-di-tlv wu-ga-ti.

052713.english: And when the south wind blew softly, supposing that they had obtained their purpose, loosing thence, they sailed close by Crete.
052713.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏧᎦᎾᏮ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏧᏃᎸᏔᏅ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎦᏚᎵᏍᎬ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏓ ᎤᏁᎵᏒ, ᎤᏂᎧᏙᏴᎩ, ᏟᏗ ᎾᎥᎢ ᎤᏂᏅᏍᏔᏅᎩ.
052713.phonetic: Hna-quo-no tsu-ga-na-wv i-di-tlv tsu-no-lv-ta-nv, na-s-gi i-ga-du-li-s-gv ni-ga-li-s-da u-ne-li-sv, u-ni-ka-do-yv-gi, Tli-di na-v-i u-ni-nv-s-ta-nv-gi.

052714.english: But not long after there arose against it a tempestuous wind, called Euroclydon.
052714.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏄᏬᎯᏨᎾᏉ ᎣᏂ, ᎤᏱᎶᎸᎩ ᎤᏃᎴ ᏳᎶᏟᏛ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ.
052714.phonetic: A-se-no nu-wo-hi-tsv-na-quo o-ni, u-yi-lo-lv-gi u-no-le Yu-lo-tli-dv tsu-do-i-dv.

052715.english: And when the ship was caught, and could not bear up into the wind, we let her drive.
052715.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎦᏃᎸᎥᏍᎬ ᎤᏂᏴᎲ ᏥᏳ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏄᎸᏅ ᎤᏃᎴ ᏧᎦᏛ ᏭᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᎧᏄᎯᏎᏍᏗ ᎣᎨᎵᏒᎩ.
052715.phonetic: Hna-quo-no ga-no-lv-v-s-gv u-ni-yv-hv tsi-yu, a-le na-s-gi u-nu-lv-nv u-no-le tsu-ga-dv wu-lo-hi-s-di-yi u-ne-la-gi ka-nu-hi-se-s-di o-ge-li-sv-gi.

052716.english: And running under a certain island which is called Clauda, we had much work to come by the boat:
052716.cherokee: ᎭᏫᏂᏗᏢᏃ ᎣᎩᏅᏍᏔᏅ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎠᎹᏰᎵ ᎤᏪᎧᎲ, ᏠᏗ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎤᏎᎦᏨᎯᏉ ᏗᎦᏲᎩᏂᏴᏗ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ ᎤᏍᏗ ᏥᏳ.
052716.phonetic: Ha-wi-ni-di-tlv-no o-gi-nv-s-ta-nv go-hu-s-di u-s-di a-ma-ye-li u-we-ka-hv, Tlo-di tsu-do-i-dv, u-se-ga-tsv-hi-quo di-ga-yo-gi-ni-yv-di nu-li-s-ta-nv-gi u-s-di tsi-yu.

052717.english: Which when they had taken up, they used helps, undergirding the ship; and, fearing lest they should fall into the quicksands, strake sail, and so were driven.
052717.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏂᏌᎳᏓᏅ, ᏗᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ ᏚᏅᏔᏅᎩ, ᎤᎾᎸᎸᎩ ᏥᏳ; ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎢᎲᏃ ᎤᏍᏓᎾᎸ ᎤᏂᏃᏠᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏓᏳᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅᎩ ᎡᎳᏗ ᏂᏚᏅᏁᎸᎩ ᏓᏰᏙᎳᏛᎢ, ᎤᏂᏄᎯᏒᏉᏃ.
052717.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-ni-sa-la-da-nv, di-li-s-de-lv-do-di du-nv-ta-nv-gi, u-na-lv-lv-gi tsi-yu; a-ni-s-ga-i-hv-no u-s-da-na-lv u-ni-no-tlo-hi-s-di-yi na-s-gi nv-da-yu-li-s-do-ta-nv-gi e-la-di ni-du-nv-ne-lv-gi da-ye-do-la-dv-i, u-ni-nu-hi-sv-quo-no.

052718.english: And we being exceedingly tossed with a tempest, the next day they lightened the ship;

052719.english: And the third day we cast out with our own hands the tackling of the ship.
052719.cherokee: ᏦᎢᏁᏃ ᎢᎦ ᎣᎬᏒ ᏦᎪᏰᏂ ᏙᎬᏔᏅᎩ, ᎣᎦᏘᏅᏒᎩ ᎤᏤᎵᎦᏯ ᎤᎵᏁᏌᎸ ᏥᏳ.
052719.phonetic: Tso-i-ne-no i-ga o-gv-sv tso-go-ye-ni do-gv-ta-nv-gi, o-ga-ti-nv-sv-gi u-tse-li-ga-ya u-li-ne-sa-lv tsi-yu.

052720.english: And when neither sun nor stars in many days appeared, and no small tempest lay on us, all hope that we should be saved was then taken away.
052720.cherokee: ᎤᏬᎯᏨᏃ ᎾᏂᎾᏄᎪᎬᎾ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᏅᏙ ᎠᎴ ᏃᏈᏏ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏲᎵᏉ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎣᎩᏃᎸᏅ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᎦᏲᎦᏛᏂᏗᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏚᎩ ᎣᎬᏒ ᎤᎵᏛᏔᏅᎩ.
052720.phonetic: U-wo-hi-tsv-no na-ni-na-nu-go-gv-na nu-li-s-ta-nv nv-do a-le no-qui-si, a-le ga-yo-li-quo ni-ge-sv-na o-gi-no-lv-nv, hna-quo ga-yo-ga-dv-ni-di-s-di ge-sv u-du-gi o-gv-sv u-li-dv-ta-nv-gi.

052721.english: But after long abstinence Paul stood forth in the midst of them, and said, Sirs, ye should have hearkened unto me, and not have loosed from Crete, and to have gained this harm and loss.
052721.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎪᎯᏗᏳ ᎦᏲᎦᎵᏍᏓᏴᏅᎯ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᏉᎳ ᏚᎴᏅᎩ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎤᎾᏣᎥᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ, ᏱᏍᎩᏲᎢᏳᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏅᏗᏥᎧᏙᏴᎾᏉ ᏱᎨᏎ ᏟᏗ, ᎥᏝ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎤᏐᏅ ᏱᏂᎨᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᎴ ᎠᎴ ᏱᎨᏥᎪᎶᎯᏎᎴᎢ.
052721.phonetic: Hna-quo-no go-hi-di-yu ga-yo-ga-li-s-da-yv-nv-hi nu-li-s-ta-nv Quo-la du-le-nv-gi a-ye-li u-na-tsa-v-i, a-le hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; I-tsi-s-ga-ya, yi-s-gi-yo-i-yu-ne-i, a-le nv-di-tsi-ka-do-yv-na-quo yi-ge-se Tli-di, v-tla na-s-gi hi-a u-so-nv yi-ni-ge-tsa-li-s-da-ne-le a-le yi-ge-tsi-go-lo-hi-se-le-i.

052722.english: And now I exhort you to be of good cheer: for there shall be no loss of any man's life among you, but of the ship.
052722.cherokee: ᎾᏉᏃ ᎢᏨᏔᏲᏎᎭ ᎤᎦᎵᏍᏗ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏗᏱ; ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᏥᏂᏣᏛᏅ ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶᎬᏅ ᏴᎬᏩᏲᎱᏏ, ᏥᏳ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏓᎩᏲᎱᏎᎵ.
052722.phonetic: na-quo-no i-tsv-ta-yo-se-ha u-ga-li-s-di i-tsa-da-nv-da-di-s-di-yi; hi-a-ye-no tsi-ni-tsa-dv-nv v-tla gi-lo-gv-nv yv-gv-wa-yo-hu-si, tsi-yu u-wa-sv da-gi-yo-hu-se-li.

052723.english: For there stood by me this night the angel of God, whose I am, and whom I serve,
052723.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏰᏃ ᏒᏃᏱ ᏥᎨᏒᎩ ᎤᎶᏗᏢ ᎠᏆᎴᏁᎸ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏴ ᎠᎩᎾᏝᎢ ᏥᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏕᏥᎦᎿᎭᏩᏕᎦ,
052723.phonetic: Go-hi-ye-no sv-no-yi tsi-ge-sv-gi u-lo-di-tlv a-qua-le-ne-lv di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga, na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi a-yv a-gi-na-tla-i tsi-gi a-le na-s-gi tsi-de-tsi-ga-hna-wa-de-ga,

052724.english: Saying, Fear not, Paul; thou must be brought before Caesar: and, lo, God hath given thee all them that sail with thee.
052724.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏍᎦᎢᎮᏍᏗ, ᏉᎳ, ᏏᏌ ᎤᏬᎸ ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢ ᎠᏎ ᏔᎴᏂ; ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎦᏣᎧᏁᎸ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏥᏳᎯ ᏥᏣᏣᎠ.
052724.phonetic: Hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Tle-s-di yi-s-ga-i-he-s-di, Quo-la, Si-sa u-wo-lv i-gv-yi-di-tlv a-se ta-le-ni; gv-ni-yu-quo-no U-ne-la-nv-hi ga-tsa-ka-ne-lv ni-ga-dv tsi-yu-hi tsi-tsa-tsa-a.

052725.english: Wherefore, sirs, be of good cheer: for I believe God, that it shall be even as it was told me.
052725.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ, ᎤᎦᎵᏍᏗ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏓᏓ; ᏥᏲᎢᏳᎲᏍᎦᏰᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᎾᎩᏪᏎᎸ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
052725.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di i-tsi-s-ga-ya, u-ga-li-s-di i-tsa-da-nv-da-da; tsi-yo-i-yu-hv-s-ga-ye-no U-ne-la-nv-hi, na-gi-we-se-lv na-s-gi-ya i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi.

052726.english: Howbeit we must be cast upon a certain island.
052726.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎠᎹᏰᎵ ᎤᏪᎧᎲ ᏓᎩᏝᏗᏤᎵ.
052726.phonetic: A-se-s-gi-ni-no-nv go-hu-s-di u-s-di a-ma-ye-li u-we-ka-hv da-gi-tla-di-tse-li.

052727.english: But when the fourteenth night was come, as we were driven up and down in Adria, about midnight the shipmen deemed that they drew near to some country;
052727.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏚᏏᏁᏃ ᎤᎵᏏᏅ, ᎠᎺᏉᎯ ᎡᏟᏯ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᎣᎩᏃᎸᏔᏂᏙᎲᎢ, ᏒᏃᏱ ᎠᏰᎵ ᏥᏳ ᎠᏂᏂᏙᎯ ᎢᎸᎯᏢ ᎦᏙᎯ ᎾᎥᏂᎨ ᎣᏥᏂ ᎤᏁᎵᏒᎩ.
052727.phonetic: Ni-ga-du-si-ne-no u-li-si-nv, a-me-quo-hi E-tli-ya tsu-do-i-dv o-gi-no-lv-ta-ni-do-hv-i, sv-no-yi a-ye-li tsi-yu a-ni-ni-do-hi i-lv-hi-tlv ga-do-hi na-v-ni-ge o-tsi-ni u-ne-li-sv-gi.

052728.english: And sounded, and found it twenty fathoms: and when they had gone a little further, they sounded again, and found it fifteen fathoms.
052728.cherokee: ᎾᏍᏛᎬᏃ ᎤᎾᏟᎶᎥ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᏒᎩ ᏔᎳᏍᎪᎯ ᎢᎪᎷᏅᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎤᏍᏗᎩᏛᏃ ᎤᏗᏢᏍᏙᏗ ᎢᎤᏁᏅ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎤᎾᏟᎶᎥᎩ, ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᏒᎩ ᎯᏍᎩᎦᏚᏉ ᎢᎪᎷᏅᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
052728.phonetic: Na-s-dv-gv-no u-na-tli-lo-v u-na-do-le-ho-sv-gi ta-la-s-go-hi i-go-lu-nv-di ge-sv-i; u-s-di-gi-dv-no u-di-tlv-s-do-di i-u-ne-nv ta-li-ne u-na-tli-lo-v-gi, u-na-do-le-ho-sv-gi hi-s-gi-ga-du-quo i-go-lu-nv-di ge-sv-i.

052729.english: Then fearing lest we should have fallen upon rocks, they cast four anchors out of the stern, and wished for the day.
052729.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᏂᏍᎦᎸ ᏅᏲᎯ ᎤᏂᏝᏗᏤᏗᏱ, ᎣᏂᏗᏢ ᏫᏚᎾᏗᏅᏒᎩ ᏅᎩ ᏔᎷᎩᏍᎩ ᏥᏳ ᏗᎦᎾᎯᏍᏙᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏚᎸᎲᎩ ᎢᎦ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
052729.phonetic: Hna-quo-no u-ni-s-ga-lv nv-yo-hi u-ni-tla-di-tse-di-yi, o-ni-di-tlv wi-du-na-di-nv-sv-gi nv-gi ta-lu-gi-s-gi tsi-yu di-ga-na-hi-s-do-di, a-le u-na-du-lv-hv-gi i-ga i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi.

052730.english: And as the shipmen were about to flee out of the ship, when they had let down the boat into the sea, under colour as though they would have cast anchors out of the foreship,
052730.cherokee: ᏥᏳᏃ ᎠᏂᏂᏙᎯ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᏍᏗ ᏥᏳ ᎠᏂᏔᎳᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᎾᏠᎾᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᏔᎷᎩᏍᎩ ᏥᏳ ᏗᎦᎾᎯᏍᏙᏗ ᎢᎬᏴᏗᏢ ᏥᏫᏙᏧᎾᏕᏏᏐ ᎤᏠᏱ ᎾᎾᏛᏁᎲᎢ.
052730.phonetic: Tsi-yu-no a-ni-ni-do-hi u-na-li-s-di-yi u-ni-ye-lv-nv, a-le hna-quo u-s-di tsi-yu a-ni-ta-la-di-s-gv-i, a-na-tlo-na-s-di-s-gv ta-lu-gi-s-gi tsi-yu di-ga-na-hi-s-do-di i-gv-yv-di-tlv tsi-wi-do-tsu-na-de-si-so u-tlo-yi na-na-dv-ne-hv-i.

052731.english: Paul said to the centurion and to the soldiers, Except these abide in the ship, ye cannot be saved.
052731.cherokee: ᏉᎳ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏳᎯ ᏄᎾᏣᎥᎾ ᏱᎩ ᎥᏝ ᏴᎨᏣᏛᏂᏓ.
052731.phonetic: Quo-la hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui di-ti-ni-do-hi a-le a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi; I-yu-no hi-a na-s-gi tsi-yu-hi nu-na-tsa-v-na yi-gi v-tla yv-ge-tsa-dv-ni-da.

052732.english: Then the soldiers cut off the ropes of the boat, and let her fall off.
052732.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᏚᏂᎦᎵᏒᎩ ᎠᏍᏕᏱᏛ ᎤᏍᏗ ᏥᏳ ᎦᏙᏌᏗᏍᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏬᎰᏒᎩ.
052732.phonetic: Hna-quo-no a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi du-ni-ga-li-sv-gi a-s-de-yi-dv u-s-di tsi-yu ga-do-sa-di-s-dv-i, a-le na-s-gi u-wo-ho-sv-gi.

052733.english: And while the day was coming on, Paul besought them all to take meat, saying, This day is the fourteenth day that ye have tarried and continued fasting, having taken nothing.
052733.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏃ ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏔᏂᏒᎩ, ᏉᎳ ᏚᏔᏲᏎᎸᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎪᎯ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏂᎦᏚᏏᏁ ᎢᎦ ᏥᏥᎦᏘᏴ ᎠᎴ ᏂᏣᎵᏍᏓᏴᏍᎬᎾ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏂᏥᎩᏍᎬᎾ ᏥᎩ.
052733.phonetic: I-ga-no nv-da-ga-li-s-ta-ni-sv-gi, Quo-la du-ta-yo-se-lv-gi ni-ga-dv u-na-li-s-da-yv-di-yi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Go-hi hna-quo ni-ga-du-si-ne i-ga tsi-tsi-ga-ti-yv a-le ni-tsa-li-s-da-yv-s-gv-na go-hu-s-di ni-tsi-gi-s-gv-na tsi-gi.

052734.english: Wherefore I pray you to take some meat: for this is for your health: for there shall not an hair fall from the head of any of you.
052734.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏨᏔᏲᏎᎭ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ; ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᏥᏂᏣᏛᏅ ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᏌᏉᎤᏅ ᎤᏍᏘᏰᏅᎯ ᎠᏓᎦᏙᎠᏏ.
052734.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di i-tsv-ta-yo-se-ha i-tsa-li-s-da-yv-di-yi, na-s-gi-ye-no hi-a i-tsa-li-s-de-lv-do-di; hi-a-ye-no tsi-ni-tsa-dv-nv v-tla gi-lo sa-quo-u-nv u-s-ti-ye-nv-hi a-da-ga-do-a-si.

052735.english: And when he had thus spoken, he took bread, and gave thanks to God in presence of them all: and when he had broken it, he began to eat.
052735.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏪᏒ, ᎦᏚ ᎤᎩᏒᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏤᎸᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲᎢ, ᎤᎬᎭᎷᏴᏃ ᎤᎴᏅᎲᎩ ᎤᎵᏍᏓᏴᏅᎩ.
052735.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nu-we-sv, ga-du u-gi-sv-gi a-le u-li-he-li-tse-lv-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi ni-ga-dv a-ni-ga-ta-hv-i, u-gv-ha-lu-yv-no u-le-nv-hv-gi u-li-s-da-yv-nv-gi.

052736.english: Then were they all of good cheer, and they also took some meat.
052736.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᎦᎵᏍᏗᏳ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏓᏛᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏅᎩ.
052736.phonetic: Hna-quo-no ni-ga-dv u-ga-li-s-di-yu u-na-da-nv-da-dv-gi, a-le na-s-quo u-na-li-s-da-yv-nv-gi.

052737.english: And we were in all in the ship two hundred threescore and sixteen souls.
052737.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎣᎦᏣᎢ ᏔᎵᏧᏈ ᎦᎵᏆᏍᎪᎯ ᏑᏓᎵᎦᎵ ᏃᏥᎥᎩ ᏴᏫ.
052737.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv-no tsi-yu-hi o-ga-tsa-i ta-li-tsu-qui ga-li-qua-s-go-hi su-da-li-ga-li no-tsi-v-gi yv-wi.

052738.english: And when they had eaten enough, they lightened the ship, and cast out the wheat into the sea.
052738.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏰᎵ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏅ, ᎤᎾᏓᏌᎧᎲᏍᏔᏅᎩ ᏥᏳ, ᎤᏣᎴᏍᏗ ᎠᎺᏉᎯ ᏭᎾᏗᏅᏒᎩ.
052738.phonetic: Hna-quo-no ye-li u-na-li-s-da-yv-nv, u-na-da-sa-ka-hv-s-ta-nv-gi tsi-yu, u-tsa-le-s-di a-me-quo-hi wu-na-di-nv-sv-gi.

052739.english: And when it was day, they knew not the land: but they discovered a certain creek with a shore, into the which they were minded, if it were possible, to thrust in the ship.
052739.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎢᎦ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᎥᏝ ᏳᏃᎵᏤ ᎾᎿᎭᎦᏙᎯ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏂᎪᎲ ᎠᎺᏉᎯ ᏭᏩᏂᎦᎸᎢ, ᏰᎵ ᎬᏚᎩᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᎿ ᎭᏭᏂᏅᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᏥᏳ ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏅᎩ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏰᎵ ᏱᎩ.
052739.phonetic: Hna-quo-no i-ga nu-li-s-ta-nv v-tla yu-no-li-tse na-hna ga-do-hi; a-se-no u-ni-go-hv a-me-quo-hi wu-wa-ni-ga-lv-i, ye-li gv-du-gi-s-di ge-sv-i, na-hna wu-ni-nv-s-do-di-yi tsi-yu u-ni-ye-lv-nv-gi, i-yu-no ye-li yi-gi.

052740.english: And when they had taken up the anchors, they committed themselves unto the sea, and loosed the rudder bands, and hoised up the mainsail to the wind, and made toward shore.
052740.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏚᏂᎦᎵᏒ ᏕᎦᏙᏌᏗᏍᏛ ᏔᎷᎩᏍᎩ ᏥᏳ ᏗᎦᎾᎯᏍᏙᏗ, ᎠᎺᏉᎯ ᏚᏂᏲᎯᏎᎸᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏂᎧᏁᏴᎲᎩ ᎦᏌᏛᏍᏗ ᏕᎦᎸᏍᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏃᎸᎥᏍᎬᎢ ᎤᏂᏰᏙᎳᏛᏅᎩ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎠᏰᏙᎳᏛᏗ; ᏙᏱᏃ ᏫᏚᏄᎪᏔᏅᎩ.
052740.phonetic: Hna-quo-no du-ni-ga-li-sv de-ga-do-sa-di-s-dv ta-lu-gi-s-gi tsi-yu di-ga-na-hi-s-do-di, a-me-quo-hi du-ni-yo-hi-se-lv-gi, a-le du-ni-ka-ne-yv-hv-gi ga-sa-dv-s-di de-ga-lv-s-dv-i, a-le ga-no-lv-v-s-gv-i u-ni-ye-do-la-dv-nv-gi u-s-di a-ye-do-la-dv-di; do-yi-no wi-du-nu-go-ta-nv-gi.

052741.english: And falling into a place where two seas met, they ran the ship aground; and the forepart stuck fast, and remained unmoveable, but the hinder part was broken with the violence of the waves.
052741.cherokee: ᏔᎵᏃ ᎠᎺᏉᎯ ᏕᎨᏒ ᏭᎾᎵᏱᎶᎸ, ᏥᏳ ᎤᏂᏝᏗᏤᎸᎩ ᎦᏙᎯ; ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢᏃ ᎠᏍᏓᏱᏳ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ, ᎥᏝ ᏰᎵ ᎬᏩᎵᏖᎸᏗ ᏱᎨᏎᎢ, ᎣᏂᏗᏢᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏲᏨᎩ ᏅᏓᏳᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅᎩ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏗᏳ ᏓᎵᏍᏗᎳᏁᎬ ᎠᎺᏉᎯ.
052741.phonetic: Ta-li-no a-me-quo-hi de-ge-sv wu-na-li-yi-lo-lv, tsi-yu u-ni-tla-di-tse-lv-gi ga-do-hi; i-gv-yi-di-tlv-no a-s-da-yi-yu nu-li-s-ta-nv, v-tla ye-li gv-wa-li-te-lv-di yi-ge-se-i, o-ni-di-tlv-s-gi-ni u-yo-tsv-gi nv-da-yu-li-s-do-ta-nv-gi u-li-ni-gi-di-yu da-li-s-di-la-ne-gv a-me-quo-hi.

052742.english: And the soldiers' counsel was to kill the prisoners, lest any of them should swim out, and escape.
052742.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩᏃ ᏧᏂᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏖᎸᎩ ᎠᏂᏴᎩ; ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᏯᏴᎢᏄᎦ ᏯᏚᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏯᎵᏗ ᎠᏁᎵᏍᎬᎩ.
052742.phonetic: A-ni-ya-wi-s-gi-no tsu-ni-hi-s-di-yi u-na-da-nv-te-lv-gi a-ni-yv-gi; gi-lo-ye-no ya-yv-i-nu-ga ya-du-gi a-le ya-li-di a-ne-li-s-gv-gi.

052743.english: But the centurion, willing to save Paul, kept them from their purpose; and commanded that they which could swim should cast themselves first into the sea, and get to land:
052743.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᏚᏲᏍᏙᏓᏁᎸᎩ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬ ᏅᏓᏳᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅᎩ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎬ ᎤᏍᏕᎸᏗᏱ ᏉᎳ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏁᏤᎸᎩ ᏰᎵ ᎬᏩᎾᏴᎢᏅᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ, ᎢᎬᏱ ᎤᎾᎵᏔᏗᏅᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᏭᎾᏚᎩᏍᏗᏱ,
052743.phonetic: A-se-no a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui di-ti-ni-do-hi du-yo-s-do-da-ne-lv-gi nu-s-dv a-na-da-nv-te-s-gv nv-da-yu-li-s-do-ta-nv-gi u-du-li-s-gv u-s-de-lv-di-yi Quo-la, a-le du-ne-tse-lv-gi ye-li gv-wa-na-yv-i-nv-s-di ge-sv, i-gv-yi u-na-li-ta-di-nv-di-yi a-le wu-na-du-gi-s-di-yi,

052744.english: And the rest, some on boards, and some on broken pieces of the ship. And so it came to pass, that they escaped all safe to land.
052744.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏐᎢᏃ ᎢᎦᏛ ᏧᏯᏕᎾ ᏧᎾᎩᎸᏙᏗᏱ, ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᏥᏳ ᏧᎵᏢᎸᏛ.  ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᎾᏛᏂᏛᎩ ᎤᎾᏚᎩᏒᎩ.
052744.phonetic: A-ni-so-i-no i-ga-dv tsu-ya-de-na tsu-na-gi-lv-do-di-yi, i-ga-dv-no tsi-yu tsu-li-tlv-lv-dv.  A-le na-s-gi nu-li-s-ta-nv-gi, ni-ga-dv u-na-dv-ni-dv-gi u-na-du-gi-sv-gi.

052801.english: And when they were escaped, then they knew that the island was called Melita.
052801.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏛᏂᏛᏃ ᎩᎳ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᏒᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎹᏰᎵ ᎻᎳᏗ ᏚᏙᎥᎢ,
052801.phonetic: U-na-dv-ni-dv-no gi-la u-na-do-le-ho-sv-gi na-s-gi a-ma-ye-li Mi-la-di du-do-v-i,

052802.english: And the barbarous people shewed us no little kindness: for they kindled a fire, and received us every one, because of the present rain, and because of the cold.
052802.cherokee: ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏁᎯ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎪᎩᏍᏆᏂᎪᏔᏅᎩ; ᎤᏃᏔᏅᏰᏃ ᏕᎪᎦᏓᏂᎸᏨᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ, ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᎠᎦᏍᎬᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏴᏢᎢ.
052802.phonetic: Nv-wa-na-da-le-no yv-wi na-hna a-ne-hi u-tsa-ti o-si-yu go-gi-s-qua-ni-go-ta-nv-gi; u-no-ta-nv-ye-no de-go-ga-da-ni-lv-tsv-gi ni-ga-dv, nv-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-gi a-ga-s-gv-i a-le u-yv-tlv-i.

052803.english: And when Paul had gathered a bundle of sticks, and laid them on the fire, there came a viper out of the heat, and fastened on his hand.
052803.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᏫᏚᏭᏕᏒ ᏏᏄᎩᏝᏅᎯ ᎠᏓ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎲᏔᏅ, ᎢᎾᏛ ᎤᏗᎴᎬ ᏓᏳᎾᏄᎪᏨᎩ ᎤᏬᏰᏂ ᎤᏪᏖᎳᏛᏨᎩ.
052803.phonetic: Quo-la-no wi-du-wu-de-sv si-nu-gi-tla-nv-hi a-da, a-le du-hv-ta-nv, i-na-dv u-di-le-gv da-yu-na-nu-go-tsv-gi u-wo-ye-ni u-we-te-la-dv-tsv-gi.

052804.english: And when the barbarians saw the venomous beast hang on his hand, they said among themselves, No doubt this man is a murderer, whom, though he hath escaped the sea, yet vengeance suffereth not to live.
052804.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏂᎪᎲ ᎢᎾᏛ ᎦᏖᎳᏛ ᎤᏬᏰᏂ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᎾᏓᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎥᏝ ᏳᏝᏏᏛᎭ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏴᏫ ᏗᎯᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎺᏉᎯ ᎠᏗᏫᏏ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏞᎩ ᎥᏝ ᎬᏁᏍᏗ ᏳᏪᎵᏎᎭ.
052804.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nv-wa-na-da-le yv-wi u-ni-go-hv i-na-dv ga-te-la-dv u-wo-ye-ni, hi-a ni-du-na-da-we-se-lv-gi; v-tla yu-tla-si-dv-ha hi-a a-s-ga-ya yv-wi di-hi-hi ge-sv-i; u-do-hi-yu-hi-s-gi-ni-no-nv na-s-gi a-me-quo-hi a-di-wi-si, a-se-no u-tle-gi v-tla gv-ne-s-di yu-we-li-se-ha.

052805.english: And he shook off the beast into the fire, and felt no harm.
052805.cherokee: ᎠᏥᎸᏱᏃ ᏭᏙᎥᏔᏅᎩ ᎢᎾᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏳᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ.
052805.phonetic: A-tsi-lv-yi-no wu-do-v-ta-nv-gi i-na-dv, a-le v-tla go-hu-s-di yu-li-s-ta-ne-i.

052806.english: Howbeit they looked when he should have swollen, or fallen down dead suddenly: but after they had looked a great while, and saw no harm come to him, they changed their minds, and said that he was a god.
052806.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᎾᎦᏙᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᏓᎪᏘᏏ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎯ ᏓᎦᏅᏥ ᏛᏰᎶᎢᏍᏔᏂ, ᎠᏁᎵᏍᎬᎩ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏬᎯᏨ ᎤᎾᎦᏙᏍᏔᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏄᎵᏍᏓᏁᎸᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᏂᏁᏟᏴᏒᎩ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ, ᎤᎾᏛᏅᎩ.
052806.phonetic: A-se-no u-na-ga-do-s-ta-nv-gi, da-go-ti-si a-le u-yo-hu-sv-hi da-ga-nv-tsi dv-ye-lo-i-s-ta-ni, a-ne-li-s-gv-gi; a-se-no u-wo-hi-tsv u-na-ga-do-s-ta-nv, a-le u-na-do-le-ho-sv go-hu-s-di nu-li-s-da-ne-lv-na ge-sv-i, u-ni-ne-tli-yv-sv-gi nu-s-dv a-na-da-nv-te-s-gv-i, a-le ga-lv-la-di e-hi, u-na-dv-nv-gi.

052807.english: In the same quarters were possessions of the chief man of the island, whose name was Publius; who received us, and lodged us three days courteously.
052807.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎾᎥ ᏚᎶᎨᏒᎩ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᎹᏰᎵ ᎤᏪᎧᎲᎢ, ᏆᏟᏯ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏙᎦᏓᏂᎸᏨᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏦᎢ ᏧᏒᎯᏛ ᎤᏙᎵᏍᏙᏏᏳ ᎣᎩᏍᏆᏂᎪᏔᏅᎩ ᎦᏁᎸᎢ.
052807.phonetic: Na-hna-no na-v du-lo-ge-sv-gi nu-gv-wi-yu-sv a-s-ga-ya na-hna a-ma-ye-li u-we-ka-hv-i, Qua-tli-ya tsu-do-i-dv; na-s-gi do-ga-da-ni-lv-tsv-gi, a-le tso-i tsu-sv-hi-dv u-do-li-s-do-si-yu o-gi-s-qua-ni-go-ta-nv-gi ga-ne-lv-i.

052808.english: And it came to pass, that the father of Publius lay sick of a fever and of a bloody flux: to whom Paul entered in, and prayed, and laid his hands on him, and healed him.
052808.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏆᏟᏯ ᎤᏙᏓ ᎦᏅᎬᎩ ᎤᏗᎴᎲᏍᎬᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎬ ᏚᏩᎫᏍᎬᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏉᎳ ᎤᏴᎸᎩ ᎦᏅᎬᎢ, ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏅᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏏᏔᏛ ᎤᏅᏩᏅᎩ.
052808.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-nv-gi, na-s-gi Qua-tli-ya u-do-da ga-nv-gv-gi u-di-le-hv-s-gv-gi a-le gi-gv du-wa-gu-s-gv-gi, na-s-gi Quo-la u-yv-lv-gi ga-nv-gv-i, u-da-do-li-s-ta-nv-no a-le du-si-ta-dv u-nv-wa-nv-gi.

052809.english: So when this was done, others also, which had diseases in the island, came, and were healed:
052809.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ, ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏧᏂᏢᎩ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᎹᏰᎵ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎤᏂᎷᏨᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᎨᏥᏅᏩᏅᎩ;
052809.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nu-li-s-ta-nv, nv-wa-na-da-le na-s-quo tsu-ni-tlv-gi na-hna a-ma-ye-li a-ne-hi u-ni-lu-tsv-gi, a-le de-ge-tsi-nv-wa-nv-gi;

052810.english: Who also honoured us with many honours; and when we departed, they laded us with such things as were necessary.
052810.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎪᎩᎸᏉᏗᏍᎬᎢ ᎤᏣᏛᎩ ᎪᎩᏁᎸᏅᎩ; ᎢᎣᎦᏂᎩᏒᏃ ᎪᎦᎵᏁᏌᎳᏁᎸᎩ ᏧᏓᎴᎳᏅᏛ ᎣᎦᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ.
052810.phonetic: Na-s-gi na-s-quo go-gi-lv-quo-di-s-gv-i u-tsa-dv-gi go-gi-ne-lv-nv-gi; i-o-ga-ni-gi-sv-no go-ga-li-ne-sa-la-ne-lv-gi tsu-da-le-la-nv-dv o-ga-li-s-de-lv-do-di.

052811.english: And after three months we departed in a ship of Alexandria, which had wintered in the isle, whose sign was Castor and Pollux.
052811.cherokee: ᏦᎢᏃ ᎢᏯᏅᏙ ᎤᎶᏐᏅ ᎣᎦᏂᎩᏒᎩ, ᎣᎦᏣᏅᎩ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎡᎵᎩᏱ ᎡᎯ, ᎾᎿᎭᎠᎹᏰᎵ ᏏᎪᎳᏗᏱᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᏔᎳᏛᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎪᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᎧᏍᏓ ᎠᎴ ᏉᎳᏥ ᎪᏪᎸᎩ.
052811.phonetic: Tso-i-no i-ya-nv-do u-lo-so-nv o-ga-ni-gi-sv-gi, o-ga-tsa-nv-gi tsi-yu-hi E-li-gi-yi e-hi, na-hna a-ma-ye-li si-go-la-di-yi-s-gi u-ni-ta-la-dv-hi, na-s-gi go-li-s-do-di Ka-s-da a-le Quo-la-tsi go-we-lv-gi.

052812.english: And landing at Syracuse, we tarried there three days.

052813.english: And from thence we fetched a compass, and came to Rhegium: and after one day the south wind blew, and we came the next day to Puteoli:
052813.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᏫᎣᎨᏅ ᎠᎪᎸᏛ ᏬᎩᏅᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᎵᏥᏯ ᏬᎩᎷᏨᎩ.  ᏑᏙᏓᏆᏛᏃ ᎤᎶᏐᏅ ᏧᎦᎾᏮ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏓᏳᏃᎸᏔᏅᎩ; ᎤᎩᏨᏛᏃ ᏇᏗᎣᎵ ᏬᎩᎷᏨᎩ.
052813.phonetic: Na-hna-no wi-o-ge-nv a-go-lv-dv wo-gi-nv-s-ta-nv-gi, Li-tsi-ya wo-gi-lu-tsv-gi.  Su-do-da-qua-dv-no u-lo-so-nv tsu-ga-na-wv i-di-tlv da-yu-no-lv-ta-nv-gi; u-gi-tsv-dv-no Que-di-o-li wo-gi-lu-tsv-gi.

052814.english: Where we found brethren, and were desired to tarry with them seven days: and so we went toward Rome.
052814.cherokee: ᎾᎿ ᎭᏙᏥᏩᏛᎲᎩ ᎣᏣᏓᏅᏟ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᎩᏔᏲᏎᎸᎩ ᎦᎸᏉᎩ ᏧᏒᎯᏛ ᏓᏂᏁᎸ ᎣᎦᏅᏗᏱ. ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎶᎻ ᏫᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᏬᎩᎶᏒᎩ.
052814.phonetic: Na-hna do-tsi-wa-dv-hv-gi o-tsa-da-nv-tli, a-le go-gi-ta-yo-se-lv-gi ga-lv-quo-gi tsu-sv-hi-dv da-ni-ne-lv o-ga-nv-di-yi.  Hna-quo-no Lo-mi wi-du-yu-go-dv wo-gi-lo-sv-gi.

052815.english: And from thence, when the brethren heard of us, they came to meet us as far as Appii forum, and The three taverns: whom when Paul saw, he thanked God, and took courage.
052815.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏟ ᏗᎪᎦᏛᎦᏅ, ᎡᏈᏲᎢ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏦᎢ-ᎢᏗᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ ᏕᎪᎦᏠᏏᎸᎩ; ᏉᎳ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᎪᎲ ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏤᎸᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎦᎵᏍᏗ ᎤᏓᏅᏓᏛᎩ.
052815.phonetic: Na-hna-no a-na-da-nv-tli di-go-ga-dv-ga-nv, E-qui-yo-i i-yv-dv a-le Tso-i--I-di-li-s-da-yv-di-yi de-go-ga-tlo-si-lv-gi; Quo-la na-s-gi du-go-hv u-li-he-li-tse-lv-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi, a-le u-ga-li-s-di u-da-nv-da-dv-gi.

052816.english: And when we came to Rome, the centurion delivered the prisoners to the captain of the guard: but Paul was suffered to dwell by himself with a soldier that kept him.
052816.cherokee: ᎶᎻᏃ ᏬᎩᎷᏨ, ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎠᏂᏴᎩ ᏚᏲᎯᏎᎸᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᎠᏂᎦᏘᏯ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ; ᏉᎳᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᎦᎵᏍᎪᎸᏁᎸᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎤᏕᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᎤᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗᏱ.
052816.phonetic: Lo-mi-no wo-gi-lu-tsv, a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui di-ti-ni-do-hi a-ni-yv-gi du-yo-hi-se-lv-gi u-gv-wi-yu-hi-a-ni-ga-ti-ya di-ti-ni-do-hi; Quo-la-s-gi-ni a-ga-li-s-go-lv-ne-lv-gi u-ne-la-gi u-wa-sv u-de-di-yi, a-le a-ya-wi-s-gi u-ga-ti-di-s-di-yi.

052817.english: And it came to pass, that after three days Paul called the chief of the Jews together: and when they were come together, he said unto them, Men and brethren, though I have committed nothing against the people, or customs of our fathers, yet was I delivered prisoner from Jerusalem into the hands of the Romans.
052817.cherokee: ᏦᎢᏃ ᏫᏄᏒᎸ ᎯᎠ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ; ᏉᎳ ᏚᏪᏟᏌᏅᎩ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ; ᎤᎾᏓᏟᏌᏅᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ ᎢᏓᏓᏅᏟ, ᎠᏴ ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎦᏥᏍᎦᏅᏤᎸᎯ ᏱᎩ ᏗᎦᏤᎵ ᏴᏫ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎩᏲᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᏱᎩ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᎨᎩᏁᎸᎯ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏥᏴᎩ ᏓᎬᏆᏘᎾᏫᏛᎲᎩ ᏕᎬᎩᏲᏒᎩ ᎠᏂᎶᎻᏱ.
052817.phonetic: Tso-i-no wi-nu-sv-lv hi-a nu-li-s-ta-nv-gi; Quo-la du-we-tli-sa-nv-gi nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv A-ni-tsu-si; u-na-da-tli-sa-nv-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; I-tsi-s-ga-ya i-da-da-nv-tli, a-yv v-tla go-hu-s-di ga-tsi-s-ga-nv-tse-lv-hi yi-gi di-ga-tse-li yv-wi, v-tla a-le a-gi-yo-s-ta-nv-hi yi-gi di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge ge-gi-ne-lv-hi; a-se-no Tsi-lu-si-li-mi tsi-yv-gi da-gv-qua-ti-na-wi-dv-hv-gi de-gv-gi-yo-sv-gi A-ni-lo-mi-yi.

052818.english: Who, when they had examined me, would have let me go, because there was no cause of death in me.
052818.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᎩᎪᎵᏰᎥ, ᏱᏗᎬᎩᏲᏎᎢ, ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎠᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᎬᎩᏍᏛᏗᏍᏗ ᏱᎬᎩᏩᏛᎡᎴᎢ.
052818.phonetic: Na-s-gi gv-gi-go-li-ye-v, yi-di-gv-gi-yo-se-i, v-tla-ye-no go-hu-s-di a-yo-hu-hi-s-di gv-gi-s-dv-di-s-di yi-gv-gi-wa-dv-e-le-i.

052819.english: But when the Jews spake against it, I was constrained to appeal unto Caesar; not that I had ought to accuse my nation of.
052819.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎠᏂᏁᎬ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᏐᏅᏤᎸ, ᎠᏎ ᏏᏐᎢ ᏩᎩᎲᏍᏙᏗ ᎾᏆᎵᏍᏓᏁᎸᎩ; ᎥᏝᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏯᎩᎮᎢ ᎦᏥᏳᎢᏍᏙᏗ ᏗᏆᏤᎵᎦ ᏴᏫ.
052819.phonetic: A-se-no A-ni-tsu-si a-ni-ne-gv na-s-gi u-ni-so-nv-tse-lv, a-se Si-so-i wa-gi-hv-s-do-di na-qua-li-s-da-ne-lv-gi; v-tla-s-gi-ni-no-nv go-hu-s-di ya-gi-he-i ga-tsi-yu-i-s-do-di di-qua-tse-li-ga yv-wi.

052820.english: For this cause therefore have I called for you, to see you, and to speak with you: because that for the hope of Israel I am bound with this chain.
052820.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ ᏥᏫᏨᏯᏅ ᎢᏨᎪᏩᏛᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎢᎦᎵᏃᎮᏗᏱ, ᎢᏏᎵᏰᏃ ᎤᏚᎩ ᎤᏅᏒᎢ ᏅᏧᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅ ᎯᎠ ᏧᏓᏕᏒᏛ ᏨᏆᎸᏍᏗ.
052820.phonetic: Na-s-gi nv-da-ga-li-s-do-da tsi-wi-tsv-ya-nv i-tsv-go-wa-dv-di-yi a-le i-ga-li-no-he-di-yi, I-si-li-ye-no u-du-gi u-nv-sv-i nv-tsu-li-s-do-ta-nv hi-a tsu-da-de-sv-dv tsv-qua-lv-s-di.

052821.english: And they said unto him, We neither received letters out of Judaea concerning thee, neither any of the brethren that came shewed or spake any harm of thee.
052821.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏅᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎥᏝ ᏂᎯ ᎡᏣᏃᎮᏍᎬ ᎪᏪᎵ ᏧᏗᏱ ᏂᏙᏓᏳᏃᏪᎳᏅᎯ ᏱᏙᎩᏃᎮᎭ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏓᏓᏅᏟ ᎾᎿ ᎭᏅᏓᏳᏂᎶᏒᎯ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏱᏂᎨᏨᏁᎴᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏐᏅ ᏱᎨᏣᏃᎮᎴᎢ.
052821.phonetic: Hi-a-no nv-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi; V-tla ni-hi e-tsa-no-he-s-gv go-we-li Tsu-di-yi ni-do-da-yu-no-we-la-nv-hi yi-do-gi-no-he-ha, v-tla a-le gi-lo i-da-da-nv-tli na-hna nv-da-yu-ni-lo-sv-hi gv-ni-ge-sv yi-ni-ge-tsv-ne-le-i, v-tla a-le u-so-nv yi-ge-tsa-no-he-le-i.

052822.english: But we desire to hear of thee what thou thinkest: for as concerning this sect, we know that every where it is spoken against.
052822.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎣᎦᏚᎵᎭ ᎢᏨᏯᏛᎦᏁᏗᏱ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎭᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬᎢ, ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᎤᎾᏓᏤᎵᏛ ᎤᎾᎵᎪᏒᎢ, ᎣᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᎨᏥᏃᎮᏍᎬ ᎨᎦᏡᏗᏍᎬᎢ.
052822.phonetic: A-se-no o-ga-du-li-ha i-tsv-ya-dv-ga-ne-di-yi nu-s-dv ha-da-nv-te-s-gv-i, hi-a-ye-no u-na-da-tse-li-dv u-na-li-go-sv-i, o-tsi-ga-ta-ha ni-gv-na-dv ge-tsi-no-he-s-gv ge-ga-tlu-di-s-gv-i.

052823.english: And when they had appointed him a day, there came many to him into his lodging; to whom he expounded and testified the kingdom of God, persuading them concerning Jesus, both out of the law of Moses, and out of the prophets, from morning till evening.
052823.cherokee: ᏕᎬᏩᏒᏍᏓᏁᎸᏃ, ᎤᏂᏣᏛ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸᎩ ᎤᏪᏗᏱ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᏚᏩᏁᎸᎩ, ᏚᏃᎮᎮᎸᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏑᎾᎴ ᎤᎴᏅᏛ ᎤᏒᎢ ᎬᏗᏍᎩ, ᏓᏍᏗᏰᏗᏍᎬ ᏥᏌ ᎧᏃᎮᏍᎬᎢ; ᏕᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎼᏏ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ.
052823.phonetic: De-gv-wa-sv-s-da-ne-lv-no, u-ni-tsa-dv gv-wa-lu-tse-lv-gi u-we-di-yi; na-s-gi gv-ni-ge-sv ni-du-wa-ne-lv-gi, du-no-he-he-lv-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv-i, su-na-le u-le-nv-dv u-sv-i gv-di-s-gi, da-s-di-ye-di-s-gv Tsi-sa ka-no-he-s-gv-i; de-gv-di-s-gv di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di Mo-si u-tse-li-ga a-le a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi.

052824.english: And some believed the things which were spoken, and some believed not.
052824.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏅᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏬᏂᏒᎢ, ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏳᏃᎯᏳᏁᎢ.
052824.phonetic: I-ga-dv-no u-no-hi-yu-nv-gi na-s-gi u-wo-ni-sv-i, i-ga-dv-no v-tla yu-no-hi-yu-ne-i.

052825.english: And when they agreed not among themselves, they departed, after that Paul had spoken one word, Well spake the Holy Ghost by Esaias the prophet unto our fathers,
052825.cherokee: ᏚᎾᎦᎴᏅᎲᏃ ᏓᎾᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬᎢ ᏙᎤᏁᏅᏒᎩ, ᏉᎳᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎠᏏ ᏌᏉ ᎢᎧᏁᏨᎯ ᎤᏁᏨᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏰᎵᏉ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᏥᏚᏁᏤᎴ ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ, ᎢᏌᏯ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᏧᏩᏔᏁᎢ,
052825.phonetic: Du-na-ga-le-nv-hv-no da-na-da-nv-te-s-gv-i do-u-ne-nv-sv-gi, Quo-la-s-gi-ni-no-nv a-si sa-quo i-ka-ne-tsv-hi u-ne-tsv-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Ye-li-quo Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do tsi-du-ne-tse-le di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge-i, I-sa-ya a-do-le-ho-s-gi tsu-wa-ta-ne-i,

052826.english: Saying, Go unto this people, and say, Hearing ye shall hear, and shall not understand; and seeing ye shall see, and not perceive:
052826.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏥᏄᏪᏎᎢ; "ᎯᎠ ᏴᏫ ᏘᏩᏛᎱᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏫᏂᏫ; ᎢᏣᏛᎩᏍᎬᎢ ᎢᏣᏛᎩᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏦᎵᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏥᎪᏩᏘᏍᎬᎢ ᎢᏥᎪᏩᏘᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏣᏙᎴᎰᏍᎨᏍᏗ.
052826.phonetic: Hi-a tsi-nu-we-se-i; "Hi-a yv-wi ti-wa-dv-hu-ga, a-le hi-a wi-ni-wi; i-tsa-dv-gi-s-gv-i i-tsa-dv-gi-s-ge-s-di, a-se-no v-tla yi-tso-li-ge-s-di, a-le i-tsi-go-wa-ti-s-gv-i i-tsi-go-wa-ti-s-ge-s-di a-se-no v-tla yi-tsa-do-le-ho-s-ge-s-di.

052827.english: For the heart of this people is waxed gross, and their ears are dull of hearing, and their eyes have they closed; lest they should see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and understand with their heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them.
052827.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏂᎾᏫ ᎤᎵᏦᎲᏒ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᏂᎵᎷᎬ ᎦᏂᎵᏳ ᎠᎾᏛᎩᎠ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᎦᏙᎵ ᏚᏂᏍᏚᎭ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏂᎦᏙᎵ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᎪᏩᏛᏙᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᏂᎵᎷᎬ ᏗᎬᏩᎾᏛᎪᏙᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᎾᏫ ᎬᏩᏃᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛ ᏧᏏᏁᏟᏴᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎦᎦᏥᏅᏬᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ."
052827.phonetic: Hi-a-ye-no yv-wi u-ni-na-wi u-li-tso-hv-sv, a-le da-ni-li-lu-gv ga-ni-li-yu a-na-dv-gi-a, a-le di-ni-ga-do-li du-ni-s-du-ha; na-s-gi di-ni-ga-do-li di-gv-wa-ni-go-wa-dv-do-di ni-ge-sv-na, a-le da-ni-li-lu-gv di-gv-wa-na-dv-go-do-di ni-ge-sv-na, a-le u-ni-na-wi gv-wa-no-li-s-do-di ni-ge-sv-na, a-le du-na-da-nv-dv tsu-si-ne-tli-yv-s-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na a-le di-ga-ga-tsi-nv-wo-di ni-ge-sv-na."

052828.english: Be it known therefore unto you, that the salvation of God is sent unto the Gentiles, and that they will hear it.
052828.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏣᏅᏖᏍᏗ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᎨᏥᏁᎸᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏛᎾᏛᏓᏍᏔᏂ.
052828.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di i-tsa-nv-te-s-di, hi-a na-s-gi a-li-s-de-lv-do-di U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi ge-tsi-ne-lv-hi, a-le na-s-gi dv-na-dv-da-s-ta-ni.

052829.english: And when he had said these words, the Jews departed, and had great reasoning among themselves.
052829.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏪᏒ, ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏒᎩ, ᎤᏅᏒ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏣᏛᎩ ᏭᎾᎵᏃᎮᎸᎩ.
052829.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nu-we-sv, A-ni-tsu-si u-na-da-nv-sv-gi, u-nv-sv ge-sv u-tsa-dv-gi wu-na-li-no-he-lv-gi.

052830.english: And Paul dwelt two whole years in his own hired house, and received all that came in unto him,
052830.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᎤᏃᏍᏛ ᏔᎵ ᏧᏕᏘᏴᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏛᎩ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᎤᏙᎳᏒᎯ ᎠᏓᏁᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᏓᏂᎸᎬᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎬᏩᏩᏘᏍᎩ,
052830.phonetic: Quo-la-no u-no-s-dv ta-li tsu-de-ti-yv-dv u-ne-la-dv-gi u-tse-li-ga u-do-la-sv-hi a-da-ne-lv-i, a-le da-da-ni-lv-gv-gi ni-ga-dv gv-wa-wa-ti-s-gi,

052831.english: Preaching the kingdom of God, and teaching those things which concern the Lord Jesus Christ, with all confidence, no man forbidding him.
052831.cherokee: ᎠᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎬᎩ ᎧᏃᎮᏍᎬᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᏕᏲᎲᏍᎬᎩ ᎧᏃᎮᏍᎬᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ, ᎤᏍᏗᎤᏅ ᎾᏍᎦᎢᎲᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᏄᏅᏍᏓᏕᎲᎾ.
052831.phonetic: A-li-tsi-do-hv-s-gv-gi ka-no-he-s-gv-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv-i, a-le da-de-yo-hv-s-gv-gi ka-no-he-s-gv-gi U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv, u-s-di-u-nv na-s-ga-i-hv-na, a-le gi-lo nu-nv-s-da-de-hv-na.
